This app is amazing for various reasons. There are many filters to customize your profile, and with this information they give the best recommendations. In addition, they also have interesting and helpful tips and tricks for the perfect skincare routine for your skin type. This app also doesn't send you lots of notifications like other apps do.

The app is beyond great bcs i get to see the details of the product but it would hve been perfect if u put malaysia country...also i think theres a crash in the camera bcs when i uploaded a picture...it turned over to the first page of this app again...and also it could be of great help if u put the product's price and add other skincare products such as safi...overall this app was great and i totally would recommend this to everyone!!😊😊😊

As someone with extremely sensitive skin, I'm learning so much from this app. I've been analyzing all the suspicious product I have, learning why I reacted to them, throwing all of them out. Lovely app, I love all the filters you can use and the information you have for beginners on what the products are used for and specific ingredients we should all know about, its very detailed! The new version is really bad. Please bring back the old one, it was soooo much better. The process of scanning product ingredients used to be helpful and quick, now it's more of an annoying slow-down and a hindrance in finding the right product for you.

EDIT: The newest update is brilliant 🥰 I love the smart schedule for products in a routine, as someone who has only a vague idea what I'm doing, that's soo helpful. Great for finding products, but not so great for keeping track of skincare results or to make skin care a habit. I am a bit disappointed, I was honestly expecting more from this app than just a database of skincare products and a bunch of advice on how to use them.

I really enjoy using the app. I've learned about my products, and no wonder why my acne issue doesn't go away. I cannot thank the developers of this app enough. The older I get the more thought (and money) I put into my skincare routine. Turns out the $38 cleanser I had was garbage and the $6 Target cleanser had better ingredients and was more suited for my skin type. I love the scanning feature as well.

I've spent an ungodly amount of time on this app. I built my entire skin care routine using Skin Bliss. I've tried many other skin care apps, but this one reigns supreme. This app is a miracle !! It's awesome , it explains everything for your skin type , it helps you which product to buy based on the ingredients and your skin concerns . It really helped me a lot to figure out my skin care routine !! , Thank you so much for creating this app.

Amazing app! But can you guys open a slot for us to edit/add more brand such as from South East Asia & other countries. That would be much more easier for us from keep typing/take photos. Keep up the good work!💪🏼✨

The best skincare app I've ever tried! It has very simple yet nice design, can analyze cosmetic ingredients, even gives you recommendations based on skin type and preferences! So far, I really like this app to check the ingredients. But why are the submitted products still had not been accepted after 3 months? Should at least change the status to rejected or something and give reason instead of pending verification.

This app is very useful, I won't lie. It's very aesthetic, easy to use and refreshing. I purchased this app as a home user for my extensive collections and belongings, such as books, toys, barbies, liquor, glass, china, etc... This app is very easy to use. As of Jan 2019, pictures still disappear and you cannot enter a book by scan or isbn (and yes, we know pro does, but I dont have a business). As an individual wanting to be more organized, I would give this app 4 stars if the pics didn't constantly disappear and it would be 5 stars if books could be entered by ISBN or scan.

I started with app in 2018. I was very happy with this product all these years. Then they sent me a email that my cost was going up 357%! Almost $1,100 more per year! When I reached out to customer service, I received a generic response and no consideration for being a long-standing customer. I had to downgrade my services, and I'm still paying more. I understand that costs have gone up, but this price increase is ridiculous. I'll be looking into alternatives.

Really loved the app i have tried for a really long time and i mean by long over a year to find an app and sortly was finally the answer there's a couple thing that bugged me. First of all there is no tutorial so there is no way of knowing what you're doing unless you do some tests yourself and even if you find a "better way" sometimes you can't repair the way you want unless you delete and start over. And the issue I'm suffering the most is that I can't adjust the items order in the list.

Meh. Sortly Plus is better than most (and there aint too many options out there that actually works or is easy to work with or has flexibility in recording/sorting any information desired (Sortly fails on this point)). One thing to keep in mind, this is just an inventory app and as such excells, but as a collection database record, it sucks. go look elsewhere. I like that its cross-platform compatible (even tho the PC is only via web browser and doesnt have a dedicated app). HOWEVER, I collect guitars and Sortly Plus cannot record anything more than the most basic info (a name and a serial number, is pretty much it, aside from ONE pic/record).  Cannot record any other info such as where/whom/when I bought it from, amount paid, color, setup info, any notes (<- note plural, i usually have more than one notes related to each item), web links to any resources/info,  cannot attach files, etc.  Methinks I may cancel subscription before trial ends...  I know that Sortly Pro supposedly does that BUT at $25US/month, that is WAY too much out of pocket for a private individual to justify.   I dont mind paying for functionality, but not that much.

For the first 4 days this app worked very well. I subscribed to the paid version and had family members help me input all the items in the container and shed. Tgen 2 days later while trying to adjust inventory up and down we noticed that items were being dropped from the or lending information was not being recorded. Then to top it off we now realize that the app has stopped syncing between devices. I tried the online help, and then reached out to the app creators....still no help provided.

Enjoyed using app past few months. Started with free version. It is easy to understand and use. When I got serious about creating folders and photographing items I immediately realized I wanted more options and was willing to pay for them. Unfortunately Sortly has decided to go with big business at $50+/mo minimum. I am an at home cookier. This is out of my price range. Glad I found this out now b4 maxing out my allotted 100 entries. For this reason I rate it a 3 bc my business doesn't matter.

I LOVE THIS APP. I used it to categorize and keep track of individual boxes when I was moving to a new house. I'll use it again and again every time I move. You can take pictures of the inside of each box AND put tags in each one. So I can literally type in "DVDs" and it will tell me exactly which boxes (I made labels and codes out of colored tape) have the DVDs in them, or the crock pot, or whatever. It's so amazing!!

I absolutely love this app! I don't own a business, however I do have an extensive collections of hobby items, such as cookie cutters, cake pans, chocolate molds, pantographs for quilting, etc, and this app makes my collections much more organized & accessible. I know where each item is stored & what it looks like. I love that I can have up to 8 pictures per item, that I can search all folders by item name or by tag, such as "Thanksgiving". It makes planning for holidays so much easier! Love it

Wishful thinking. I've been watching reviews of this app on YouTube and was really exited to use for organizing my crafts. In the middle of adding folders and getting excited about it, I decided to read more recent reviews. Seems like the home version (all the reviews I had watched) is no longer avl and the app has moved to a consumer version at much higher prices. Even with the current discount $25 a month, its just too much for me. Guess going back to excel or paper. Really too bad.

I've been using this app for my balloon business for the past 7 months and I love it. I've not had any issues outside of loading time after major data input. It saves me a ton of time and labor on weekly & monthly ordering. The beat part for me is I don't have to be at my shop to see if I have an item in stock. It does take time to setup & get all of your items input but once that's done it's a breeze to update your inventory.

Great concept, but flawed program. I use this for my company (we pay monthly) and at first it was great but now, after using this for more than 8 months, I am always having to login / out (even deleting/reinstalling) which prompts it to begin syncing (can take up to an hour). Many of our employees have similar issues. Items will either be missing or not sync properly and can be very frustrating... Like I said, the thought behind the program is great, but definitely needs a lot of bugs fixed.

This was a fantastic app for packing up for my last move--nothing else comes close. After days of searching I can't find anything that even begins to do what I want. Unfortunately, they've now switched entirely to focusing on business use, and the prices are too high for anything else. I would happily pay $39.99 once to own this app (and that's a huge price for an app), but that rate per month is simply not workable for home use. This is a very disappointing decision.

Sortly is the perfect solution for me. I'm using the subscription (~$5) version of the app to organize a major move. I have temporary accommodation for the next 5 months before moving to another continent, I have things in storage, things for sale, things ready to be shipped - a lot to keep track of. With Sortly I know exactly what is in each box, and what I intend to do with each item. Couldn't be happier.

I'm sorry, but paying $5 a month just to have an inventory of items I own is freaking ridiculous. I was hoping to have am app I could keep track of items that arent books or movies so I know what I have before buying. It works well for free, but I have more than 100 items to sort in my life and the fact o have to manually input each one because the scanner is only available for the paid version makes me never want to use it. If it was one, SINGLE payment. Sure. Not yearly/monthly, though.

I got the free version to try initially & loved it...easy to use, nice look, & allowed me to organize things well. After about a month photos & icons started disappearing. It wasn't because the photos synced to the cloud & were no longer on my device -when I moved them back they still wouldn't reset. I reached out to Sortly support, but they weren't helpful. The only way to get photos back seems to be to retake them. No thanks...I wouldn't pay for this. Without photos I have no use for this app.

i am a collector and wanted to be able to sort my collection, however I have more than 100 items, and the upgrade price is HUGE! $40-$100 a MONTH! It sucks because the platform and app was perfect for what I needed it for, and now I can't use it and I wasted all that time entering for nothing. I see that they are trying to move on from personal inventory to being a business platform, but it sucks that they are ignoring the needs of their original consumers by getting rid of the home option.

I trained our student government to use this app in the school store and while we haven't implemented it fully yet, it has been useful so far to keep track of inventory. Unfortunately, because they don't have a cheaper options to use, the scanner will have to find some other way to use it. Besides the most convenient way. It'd be great if they had some sort of sortly for education option as I think schools and students might use this app if it was more affordable

Love the app. I'm not a business but I'm a collector. I would have preferred if I will be able to do a 1 time purchase for additional entries past the free 100. I only need about a 1000 entries at the moment but the monthly subscription plan is too steep for me and 2000 entries is overkill for my kind of usage.

I’ve been using this app for 2 years. I like the app but they raised the price from $468 a year to $1428! That’s an increase of $960! Unfortunately I can’t afford the new price and have to cancel my subscription. I understand everything is going up but this is ridiculous and greedy. 2 years of entering data, pictures, quanities, etc - GONE. I contacted them to see if they could do better, and was told NO! STAY AWAY!! BoxHero is just as good at a fraction of the price.

Really happy with app but disappointed with pricing. The interface is great but $40 a month as your next price point from the free trial version is going to exclude a lot of customers who'd pay maybe $5 a month just to have more entries available. Was excited to use this but going to have to switch to a different app given the pricing.

I've only been using the app for a few days, but would like to see a dedicated way to inventory. Would also like to see the ability to add more information on labels and the ability to have that information on smaller labels. More granular reporting would be nice as well. The ability to print labels out of the app/website, and a dark mode.

This is by far the best Inventory Management Software that I've used. Not only is it simple but it's also powerfully customizable to set options just the way you desire. This companies clarity, transparency, and communicatiom is top tier professional. I can see Sortly overtaking other tech giant organizations in the future if they continue on the pathway they're going. So in conclusion, I love the software, I love how helpful their team is, maybe one day I'll join their team. 👀 10/10 🌟!

Advertised as a free application for inventory, I downloaded it to my tablet and after installing it and creating an account a limit of items per non-paid account. I also downloaded it into my tablet as I would want to use it there more and when running it the application clearly looks like a phone app running on a tablet. The text and buttons don't show correctly in some cases part of the screen are hidden with no scrollbar. May be great but this wan't a good first impression

When accessing an already established database with thousands of pictures it wants to download and sync those thousands of photos, rather than downloading them as I try to access that item. This seems entirely backwards. The rest of the app seems to function as expected, but I'll be deleting it and sticking to the desktop version until that issue can be addressed.

Easy to use, app and desk. I do use the app, but the barcode scanner doesn't work. Using note 10, everything up to date, including app. Helpdesk asked if it was all up to date, replied with yes and specs. Never received another reply, even reached back out and nothing back. I like all that it offers, I will continue using. My rating is based on lack of help of their software, and the main reason for users of this app is the scanner and it's not working.

Great app! I wish they had a membership tier aimed at individuals (vs. at businesses) that would give you 200-300 items for $10 per month. I can't justify $30-50/month for what's essentially a home inventory app for me, so I just prioritize the 100 items that I list in the free version.

I love Sortly and use it daily in my work, but the app isn't fully supported in Android 13 and I'm not able to add photos from my library as a result. I also wish that photos could be cropped within the phone app like they can the iPad and desktop client. I hope this is all fixed soon, because it's otherwise great

Need a way to communicate trouble ticket details to development. Found several issues. We think the system is easy to use and the label system saves time. We did run across some development issues but there's does not seem to be any phone support to troubleshoot or advise of issues within the program. Great start to an easy to use system. Needs a little tweaking.

App is fantastic. Business Model is horrendous. In my months of using Sortly Plus (now called Sortly Plus Legacy) the software is great. UX could be better, but it's a pleasure to use. I paid $5/mo. for my 1st year of Plus. This price has since been raised. The same plan is now $40/mo (80% increase). I back up my data via csv. If my legacy plan expires they will lose my business. Software good. Sub. Plans are awful. Any paying customers should have unlimited items. Storage is cheap. No excuse.

I signed up to try this app/service to track a personal collection. Internet searches seemed to recommend it. Although they have pages on their site that indicate the service has version for home inventory, collection tracking, and even tracking items for a move, in reality they are only after business use cases. The app and service appear like they could handle these use cases quite well. Sadly, they are not interested in the business of an individual.

Update 6/11/21: contacted Sortly support as indicated by developer comment to find bulk editing feature. I was told that it is not offered in the mobile app and must be done through the desktop version. Bulk Editing in the mobile app would make the app so much more useful. Add Bulk Edit!!! You can bulk edit items in specific folders from the website but not from the mobile application. This would make it so much simpler to use at different points in the supply chain.

this app asked if you are using it for personal reasons or if you are a business then proceed to treat you like you are a business the whole time. The prices are crazy also. 24.99/ month to keep a list of my personal belongings and be limited to 500 items is crazy work. One of my collections is larger than that.

I actually really like the app. it had all the features I was looking for. the only issue i had is simply with the pricing. I would gladly pay $50 for this app which is alot but worth it. unfortunately the subscription model just makes the app not worth getting.

So many developers seem to think that it's ok to deliberately mislead there potential customers, this isn't a free app it's $4 a month, My complaint isn't about the cost which maybe a reasonable amount, it isn't about the functionality of the app which again maybe very good, my complaint is about the underhand way they trick you into downloading and using this app before telling you the price. Im sure I would have found it useful , but I will never know as I refuse to be manipulated.

It all looked very nice at first glance, but there are some errors that make working impossible, I have 3 users and right now for an item I'm checking not a single device contains the same information in one of them there is even more inventory, somehow moving an item from one folder to another increased the total amount but only in one device, all devices have been synced but the problem persists, it totally sucks and would mess up all your inventories so bad

LOVE this app, started with it in iOS and so happy it is now here in Android! Bought a lifetime subscription. If you have things in storage or between multiple houses, DOWNLOAD THIS! I have been able to earmark things for decluttering in Scotland from a beach in Queensland (I didn't have time to make decisions like that when I needed to pack up, but now everything to go out is tagged and it will be quick and easy when I get back). It also saved me bringing shampoo and conditioner on the plane because I could check that there was some waiting for me on the other side. These sound like small things but they have been such a blessing and have saved me money simply by virtue of being more organised! Also haven't used the QR code labels in a while but they have helped my family determine which boxes are fragile when they have had to move them for me in my absence. A very handy little app. I will be trying out using it for suitcase planning in future (e.g. tagging my clothes by weather/occasion so I can see a list of viable options based on the forecast/activities packing). Recommend!

It's an ok app. It had bugs which were annoying, although maybe fixed with updates. Backing up and sharing is quite limited, however, making it difficult to transfer a list of you step using the app or change phones. You are then stuck with the app and ongoing monthly cost, the value of which I cannot see. Why a monthly subscription? Most apps have a small yearly fee or slightly more for full, lifetime access.

Works well but expensive. I needed something with a barcode scanner to catalogue my sewing patterns, sheet music and books at home so I don't re-buy things. It was really easy to use and Ifigured I'd upgrade when I hit the hundred item limit only to discover that the plans are super expensive. This is not an effective choice for home users, only businesses. I recomend the developers look at making a single user subscription for others like myself who just want to keep track of things at home.

It's a pretty nice app, it even has an option for tracking personal items, which is what I want. I went and started setting it up and then find out that only the first months free and then they start charging you. Nothing ever mentioned on here about that. Its super frustrating to spend time setting stuff up and then you have to either pay or just say oh well. It was for personal use there ain't no way I'm paying $25 a month to keep track of what clothes I have !

The app (free version) works great particularly in our case where we have a very limited amount of entries since the raw materials are the same and the end product (only one) is same as well. But we do need this to manage the inventory levels on the go. One problem that kindof defeats the whole point of sortly of being simple and robust is that I cannot give a universal minimum stock quantinty. Meaning I can only manage low stocks in individual locations and not as a whole. Pls fix it.
Waste of my time. It says it's a free app but you can only enter 100 items before they want to start charging you for further inventory entries.

Alot of options and informations are hidden behind a paywall for free users. Good for small business with 50 or lesser items and this is accounting for variants of the same product which will add up. Use Excel sheet if you are unsure. App is great though. All basic information are displayed at one glance.

I'm a sole trader selling a few knives and such. This is the third inventory app I've tried. Although I'm only using the limited free version I have to say it does everything very well with no glitches or hiccups and is easy to use, especially for a novice computer user like me. Thank you Sortly!

This is an awesome app to help organize & sort my stamps, inks, dies & supplies. I'd give it a 5 star if only within the app can the photos be edited. I tried taking the pics with the camera & editing there & adding it to the file. I didn't want to have all my craft supply pics on my phone. So, I deleted them from my phone's album & storage. Only to find my pics were also deleted from the file I created in the app. Using the camera within the app, the pics are not centered & no editing option.

crazy expensive for a personal tracker. and talk about a rip off. even if you pay for it it's next to useless as a professional tool if you run a real company. they even limit the top plan on entries. lots of better apps for lower and free cost out there. nothing like charging customers a monthly fee and offering zero services to explain these cost. this is a one time payment style app that someone just got greedy with.

Update: Support was very responsive and I am now able to sync on my mibile device. I also am able to download reports on my laptop where I can easily edit them to fit my needs. I like the ease of use and that I can create tags and add written descriptions. I am using it to catalog my jewelry for insurance. I subtracted a star because the sync won't work and so I can not download the inventory to print or email to my insurance company.

Bought in and decided it wasn't for me right now. Great app but the only negative is that I'm still paying and I can't even use it unless I subscribe again... then I'll pay double? I tried clicking on the notification that read "You will continue to receive charges on your account until you can" yep, that's all it said and I clicked to find out more... took me right back to download the app and pay. Guess I'll just have to close my business account.

Web app is super slow, it takes minutes to check one item in the item flow section, UX is not the best, it could be way simpler to see a graph and get relevant information about my stock. Seriously, is this what we are paying for ? Updates upon feedbacks from customers takes ages. Seems like an abandonned app to be frank.

Can't even open an account. When I enter my business name a drop down appears asking for my industry. app won't let me select anything and won't continue. I wasted a lot of time trying to troubleshoot this. Very annoying/buggy and certainly not living up the "simplified" part.

Might have been good at one point but the price point now is $50 a month just for a family move if you want moving labels. I wish the had a non-business more affordable option. It's crazy they make you spend $100 after you've done all the inventory work to put the labels on the boxes for a 2 month move....

Was unable to setup automatic backup/sync through google drive integration. Also found no manual export/import functionality for backups There was however syncing on by default (to unspecified location, likely their own servers), so if you don't feel any issue with relying on a single party for this, and the inability to setup redundancy (which I do), the app is likely enough for your usecase.

Not bothered by the cost for the app. The app needs some work. The camera feature is awful!!!! Needs work. No editing options and it often turns photos upside down. Adding multiple items at one time needs to be added. I think adding an option for one to designate their own Fields.

I like it a lot except very few of the clothing UPCs pull up information when you scan them. I'm not sure if it's the fault of the app or of the clothing manufacturers though, as there are a couple that have worked. I wish there was an option to enter the UPC manually to pull up the information. (Or is there and I just missed it?)

Sortly has revolutionized the way I handle my hoarding disorder :) --- I am now able to store things in such a way that their utility is restored by virtue of being able to find then again! Wishlist to reach 6stars: > Improved search intelligence --- often I am unable to find things as search function returns only exact matches. E.g. "Black plastic drop sheet" will not find an item named "Drop sheet plastic black". > Photos --- Remove cropping of displayed images. I.E. Show the entire image.

Love the app, everything I was looking for. I wanted to continue after I used all 100 entries, but I saw the cost, and it was way out there. I would happily pay a small fee per month or annually, but not at $40 a month just so I can keep track of my collection. It's time to look elsewhere, I guess.

I downloaded this app and have just started using it to populate the database. The barcode scanner, however, does not seem to recognize mainstream products for markets outside the United States. It does not recognize items I have purchased in Europe, Africa, and the Middle East. Is there another way to scan in those items without having to do so manually? Thanks!

Love it! It was exactly what I was looking for. A phone app with pictures of the inventoried item that reads bar codes and then pops up the item to change qty or edit..whatever. Could not be easier to use.

Holy expensive! Just wanted for home use, but with what they are charging I'll just throw my stuff away and buy new everytime I need something. I mean they are talking crazy prices for the non free version and the free version is too limited to be of use to anyone. Uninstall...

In theory the app is great. But I paid $60 for a year subscription, spent a weekend taking pictures, uploading and organizing everything, and now when I click on items in the app the pictures are gone and they don't show up.

The app works great for small business like ours(appliance repair) Parts, change inventory location so oft en(received, installed, cores, returns etc)Sortly makes quick easy work of classifying these parts and you can use the lent(as in something loaned out) feature to get a reminder on parts whose "return by date" for either (core , damaged or overstock ) so that you dont lose money due to expired windows of return , love that you can scan item in with phone and then assign folder/location ....

Was working exceptionally well until the last update when it was telling me I couldn't upload photos to Sortly from my camera. It was stating that this app didn't support the new Android update. Simple question why? It seems to work on my boss's Apple phone but it is not working on mine. Is there something with the Android update that is different from an apple phone? I do not want to switch to an iPhone just because of one app if it is not necessary.

Using to inventory waste treatment plant machines. I am able to take multiple photos, enter a serial number using the free custom field and then export all the data so that my pictures are neatly organized in an Excel sheet. This saved me a ton of time as pairing photos of machines that all look simular to their actual names, locations and serial numbers would have been a nightmare.

We were looking for a way to consolidate our inventory and improve consistency, reliability, portability, efficiency, etc. Sortly has allowed us to accomplish these goals! We are very pleased :)  Easy to use. I like being able to add pictures of items. Tags are powerful because items can be in multiple overlapping groups if needed. I used the .csv import feature and it worked great after I got all the data correct in the spreadsheet; spreadsheets are very logical and unforgiving so make sure your data is in order. I asked sortly a few questions and they scheduled a web meeting to meet with me and my boss; they were helpful and very professional.  Thanks Sortly!  Danny Woodring  Sonocare, LLC

took a little while to figure out how to view inside folders and camera function is wonky with my galaxy phone but I figured out to take pics before hand and add them from my library. for some reason the camera image pulls to the right showing things out of frame after the pic was taken. otherwise it had worked fine using my method.

I was looking forward to using this app but everytime I take a picture using my camera in the vertical position it flips my picture to the horizontal position so my pictures are not right when its complete and the flash option doesn't work. I'm not sure why it keeps flipping my pictures but that won't work for me.

I use this app to keep track or my baking tools (cookies cutters, stencils, sprinkles, etc). I can easily search for what I want and it is great. I love being able to tag items which helps my searching even more. LOVE this app!

2 years ago had the unfortunate event of a house fire. Sortly was labeled a better bet to inventory the items lost in the fire. Ended up purchasing a year in the $700 range, for inventorying 5000+ items. Since reconstruction/fire claims lengthy process my year ran up and they began charging over $350 a month for data barely used. After contacting to try and reduce they showed no empathy and stated pay or delete essentially holding information hostage. They will do the same to you. limited respon

I signed up because I need to get this off my chest. My employer used this to track his inventory. Whether you should use this app to inventory over 30,000 items is questionable. What isn't questionable is Sortly's terrible search algorithm and space-wasting UI. Search gave me different results depending on whether I used the iphone or laptop I was provided with. Using the "" operator was a total gamble. My supervisor hated it. My coworkers hated it. Sort your search out, Sortly.

This has made my business so much easier and so much faster to Add inventory management inventory and I love the alerts when my inventory gets too low now if only we could link it to an invoice app that took items out of inventory when you sold them would be good

This would have been the most ideal app for my small business but the idea that I can pay for this or the entire adobe suite of products is just unfathomable. I Went to upgrade and thought I would buy this app for $5 tops. But $50 a month? Not on your life. Small businesses just starting out are so tired of this. We can't get a break $29 a month is even steep for an app. I would consider $5 a month for my 150 products or so.

No custom feilds in the 'Home' version, only in the Buisness version it would seem. Quite strange and dissapointing, everything else looked good, but as I collect old maps I need to input far more information than 'price' and 'serial number' etc.

Looks great but I can't justify spending $40 every month for the rest of forever just to organize my home (100 item limit for free accounts). I'll come back when there's a one time fee for normal people 😔

I love the idea of the app, and I want to use it as an individual owner taking care of my home items. The sum every month is simply too heavy to pay. I wish there was something similar for home inventory. I'm afraid I will have to stop using it. Would appreciate anyone recommend a home inventory app though!

Worked great for the first 50 or so items. Now it constantly crashes and freezes up my camera in app. (Using Samsung S7.) Newest update seems to have fixed the crashing, camera still freezes from time to time, but hitting the on screen settings button seems to fix it.

I am keeping inventory of crafting supplies and Sortly has made it fairly easy. The pictures are important and I love the search feature. I haven't used all the functions yet so I can't speak for it fully, but I am very glad I have it.

It has been a terrible experience. I installed and set up my account. I went to make it the paid for edition and as soon as I entered my cc information in suddenly could not login nor change my password or even use the app. I am very angry and have already had to contact my bank to make sure no charges will be taken from this application since it locked me out as soon as it took my cc information. very disappointed as we are military and move a lot this app would have come in handy.

This app has so much potential for people who want to do home Inventory the problem is they give you up to so many free entries but if you have a lot of items to inventory at home you going to need more than that and the prices for this or outrageous they need a non business price.

If using Samsung phones, the picture taken by camera always being rotated 90 degrees. I would suggest to please add some basic editing features (rotating, cropping, flipping) when using camera. Thanks.

Nice interface, it looked promising, but unfortunately uninstalled due to the limited free functionality, expensive pro version and the fact that I literally got 6 emails from them within 24 hours of signing up for the free account

It's been ok, but on Android 10, I am completely unable to add pictures unless I take them from within the app. I can't add pictures from my gallery. Every picture comes up gray and unreadable. It works on Apple, but not Android 10

I spent hours and hours logging inventory and trying to get everything organized only to find out that it is not unlimited on the number of items you can store. You must pay for this feature. Very misleading.

Won't even let me set up an account. None of the buttons work to click through. Got as far as "how do you want to sort items?" and even though I chose Personal, not Business, all the options are for business inventory management. Then I couldn't select anything anyway. Uninstalled

Too buggy... 5 minutes in and it froze twice. Dont know what the app would be like. A bit much asking money for it by closing off functions when it doesnt work properly. I hate having to sift through these junky apps to find a good one...

As a new company manager and soon to be owner I was looking for something that would make inventory a breeze and from a beginner's standpoint up until present I have had a wonderful experience with this app. It is very detailed but also simple enough and to the point when it comes to user interaction. I would highly recommend this app!!!!

Great app, its just that the flash while taking pictures of item doesn't work when set on "auto" mode or "on" mode. It has been like this for a long time but i remember in the initial releases of this app the flash used to work.
Terrible app - the worst customer service and WAY too expensive for not a lot of functionality. Very disappointing that they raised their prices in a crazy way. Went from $50 a year to $40 a MONTH!

Well designed, maintained, and functional. BUT, for any halfway serious use the cost is very high. The pricing is way out of proportion to the service's value. That feels greedy. I'm not looking for a freebie, just be reasonable.
Really good app for tracking small inventory sizes. It'll be great when it is able to scan a UPC and auto lookup the info and populate the data. Otherwise good app. Developers are on the right path.

Couldn't truly try the apps features. Nearly everything requires the premium plan to try, and you can only edit one custom field before you're hit with the pay wall again. And the list of custom fields magically denies you to use the barcode scanner as your one custom field to try. This app is awful from the literal 3 things I could do.
i like this app but the app has currency symbol problem just like i select united arab emirates is writing total value in arabic but i need in english AED symbol

This product is great, but when the app needs to update it constantly freezes and takes a very long time to update, IF it does update. Of it does not update you have to uninstall then reinstall the app.

The idea is great but it doesn't have a good database of items. Maybe just Canadian items? I'm from Canada. I would love to scan and go but I had to enter everything manually, even very common items like ketchup
Its GREAT! My only complaint is in regards to some of the barcodes that won't register. I wish we had the ability to import info from the web or such to compensate

Perfect in every way possible! Works flawlessly and keeps your job perfectly organized.. Looking forward to try the upcoming features 🤩. Needs very Minor tweaks to fit every business module but good thing that the developers are open to suggestions and feedback at their website and they take every tiny remark in consideration 👏🏻. All in all it deserves a solid 10/10. Thanks sortly ❤️

This is trash. At least $40 a month to use it or 100 to get anywhere. Purchasing it would be fine a steep monthly subscription for organizing my garage is rediculous. Poorly advertised as good for homes and it definitely is not

What a waste of time. You have to go through all that crazy process to find out that it doesn't even support the at codes in the free plan and that the first available plan costs $49 a month. That's crazy! Deleted! Stay away
After going pro for 5 months, I can't access my information without being asked to upgrade again. Completely sucks. What more, the info that I need is not viewable. I want my 5 months money back.
I don't know what they did but all my items are gone that I had saved. No pictures, nothing. All the work I did. App was already limiting with only allowing 100 items. Time to move on.

googleAutocompletions: cosmetic inventory upgrade grow a garden
what is the best free inventory app
is there an app to keep track of inventory
serpPaaQuestions: Are you using iOS or Android?
Do you mostly want to track expiration dates, spending, or how fast you use products?
What is the best free beauty app?
What's the best inventory app?
What is the app that lets you see what's in beauty products?
Which is the best app for beauty products?

playStoreApps.name: RePOS: Restaurant POS System
playStoreApps.developer: Turkuaz Grup Teknoloji Enerji Ticaret Ltd. Şti.

playStoreReviews.text: I was debating between this and another app. but after trying to add items, KP made it quicker and easier. just a couple taps and less typing. Would be nice if I could go back to recently scanned items by tapping them below the scanner but that's just more convenient than an issue. Thanks for helping me clean out my kitchen!

love this app!! I LOVE the pantry and the ability to see what ingredients I already have for a recipe. this app is making meal planning, grocery shopping, and kitchen inventory a breeze for me and my family!! The option to do a lifetime purchase instead of a monthly/annual subscription is a huge plus. my only problem with this app is just that some recipes don't import from certain websites. this is my favorite pantry organizer/recipe storage/meal planning app that I've found!

I really like this app, for the most part. I went to add a couple items to my inventory today, though, and most of my items I had already logged are missing!!! This is frustrating. For some reason it first said I didn't have an account, so then it said to sign up or sign in. I signed in and now most of my stuff that I already had logged is no longer there!

incredible app! worth the premium subscription. I scanned everything in our kitchen and it's made a huge difference, the recipes are really good and the ability to add your own recipes is excelent. adding food to our planner updates our pantry. from the amount of oil I use to how many eggs I can't believe it! if you want to organize your kitchen and reduce waste, know just how long you have until expiration dates and even what you need to pick up on your next shopping trip this is the app!

It's a wonderful app to keep track of what's in your pantry. You can download your entire pantry inventory and make your own grocery list like I did. The app's suggestions for meals with what's in stock is really helpful on those days when you are pressed for time and clueless on what to fix for dinner. It's very easy to scan each item before putting it on a shelf. I highly recommend KitchenPal.

I love the app, and only wish there was a way to sort by type of food. It would be great to be able to plan my menu based on whatever meat I have in the freezer, or what veggies I have. Also, in order to change the name of an item, is my only option to delete it and re-enter it? Thanks. UPDATE: I still love this app and the new features, and tried to share the app with my family. I sent the link that the app provided, but they got an error message. What am I doing wrong?

Fantastic app. One of the few out there worth purchasing and just does what it states. It definitely took us a bit to get into the habit of adding things but once we did, the app greatly helped in lessening our food waste and stopped us from buying extras of what we already had. Also shout out to the dev team for developing an optimized, responsive app and being open to suggestions

Works great. It's helping us get track of what we have in our freezer. I upgraded lasy month so I can additional storage spaces so I can keep track of what is where. And I am able to share my inventory with my household! I have 1 minor complaint - It is SLOW. Slow when creating a new item, slow during item search. But most frustrating is it's slow on the initial load. I have a lot of stuff logged, so I'd be tempted to blame this, but this load has been slow from day 1.

One of the better kitchen keepers I've tried so far. It would be amazing if I could do a batch import of my foods, similar to how the app is set up to do a batch export. Sometimes the app runs a little slow when trying to get everything catalogued, which makes it a little tedious. Additionally, not all of the recipes that I find in the app can successfully be added to the meal planner, which is frustrating.

The app is decent but : It doesn't tell you how many of the 100 free scans you got left. The nutritional assessment is mostly incorrect I cant add expiration date of 31 dec 2025, it always set for 2026. It should enable you to switch storage as some are just incorrect That said , is a good app overall, but needs some polishing for me to decide to buy it at least. The scan feature is perfect, quick and reliable, the database of products is very good!

I am glad to have found this app! I tried to manage my fridge/freezers with whiteboards, but it quickly became unsustainable with the amount of time it took to update and reorganize after shopping. KitchenPal not only solves this issue, but it also adds other features such as remote access (checking inventory while at the store), sharing between family members, shopping lists, recipes from your ingredients, and so many more. I am so happy to have become a lifetime member.

This is an awesome app. You pick recipes to cook for the week, it puts the ingredients on a list for you, you mark off what you already have and it has in app links to Walmart grocery, Kroger, instacart, etc. It not only takes you to the site but imports your list as well and looks up each item, which you can check off and purchase. Awesome!

Positives: lets you track whats in your pantry/fridge & create recipes based on what you have and allows you to create shopping lists based on what you dont. Helps you calculate how many calories a day and helps you pick recipes based on that too. Negatives & this is a big one: NOT americanized. most recipes are under metric measurements (which I could of put up with) but the pantry photos & recipes are for items not even available in my country vs generic choices. And no way to substitute.

I love that I can add the photos to the items and the look and view of everything. One major flaw is I can not edit the name or add a quantity. I saved the product quickly figuring I could edit the title later and it won't allow me to do it. Please add that feature. This would get a 5 star from if it had those two major feautures because it works for what I need.

Cool idea, but terribly limited. Many UPC come up with the wrong info or no info at all, requiring the user to manually input data and pics. The shopping list is clunky, with annoyances like deleting items that you don't purchase on a single trip. I wish I hadn't subscribed so quickly. Keep continues to be a better solution for me.

Using the free part, you're only able to have 12 items per category; regardless if you put in an expiration date or not. It's nice that you can create new items, but you have to associate them with general items already in their lists in order to get recipes. I started with canned goods, and most bean types were not listed. I stopped there and uninstalled. It could be a good app eventually, but needs some work. At this point, I'm not willing to pay.

I have been downloading almost every application to find a good grocery list app that I like. although I don't love this application, I feel that this is one of the better ones. I just want simple with more suggestion items for my list. The recipes in here are not appealing, they need work there; add more recipes. It is a good start for the app. Would love to find an app that makes my list with many icons..but after I add the item to my list, the application also would search for coupons

Don't waste your money. I downloaded it, was trying to load all my data, and had limitations. So I decided to try premium. Well, after dropping $12, I don't see any added features, it's still restrictive as to describing products, then it locked up my whole phone when I tried to scan barcodes. I deleted it. I'd like my money back.

The categories that it places food in are not accurate. It says you can change them, but only in some cases, and only using their very limited categories. It doesn't recognize fresh foods. It says the free version holds up to 100 food items, but it only allowed 50 before it required me to share the app with others. You can find a recipe based on 1 food item, but not multiple foods, so you may not come close to what you are seeking. Really, just too many obvious problems to continue after 1 day.

We had a lot of waste trying to meal plan from work post-covid. I wanted an app that would quickly / accurately scan our pantry to help us like we were still at home. Sharing the app has let us show our older kids what's in the cupboard to eliminate "I didn't know we had that!" waste. I wanted meal suggestions that they could make with what we have. This covers all the bases and seems to work splendidly. We had one issue with registering but the team fixed it in < 24 hours - great!

I just started using the app, and I've hit quite a few issues, as listed below 1) New/created ingredients won't save 2) I can't change inaccurate information for food I scan 3) There's neither an option to name a quantity of a product nor a level of a single ingredient (full, half, etc.) 4) Maybe I don't have enough ingredients, but I can't figure out how to use the smart cooking feature for what I have in stock 5) Everything is in grams and such, so I don't have a real concept of what's left

Barely works. Barcode scanner wont find ANYTHING, and its all name brand products. After typing in all the information and hitting save, THEN it will actually find the product most of the time. However, when you then click Add To Kitchen, it wont let you select whether to save in pantry,freezer or fridge...it just seems to add it wherever randomly. So you have to select the products one by one and change the storage option to move it to your freezer or wherever you wanted it in the first place

The pantry scanning app was okay, but adding something new (such as the obscure stuff I bring back from overseas) is tedious at best. I'm also not at all impressed with the recipe import from online sources. Seemed overly picky on the recipe site format, sometimes claiming a perfectly fine site as unreadable or invalid. And since there's no web browser extension of site to work together with this app, there's no means of going from an online recipe on a PC browser to this app. It has potential.

The interface is slick but limited. You cannot add an item, just select from a limited menu. Quantities can only be entered in predetermined servings, not ounces. Multiple individual packages of the same item of different sizes cannot be entered. I was looking for an inventory system, not shopping and cooking advice.

I'm still adding ingredients in but it is taking me longer because I have to plug in the expiration date more than once since most of the time it doesn't save the first time, which gets frustrating after a while. Also, a lot of the products are not in the system so I have to create most of them. But I'm still in the first few days, hoping to really enjoy it after finish adding everything.

After reviewing information about Kitchenpal, It was a no-brainer to sign up for the premium lifetime option. I needed some help after sharing my kitchen with family. Even though it was late Eastern time, their team replied to my email in very short order. I did what they asked me to do and it resolved my issue. They followed up with me within a few minutes to be sure I was satisfied with the result. It was so easy to add items, and I can't wait to utilize all the other planners & features!!!

I was able to scan many items into the inventory! At times tried to enter new items but, found it to be too cumbersome. I barely had time to get a feel for the app before it stopped allowing me to scan after 100 items. Needless to say I will not be spending any money getting to know this any better! Reply to below: as I explained above it does not work well to add manually. I don't care if it was free to use.

The app is great and does what it claims to do, and quite a bit more. The organizational boost of having my entire inventory available at all times is wonderful. Also important to mention is that they provide amazing support. I am absolutely content with my purchase of the lifetime family subscription. Definitely would recommend. The only real critiques I have are that the shopping could be streamlined a bit, and I would love to be able to restrict recipes by calories. By the way - not a bot. 😁

I am disappointed in the purchased version, as "uploading your own recipes" is misleading. You can only upload FULLY described recipes WITH an image and must wait for "approval" to use them...so they're not for helping you ensure you have ingredients on hand--they're for everyone to access. And you cannot customize available recipes, so if you don't want meat in your biscuit and gravy recipe then you'll always appear to missing that ingredient. I bought a year but it's not really what I want.

The barcode scanner is phenomenal and enters about 95% of the things I try, even generics and store brands. The expiration date feature is awesome, especially for things like milk. But the idea that I can't just save my own recipes (and I shouldn't have to publish them) is awful. And when I tell it "give me drink recipes with only these 5 ingredients" it gives me everything from quinoia to cheesecake to pancakes. Either I'm too dumb to use the app, or it doesn't do what I need.

I left a review yesterday. Just wanted to add that I'm getting less excited about this app. The first recipe I tried I got 5 steps into and it called for an ingredient it doesn't tell you you need. I can't go get it, so the recipe dissolved into buttered noodles and garlic. Which I don't need an app to make. Still gonna try the app for a while longer, but so far its managed to bungle up its major selling point. And the recipe selection isn't impressive

I LOVE this app. Not only is the customer service amazing but the organization of my kitchen is so much better now. You can have different types of storage so you know where your items are located. Premium membership let's you share your shopping list with others so they can add on to it. My favorite part is the expiration dates and that it uses what I have to suggest a meal.

This app is an absolute life changer! It has allowed me and my girlfriend to take control of what we have on hand, and eat healthier, while saving time. We also are able to enjoy what we have on hand more thanks to the built in recipes search. It will tell you what you can make off of what you have and prompt you on what's going to expire soon automatically. It even gives you a shopping list for what you need. The developer even responds quickly when you message them. Can't recommend it enough.

This app is helping us save money and time! We set the different locations where we store food and goods and when we use them, we remove it from the list. No more unnecessary purchases. I always bought too many things because I wasn't sure if I had an ingredient for a recipe I wanted to make. Found out I have 3 bottles of BBQ sauce and 1 in the fridge! Just purchased the lifetime membership. Also, I want to thank the KitchenPal App Team for helping so quickly with a mistake I made.

I really really love this app and cannot believe it is free! For years I've been looking for an app that would allow me to take an accurate inventory - using barcode scanning (I have 3 kids and ain't nobody got time to type everything in) and this app does THAT ! AND DOES IT WELL. It even worked on aldi and generic brands! I'm high impressed. We've saved so much money and food already - it's so helpful to be able to pull up what I have at home when I'm at the grocery store or meal planning.

Very nicely put together, but requires your email just to use normal functions. Limited to no information for exotic produce items, beyond basic fruit and vegetables. Inputting your own details for items requires you to connect it to another on their search results. Ultimately, there are other apps that don't require your information, or try to sell a premium membership.

I got your email, thank you. It's just difficult to go through and update the categories for all items. I went to open my app today and it asked me to sign in. Once signed in, it shows zero items. In my profile, it says I have 448 products in the kitchen, apparently I just can't see them. I tried changing the view and it didn't make a difference. I also can't perform a search.

Love the app. The ability to track expiration dates is awesome. Also, I really appreciate the accessibility of the nutritional information at my fingertips. I'm on a very strict medical diet, and this is making my meal planning a lot easier. I have one suggestion, though. I'd like to have the ability to search for items alphabetically. As it is, locating specific items is a bit cumbersome. I look forward to your updates.

KitchenPal is close to being the grocery inventory app that I dreamed of, but it is not perfect. THE issue that could cause me to just stop using it is adding grocery items to KitchenPal and then changing the item description in the app to suit my unusual filing system. I get the impression that I'm "updating" the KitchenPal Universal Database each time I do so. If so OK, but I DON'T want item descriptions uploaded by other Users to change my item descriptions and mess up my filing system!!

Overall this app is definitely a 9/10. There is some room for improvement though. Sharing kitchen across devices is amazing, bar code scanning is awesome despite having to enter any generic foods manually (consider adding this as built in option please). The units of measurement and quantity need refinement as there isn't a way to add fractional items or partial inventory ie half box of macaroni? The recipe suggestion and lookup is terrible this need to be boxed up. URL link recipes instead.

I've downloaded to my tablet and to my phone. I signed up and it gave any error. I deleted and tried again. This time is said sign in.. I guess it got me signed up, but now it says it can't connect to the server. Maybe y'all are down for maintenance or something. I was so excited to find this app. Not so much now. Please let me know so that i can try again. FYI, i connected through Google, which i do with all my apps. Also, no problem with connecting to other games and apps right now, just this.

Update: App is very good at keeping your kitchen organized. Most items I can scan the barcode and they are recognized. Customer support is very responsive, and responds to queries quickly and efficiently. 2/27/21 Just started using this app and I think it's great and really does what I need it to do. I would love the ability to mark something as non-food so that it doesn't ask me for nutrition info. 😁

I love the concept, however, it will not allow you to modify where the app puts the products. For example, it placed the ranch dressing in the pantry category. I keep my ranch in the refrigerator. Also, I freeze a lot of my meat when I buy it but it automatically puts all the meat in the refrigerator. I even upgraded to the premium and it still just puts the products wherever it wants. Not happy about this.

It's ok. It assigns many items to the wrong storage - frige vs freezer vs panrty. Spaetzel is not a beverage. Just because something is a liquid doesn't mean it is a beverage (sweetened condensed soy milk). Fixing it involves going into each item individually to reassign it. If you don't assign an expiry date, guess what? It is assigned an expiration date of today! Do you have one item in the frige and the other three on the shelf? It's not that smart. If you have many items, the app bogs down. Use categories in addition to storage categories would be useful. I have Spices (storage category) that I keep in the refrigerator.

My cousin bought this for me for a birthday present. Originally, the subscription wouldn't go through, but an email over to the support team fixed it real quick. Took like 5 seconds. It was great! While the many app features are incredibly useful, the one I want to highlight the most is the recipes system. It is an ever-growing list of recipes submitted by the users themselves. So if you don't see a recipe, just go ahead and make one! I love this whole app. I'd swear it was designed just for me

Can't sign up for premium, keeps saying I have no connection. Most of the recipes are wrong, and it's not possible to see the original recipe. Example:there is a ham macaroni and cheese recipe that says its "pork-free". I sent the devs a list of screenshots of items that are wrong, sometimes ridiculously so:milk scanned as bullets, and a jar of pickles scanned as an Amazon Fire tablet... Maybe they would be better licensing their scanning tech to Yummly?

The program obviously has potential, but unfortunately, many things still need to be fixed and improved. And first of all, the server speed - it takes too much time to search for the product and update it. I am not at all sure that the program will work if there is no Internet. Update: minus two stars. Recipes section is bad. You cannot control your own recipe after submitting. Cannot edit or delete. Unacceptable.

Installed yesterday, I've had a shopping list app but it didn't allow for pantry inventory which Kitchenpal does. Had to spend the $30 to upgrade to professional since the free only allows 99 pantry items plus other limitations. It's well worth it. One suggestion is when adding an item to shopping list instead of having to click add item and then add to shopping list it would be much better just to have a single add to shopping list button (double buttons are redundant)

Paid the higher price for the one time purchase. I keep getting an error message and I was unable to delete or update items in my kitchen. I emailed and the only response I received was they wanted to know if anyone else was experiencing the same issue. I requested a refund since the app clearly didn't work and was denied. Also found out they had a data breach and potentially had customers information stolen. Would not recommend this app.

I really love the concept of this app! I feel as though I am always throwing food out that I forgot that I had, and expires. Or, I'll buy an item at the store that I already had 2 of in my home. This is a great way for my husband and I to keep a full inventory of everything in our kitchen. I REALLY like the feature of the app alerting you when food is about to expire. I'm looking forward to using this app more. Tech support is great, and promptly handled a small issue I had while signing up

This app is very easy to use and it has been a life saver and money saver. It definitely helps us keep from overbuying certain items and it helps us keep track of what we have which makes it easier when meal planning and grocery shopping. Scanning all of the items in is great, I highly recommend this app if you need help keeping track of items in your kitchen without opening cabinets, fridge or pantry. It will help generate shopping lists too based on inventory you input!

It's a no for me. The features sound and look great. I tried to create a list and added about 10 things to the list just for only 4 of them to show. I tried going out of the app and back in to see if they would appear but nothing. I tried to re-add the items to the list, but that dint work either. It stopped being able to recognize basic items like broccoli and would say that I needed to create the item. I created it and it still would not add to the list. Not the app for me

I've been having a problem with disappearing expiry dates on my items. Through trial and error, I've discovered that both increasing or decreasing an item's count will clear the expiry field for that item. It happens on both the storage list or the individual item page. For example, I'll create a single pantry entry quantity of 12 items along with the expiry date for those items, which is obviously the same for all qw. When we use an item, the expiry is gone. What do I do to prevent this?

I love this app, immediately decided to purchase premium. However the more I use it the more performance issues I have. Frequent connection error timeouts and spinning wheel when I try to add/update inventory or modify shopping lists. I have fast cable Internet and 5g cellular but encounter this on both. Other than that it's excellent and I'll modify my review if they can fix these issues. Just not good enough performance for a paid app.

I just downloaded it today. It is a bit tedious getting started, but most of the things that I have scanned are scanning properly. because I use a lot of generic type products or product-specific to my area, I am adding them to the database, just as I have with my Fitbit application. I think that this app has some great potential, and I am looking forward to continuing to use and learn more about it. Profile page--needs to be dropped down choices, not calendar.

Love this app. As a prepper it is exactly what I am looking for to manage my food supplies. It has also helped to let us know that we already had an item in stock and did not need to buy it again. I also like the history so we know how often we go through an item. It is easy to use and the customer service was excellent. What I would like to see in future updates is to be able to edit/delete stock locations, and to be able to set target amounts.

easy to add items to the pantry but sometimes glitches and adds it multiple times and when you delete the extra one it deletes both. sometimes glitches when trying to move items from one location to another. the recipe option has no understanding of the ingredients. example: rice cakes cannot be used as rice or egg drop soup as eggs. so I don't even bother with trying to use that function, which I would really like to utilize.

Items get deleted when you run out and then you have to rescan each time. Because there's no zero balance items, you cant just look at your pantry to see what you need. Update. I have been using the app and I'm adding a 1 to each thing so I can look at my pantry and know what I'm out of (1=0). The labeling is a mess. I have to rename every item because when I want to see my "olives" I'm not going to be looking under "Aldi". Or "great value" when I wasn't too see how much ketchup I have.

Really underdeveloped app. Super laggy. Not worth paying a premium. Couldn't even leave feedback without selecting Google Play as the app source. Quantities are not standard. Doesn't have basic things like sliced turkey. Puts "everything" in the spices category and tedious to move to fridge or pantry. Put all our food in and only gives me recipes for dessert cakes and coconut curry chicken. We have a ton of food. Frustrating to spend so much time setting up just to have these limitations.

I just started using this. I'm really bad at keeping track of what I have on hand and keeping a grocery list. Using this app I can now just look at what I have and when it will be expiring in helping me figure out what I need to get. It will also help me stay more organized in keeping my fridge and pantry well stocked on the things I need. I love how it has the categories to look up it is so much easier for when making your list. I highly recommend using this app.

We have the premium version. It's a good app, however there are some things we don't like. More specifically the shopping list, very weird setup. When you used up a product it goes directly to the general shopping list (My List), not giving you the option to select the actual shopping list that you want it on. The only way to move it to the list you need is from the product profile in which case you cannot mark it as used up. Moreover, there is no way to move products between lists. Disappointed

Didn't even have it 30 minutes, scanned a few items and it suddenly stopped working. Update: I have re-downloaded the app and it appears to be working ok. Gave 4 stars instead of 5 because I do find the app to be a bit slow and laggy. I have tried it on different devices so the phone isn't the issue. Update: I went to start adding items for my freezer & it keeps getting SLOWER and SLOWER. I closed the app and it said I was logged out and it won't log me back in. I give up. Too much work!

Decent app!! Has separate tabs for fridge, freezer, pantry and more. My husband and I can connect on Android and iPhone and are able to both scan items in and it shows up. Some downsides are that is does crash and run really slow when using it and I wish it had more recipes that just stuff for my eggs (we do have more than eggs in our house haha) Overall, a decent app but with some improvements it could be awesome.

Don't waste your time. It is difficult to use, defaults to add items to places that you don't want, and does not allow you to move the items, such as when it defaulted to Fridge. I had originally chosen Pantry, then when I had to create a description for a bar code, it defaulted back to Fridge and every canned good I scanned after was added to Fridge. It did not allow me to move the items. If that is an option, it is definitely not easy to find or do. I deleted the app.

After manually adding sweet orange marmalade as an ingredient, I am shown a recipe for marmalade. Not helpful when I already have it and the app didn't recognize it. I also don't want to see recipes with "missing ingredients". I am specifically looking for help with the items in my house, not extra stuff I have to buy. Other than having to create several common ingredients, the app has real potential. A filter option for breakfast lunch and dinner would be great too.

Why when I'm under the freezer section, hit the plus button, type in 'burger patties' and make an entry and hit add the only option seems to add it to pantry then i have to drag it over to freezer? Edit: thank you for the reply, I was getting frustrated but altho it's a lil clunky I like the app, a surprising number of products were scannable which is convenient. Weird pics pop up sometimes but I like that I can just take a pic of mine. Still needs work but giving it a second try

Really good overall. The only issue I have is when adding a custom recipe, the measurements of teaspoon, tablespoon, cup are not available (I have it set to US measurements) and I don't want to convert everything to weights. I'd like the ability to add 1/2 increments of items. If I have 1 1/2 gallons of milk for example, right now it shows 2 until one is gone.

I enjoy how easy it is to scan items to have them recorded. I do find the categories to be off (how are cashews considered a "sweet"?) And I really don't enjoy the app telling me that having coconut oil in my pantry is a "poor choice". I cannot find a way to turn off that feature and I find it very annoying. But I think the ease of use, for the most part, outweighs those annoyances.

The overall layout and function of the app is easy to use and to understand. However, I have to agree with other reviews that there's not a whole lot in terms of recipes friendly to specific diets. Under the healthy food section there was fried hashbrowns, apple pies, and other baked goods. I think the developer needs to fine tune the apps overall sorting system. Or maybe moderate user submitted recipes a little more closely. Wish I could have kept using it!

After buying my sixth can of black beans and realizing we have three jars of thyme stashed in our cupboards I'm excited to have an app that my partner and I can both use and check when we're at the grocery store. I wanted this for inventorying dry goods, but interested to see how we use it for perishables as well. Maybe we'll even love the recipes! Right now, the main frustration is not being able to add / edit categories. I want to know what cupboard something is in or if it's stored away!

I got all the way through my pantry only to find out I can only manage a certain limit. When that limit has been hit, I have to buy premium or unlock the products. I was looking for an app that would make it easier to manage my food so I don't over buy. This is inconvenient considering I can't delete a certain product after using it. It also won't let you put a certain number of items. like canned goods for example. I can not put that I have multiple of this one thing. Deleting this app ☹

It has been great so far - just started today. The reason for 4 stars & not 5 is that it locks up often & has to be restarted, as well as suggests meat entrees, even though I changed my preferences to vegetarian. Not perfect, but will definitely help two very busy people know what we have & what we need! A few things I wish I could do.. 1) have other categories for items like paper towels, etc. or add custom categories. 2) add something in two places (ie one in fridge, one in pantry).

Exactly what I wanted! Needed an app to keep the pantry, freezer and fridge organized with no waste, so easy to use & set up - adjust for the number of a certain item, expiration dates, recipes, shopping list, and more. Intuitive, clean visuals. I'm a fan. Liked it so much I bought the Premium subscription for $12 bucks for the year! Keep up the good work.

It's definetly the best pantry organizer/tracker on the market as far as how much you can add to it. However, ever few adds I get the spinning wheel of death! It's extremely annoying to back out and force close each time this happens. Especially since it's my first time adding stuff, it's going to take me forever to add things. But I check out other apps, and they just don't compare to the options this one has. Please keep updating. :)

Has potential. It's cool that you can scan in your items and it does a good job, for the most part, of finding them. The recipes section was quite a bit lacking. I'd like to see more "simple" recipes available (for instance, if I have macaroni noodles and Velveeta cheese, then simple 2-ingredient Mac and cheese should show up). Mainly because it would be really cool to have the ability to "use" a recipe and it automatically remove that amount of product from your pantry.

Subscribe, it's well worth it for the increased flexibility of program. Pros, scanning, easy to add and subtract the number of items, easy to explore app without accidentally changing things. Con - I haven't figured out how to enter items in multiple places without scanning item multiple times or to move them from say, fridge downstairs to fridge upstairs. and where's the help menu? But I'm having fun figuring out and minimal frustration.

It's a solid app. The free version's not great but it's certainly enough for you to evaluate whether you want to shell out for the premium version. I'd do that but I'd REALLY like a webapp included on top of this. This is great for scanning and "gathering" your inventory but it's not so good for the sorting and manipulating that's better left to something with a real keyboard and a generous sized screen.

Neat idea for an app - it has most of the features I was looking for! I love that it lets me choose which category to file items under, & expiry dates. Downsides - it runs slowly and crashes often. The barcode scanner doesn't work so inputting your items takes a long time. It doesn't realize paprika and smoked paprika are two different spices so it keeps combining them into one which is super annoying. And I wish I could put in my own recipes that I use regularly, to make meal planning easier.

I'd been looking for the perfect kitchen organizer app for years. Kitchenpal isn't perfect, but it's the closest one I've found and has the most potential. I love the automatically adding low items to my shopping list & the expitation date warnings. You'll spend a couple of hours getting your food scanned into the app, but it is worth the effort. Not everything you scan is in the database, but it's also easier than most apps to add or modify items.

After a while, the barcode scanning functionality (and certain other features) gets hidden behind a paywall. You need to use WiFi to access your shopping list in a grocery store. It doesn't save your sorting method between sessions. If you just want to delete an item, it still gets added to your shopping list and you'll need to delete it there, too.

I love having an app that allows me to not only keep up with what's in my pantry but is easy to use. I just scan the barcode and I can even update the picture if I don't like the one that it defaults to. I also had a great and speedy response with the customer support email. I had a little trouble with getting the app to install on a new tablet but after a few days of them working with Google PlayStore they were able to resolve it ☺️. Definitely would recommend!!!

Loved this when I first installed, but as soon as I paid for premium it went downhill. Crashes all the time, voice search no longer works (worked initially, making adding items so easy, but now it just gives me a message saying it will send to Google for a transcript...which it never does). App only works for a few minutes and then freezes and crashes. If it worked consistently and voice worked, I'd give it 5 stars.

So far we're loving this app! We've been able to scan everything in the pantry and fridge and it's so nice to see what you have right at your fingertips. We plan on scanning more too. The reason I'm not rating 5 stars is because the app does crash occasionally. Not a huge deal and I haven't lost my info. Just an annoyance that makes things take longer when adding items. That being said, I would still 100% recommend this app! One of my favorite features is the recipes suggested based on dates!

The barcode scanning feature is the best part of the program. If you planning on tracking a lot of items you should look into a pc based database. It takes this program forever to respond after every entry. It crashes all the time, yet doesn't seem to lose data. Very aggravating to use because it is so slow ! ! Once it has your data there, there isn't much that it lets you do with your data, like a simple print out of what you got. I regret using this app for my food pantry project needs more simple database features to manipulate the food storage data the way we want !

This is a great app but lacking functionality and practicality. Adding things to the pantry is fairly easy. The barcode scanner works great and what can't be scanned can be added manually. I'm giving it 3 stars for a few reasons: 1) They say it's free, but after a certain number of items, you have to pay to add capacity to the pantry. 2) If you try to add a recipe, there's a lot of measuring metrics missing i.e., no cups, tsp, or tbsp).

It's pretty good, but it needs way more recipes. I have a similar app that produces hundreds more recipes I can make simply because it is more flexible on the ingredient substitutions. And as someone else mentioned, having smoked paprika and regular paprika in my kitchen seems to confuse the app. Sometimes it pulls up one after I select the other. Weird and annoying things like that. Also the drag and drop in the kitchen is very difficult for me, it zooms past the tab I try to put it into.
