I started out really liking this app, but the more I use it the more problems I'm seeing. Example 1 - It takes forever to load when I first open the app. Example 2 - It randomly adds ingredients to my kitchen which confuses me on what I actually have and I have to manually delete them all. Example 3 - It won't let me add a picture to the items I create, it lets me take the picture and "okay it" but once I go off the app the picture deletes again. Very close to just finding a new app!

I liked the idea and the reviews are stellar. So I was about to buy the subscription, but then this thing started crashing every minute so I am not so sure now... Could be worse though - could have started crashing after I paid for the subscription :) Update: Tech support got in touch and were most helpful. So I gave this app another shot and I really like it. The recipes are good and refreshing, and my first grocery bill was 50% lower than usual.

We downloaded this app and started doing inventory in our kitchen. This app is the solution to all the headaches we were experiencing with our meal planning and shopping. From keeping inventory to making shopping lists to making and planning new meals. We quickly realized that this tool is worth the lifetime plan. We had an issue with our premium features not working. I sent an email to the support team and within minutes the issue was solved. I really would like to see a desktop version.

Looks like it could be a good app except adding everything manually is going to be a royal pain. Half the things I started scanning came up with no data or wrong data. One of the items was cranberry sauce and I had to laugh at the data when it said I had an inflatable airplane lmao. Not sure I want to manually add all the items to set this up.

I would love to subscribe to this app, and it was between this app and another yet this one seemed easier to input items. Except when your inputting an item in manually, it seems cumbersome. also, wish they had a drop down menu for the stores you frequently visit rather than me having to input the store each time and a purchase date. Both those features and an easier way when inputting manually would have me sold on this app.

Okay app, but it absolutely is not free I you actually want it to be useful. All of the useful features are locked until they receive payment, which makes the "free app" advertising false advertising. The recipes are okay, but again, you can't even use the search function without paying 1st which is just downright ridiculous.

I spent an entire afternoon adding things to this app and organizing it, and everything has been re-organized according to the app's algorithms. I now have salad dressings under "Beverages", Goldfish crackers under "Cheese", and pecans under "Sweets". All the categories I paid to add have been removed. What a waste of my time. If you don't care about being able to actually find things in useful categories, this app is interesting, but if you are looking for a kitchen management app, go elsewhere

They must be using AI, because every feature in very dumb. scanned items have pictures of completely different products, the categories are nonsensical, and the recipe suggestions are useless. I didn't have any strawberries, and only canned corn, but all it would suggest for me is various fresh strawberry and corn ON the cob recipes, pretty much ignoring all the other, more appropriate ingredients. Very busted, probably just a data scrapping scam.

Awesome app, but some recommendations: have options for how many days before an ingredient expires before you want a warning; have an option to set ingredients to automatically be added to certain shopping lists and certain food groups (having to move foods back to the food category I want them in after moving the from the shopping cart every time is taxing); add an option to create your own food type categories and food storage area categories. Awesome work so far!

Poorly designed... If a product scan incorrectly, it is a hassle to correct. You gave to scroll through countless products to find the product you have because the magnifying glass does not bring up a search box to search for a certain product so it makes doing a pantry inventory a hassle that should be quicker and easier me. Change that part then I could give it a high rating.

I've just downloaded this app and love it so far! I have Multiple Sclerosis and trying to remember if I have an item or not while I'm shopping will be easier with this. I love the ability to scan things! I have seen several reviews where I think people just need to explore a bit more to fix their issues. Pantry & spices are done, now to the refrigerator. Thank you, developers!

Recipes are hidden behind a $12 pay wall. App will scan barcodes, manage inventory, notify you about expiration dates freely. Uses pre-generated list of 'free' recipes to display whether you have the items or not (tested across 4 accounts on 4 devices and 4 separate inventories). I had neither salmon nor sea scallops and the top recipe for my kitchen was salmon w/sea scallops based on the fact I had butter. It contained no recipes for any of my ingredients whatsoever (not even pbj or spaghetti).

I really don't like this app. First, you can't import recipes to it from the web. Sure, it maintains a link to the recipe you are importing, but if that page moves or the site is not available, then you're out of luck. It won't import from recipe keeper. Next, the ingredient interpretation leaves everything to be desired. A can of diced tomatoes and it'll tell you that you need whole tomatoes. And you can't change it. Pantry sucks to. You can add 14 or 21 ounces of chicken. Not 16 tho.

Thought this app would be something me and my family could use to track our groceries but after using it for a few days it is lacking. We can make a shopping list but when she shares it I can initially.view it but if you close out of the app and try to go back in and view the shopping list the app displays a crash notification and shuts down the app. We can never consistently view each other's kitchen or anything. Sucks because I had high hopes but on to the next app.
Zoho simply makes your organizational workflow, simple, smart and professional! highly recommended 👌
Such a useful app to keep track of my stock and sales. if i had to pick a con it would be that its slow but that's not a train smash

I have just started using the app, but it is working great! I have been looking through many inventory apps, and this is my favorite so far. On top of being free, there are so many features including adding prices that other apps make you pay for. My only complaint so far is that I wish the prices were shown on the inventory list instead of the unit or even the option to have either. I feel like that would be more convenient. Otherwise, highly suggested.

I keep the snack inventory at work. We only keep a small selection on hand and rotate different items weekly. Since we're using the honor system and none of the items are sold for profit, I gave the free version a try. It's AMAZINGLY USER FRIENDLY and does just what I need to track what I spend and what people put in the collection cup. I can only IMAGINE what the full version is capable of doing. I TOTALLY LOVE THIS APP!!!!

It can be a little time consuming BUT the value is unbelievable! Everything is on point when you enter in the data. Love how you can attach files and photos, record payments and shipping data, purchase orders. If I could get it to link my shipping label data to my customer package section it would be wonderful. One shop setup for all my transactions on the go. Love it!

This app is great. It includes every field, every thing I was longing for. But despite being an Excellent inventory option, it lacks a basic function to change inventory with+- without editing pen button. It's very time consuming. I have to deal with hundreds of items. Editing each time would be very time consuming. If you add this basic function, it would be truly fascinating. Please add. update: Found in inventory adjustment. Thank you.

Easy to use and accurate. one thing I would like to be corrected though is that once a bill is raised, it deposits inventory quantity as stated on the bill into the system. this way, sales can go on before receiving. I think inventory quantities should be deposited after receiving. thanks. I love the app though. it's a great one. all Zoho apps are great. will always recommend.

Great app!! However, a number of downsides; can't use browser on the phone as it always opens on the App even when done on the phone browser I can't simply access the browser on the phone as it will always open on the App (unless I unistal the app 🤷‍♀️) Secondly, the search engine is not user friendly when doing transfers and processing orders as it won't generate results unless you type the exact item name and not just a key word ..quite time consuming.
Helped me a lot as a Junior manager. so fluent and friendly on the nerves.

Was using the simple inventory tracker app when it popped up saying I could use groups for items in this inventory app. Sounded great so I downloaded it.Except the group function doesn't do anything.Its just a blank page with sort/filter settings that are useless. There is no way to add items to groups on the group page or while adding or editing an item This was the one feature I needed and that was ADVERTISED TO ME and it doesn't even work. By night I'm a small business owner and by day I'm a product owner for inventory software (not a competitive product as the one I work on fits a very niche market only) I can't say enough good things about Zoho and this app is no exception. My business does kitting of some of our items and the composit item function is a life saver. Lots of different item description options etc. But in this inventory management application you cannot actually track the location of your inventory which is literally step bloody one. Absolute utter joke of an app. Deleted and will never use again especially seeing as how the company itself said that it would have that feature out in 2021. So they also shamelessly lie to their customers FYI.

The program functions like a dream.. but it's a bit cost prohibitive But functionally, I couldn't ask for more. One critique: If I create a payment method of escrow, it changes to paid as a status. Technically, escrow needs to close or be disputed...so ideally there would be a status of IN escrow, then paid/closed. I would suggest adding escrow as a preloaded payment form and adding the other two invoice status....that's the only work around I found that I needed. Not sure if I can afford it

I really love this app. It makes keeping track of your inventory very easy. I don't know if I missed it but I need an option to include partial payments. Like the customer paid one amount and will pay the balance at a later time.

playStoreReviews.text: This is a very nice app. I have a couple suggestions that the devs may consider implementing. It would be nice if there was an option to display the image of the coffee bag more prominently. Possibly where there is an image of the bag that takes up a large portion of the card and then right underneath the roaster (+country) and origin are displayed. Then you can click in to see more details. It would also be nice if we could group the entries by country of origin or country where the roaster is

This is so close to greatness. I don't understand the details that it lists up front for each added coffee. Do you select a coffee by identifying it's altitude/variety or do you identify it by the name the roaster sold it under? I bet most people choose the latter, but you can't see the entered name of a coffee without clicking into it. You hit the wrong one? Now you get to repeat that until you eventually find the correct one.

Useful app. Nice interface, I have not found major bugs. I do not agree with the chosen pricing model, luckly the important features are in a cheap one-time purchase. Some features are missing: cupping as a brew method (a quite important one, I'd say, to compare one coffee to another) and water recipes in the equipment tab.

I like this app. I can key in all the information that i used to write in my coffee brewing journal. This app even plots the taste profile for me, and allows me to compare, at a swipe, the different coffees that i have brewed before. Within each type of coffee, i can compare the results from my previous attempts and brew methods easily. The layout is very easy to read. I would give it 5 stars if it could also plot the SCA TDS/ Extraction graph for me.

Very good app. Lots of options although not fully customizable. Unfortunately it's linked to only one device so when you switch phones you loose your whole archive, unless you pay a subscription. It doesn't make sense to me just for the new device every two or three years, so I won't keep using it, but other than that it's really good.

I've just started my coffee journey and i will say this is extremely helpful. The customization is amazing but what I really love is the detail. It's a great app for tracking progress and really seeing your pallet grow. I don't normally leave comments but I wanted to for this app. I see other people complaining about the price but $45 AUD is nothing compared to the amount of money I've already spent on coffee. If you want a cheeper option, get a notebook. I think the features are worth it.

Great app but way too expensive for local functionality. We should be able to locally store as many brews/products as we like. Totally useless for anyone wanting to use it as a record of all their brews. And no one is going to spend the crazy high price just to store more locally. I understand cloud backup being charged at a higher price, but not basic functionality. Update: changed my review rating after changes to app pricing model. This is now worth the money to support the best coffee app.

Interface feels a little clunky/cluttered sometimes just due to the huge amount of data it can keep track of. It would be nice to see the data summarized and aggregated different ways, like what coffees were rated highest for a given brew method, or to quickly see what grind size has given the best rating for a given coffee.

Let's you track coffee, roasting, and brewing. Very nice app lets you track a lot of information. You can get as detailed as you want. I like that you can enter the type of coffee you have, and then brew are linked to that type of coffee. Tried other recipe based apps and to change some things means generating a new recipe and recipes aren't linked to a certain type of coffee. In this, brews are linked to a specific bag of coffee and you always know what you're dealing with. Makes sense I hope

So far so good. Online pricing a bit steep but at least the offline features are reasonably priced so i got those. The app is great for its intended purposes. A couple of things i would add 1. A recipe option. So far been using it as equipment option. E.g. using the JH 1 cup V60. Creator: James Hoffman Link/url/video: xxxx Steps: 1, 2, 3 etc 2. Filter by brewer type (v60, origami, etc) 3. Screen customization hide . E.g tds, extraction yield which i dont use

Everything was good until you signed out. Everything will be lost, and this is a strange way to force you to become a premium member. It's like they betrayed you for money. Even if I wanted to pay for this app now, I would never do it because they might sell your data for a higher price to third parties.

I went from using a note app to this and it's been scratching the itch for organization and ease of use. I love being able to creat a bean profile and then being able to register different brews in it. I've started paying attention to fine details in tasting notes since using the app and it's been fun!

Tbh, I really like how it works generally. Simple and easy to use. A few things bothered me though. 1) The price (That's slaughter) 2) Anyway to link the product to the brew method? Combination of products and methods yield different outcomes. 3) More details for the brew method (Bloom or No Bloom, if Bloom, what's the duration, how many pours etc.) That's my humble opinion, will keep supporting it.

I understand paywalling sync but basic features such as >15 products, >3 local pictures, and >15 PDF data export for $4.99 a month, $34.99 annually, or $134.99 lifetime is outrageous. Unlimited local data entry should be free or a reasonable one time purchase price as there is no overhead outside of development costs. Furthermore, there should be local data import/export in standard formats such as csv or md. This is all hidden before registration. Good design, horrible monitization. Hard pass.

I have three different coffee tasting apps I'm trying out and so far this one has been the easiest to use and provides exactly what I need. a lot of easy features that work well in the mobile app including slide bars for taste.

Great app for coffee lovers I'm no coffee aficionado, but I have upgraded to a decent hand grinder to go with my Aeropress, and wanted an app to journal all the tweaks that I can now do to achieve the perfect brew. With the recording of brew settings, measurements, and flavour profiles, this app is making me reflect on the results of my brewing efforts. I may soon be that coffee aficionado.

i like the app, its a way for me to simplify the "trial and error" while learning to make good coffee. i just dont like that some sort of community and ability to interact with other users doesnt exist. adding my friends and comapring our brews would be a very good thing. " have you tried the new brewing technique? check your ibrew i sent it to you"

My favorite coffee app so far, have brewed over 250 cups and tracked them in the app and it works wonders. Probably the best app available for this kind of thing if you don't want to create a spreadsheet for your daily brews!

Amazing app. May I suggest adding some stats like which origins, processes, etc the user liked the most based on ratings? It would really help people narrow down certain types of coffees they might prefer.

First impression: WOW! It works exactly how I want, with plenty of features and interesting options. I will try it for a couple months but I wouldn't be surprised if I end up paying for the annual subscription or the lifetime plan! Excellent!

One of the best recipe entry apps I've found but no searching and finding recipes of others. Combine the recipe features of this app with the share and search of Good Fika app and it would be useful for me.
I like the interface and tracking abilities a lot. I would love to have even a read-only version on my Pixel Watch for when I'm not trying a new recipe!

Fantastic and highly useful app for us coffee fanatics. Dev is highly responsive. Offline features are fairly priced, up to you if the cloud sync is worth paying for. Either way it's worth it to support the dev and this very helpful app!
Very high price for what it is. Big number of limitations. Subscription-based model. Some kind of hybrid app, not native. Thanks no full-screen ads.

Really good app for tracking coffees and brew methods! Wish more features were free, but may subscribe in the future, will see. Fairly happy with it and looking forward to future updates!
Please don't stop developing this app! Best app I've found for recording brews and note taking.

Amazing tool, great database of roasteries, perfect user-friendly note-taking for each shot, very detailed, helps a lot with staying on top of your brewing game.

The best app in it's kind I could find so far. Of course, it's not perfect, although it does its job nicely and the UI is tailored pleasantly. Personally, I was surprised by the number of available equipment options and roasteries from the stock.

A simple option and useful would be a CC'd order notice to another email address so that another person could keep up with / print orders too. Overall nice job. Thank You.

If the app loses internet for more than 1 minute it does not reconnect once the internet is back online.(even if you have the app open all the time) As such all orders are missed until you restart the app only to see a flood of missed orders. This is horrendous as a team in a busy kitchen does not have time to check the app every minute to see if it lost internet briefly and needs restarting. They listen for the sound. Old app was much better than this.

So I'm giving one star for these reasons and because the app literally won't accept orders if it's running in the background and crashes if you try to email feedback.

Good application. Fully satisfied best ..........no tension of management
Great app solve gym management issue and really helpful
Great application to manange your Fitness Center..!
Everything is fine but there should be follwup reminder. Otherwise 5 star from my side

An app that for once is generous in features to its free users. After about a year of use, I have only seen updates to the app that have contributed to the app as a whole. Bonus points for actually including patch notes with each update. The app has helped me commit to a personal goal of gym activity and it would not have been possible without it.

Been using the free version for years and finally upgraded to the paid plan for the ability to add more templates now that I'm more consistent with weight lifting. I'd love it if they added more smart watch integrations besides just Apple watch. I'm not on the Apple ecosystem & being able to workout without my phone would be awesome! I was able to quickly set up Health Connect to import my Strong workouts into the Google Health app.

I enjoy the simplicity of the Strong app and love the notes/sticky notes feature. However, on Android I can’t duplicate individual exercises in workouts or templates, only full workouts. Also, replacing an exercise erases notes and sticky notes. I’d love an option to keep them. Built-in fields for body side, equipment, RIR, goal sets/reps, rest, and tempo for each exercise would also make tracking much more organized and efficient. I am a Premium User.

Nearly perfect but needs another post-workout option. Currently you can assume all non-completed exercises were completed, or delete them. What if the gym was full and I didnt get to it today but want to leave it for next time? App just needs a "update weights only and dont delete non-completed exercises" option.

I've been using this app for about 2 years and I love it dearly. Simple and straight to the point, no ads in your face all the time. I still havent bought premium and you can use almost all the app without it. Some people complain about the fact that you cant save more than 3 workouts without the premium version, but you can just go into the history and click "perform again" and it automatically pulls up the whole workout. I've downloaded atleast 20 different workout apps and this was my #1.

Honestly, this is an amazing app for the gym. You get up to three free workout setups and can choose what machine/movement you want, along with reps, sets, warm-ups, and even rest timers. It saves and tracks your previous workouts, and during the workout, it shows what you did last time (reps and weight), along with total weight moved, and tracks your volume per movement. All in all, it's an extremely helpful app when you're at the gym, whether you're new or have been going for a while.

I purchased a lifetime membership then stopped using it for a few years. Started working out again and realized it had glitched and my membership expired despite being lifetime. I emailed them with receipts and they fixed it immediately, apologized, and we're extremely easy to deal with. I also love the layout of it, it's simple and easy to use. Overall great app!

I've used the free version for two years now. Love how it tracks my workout data, and the UI flow is better than any other fitness tracking app. It is so intuitive and doesn't feel clunky at all. My only request is for the app to add specific stretches so I stretch more and better! In any case you can add these yourself (as I have) but this would be very helpful.

Been messing with setting up a routine for over an hour now instead of working out. Seems decent. Trying to do ramping sets via the paid warmup feature to auto calculate the percents with rounding. I can't be bothered to do the math myself. Why are warmup sets not counted in metrics? that's dumb. Warm up sets don't seem to populate after the workout is started. There is a dark theme and the rest timer doesn't interrupt your media which is nice. I'm glad there's a free trial.

I've only used the premium, and cannot say if the free is different. I've been lifting for 12 years and this app is the closest you can get to blank paper that you format yourself. I can save workouts, and then the app will tell me the previous weight I did for any exercise, and I can adjust for linear progression. There are better apps if you're following a periodization program (Wendler Log for 5-3-1), but I'm recovering from surgery and this is perfect for getting back to it.

If your looking for a ridiculously easy way to track weight lifting and cardio, here you go. You see your last rep / weight / value when in the middle of adding a new exercise instance. It may benefit from a small 'help' file indicating how to add exercises and what each colored box means but that's minor. It really easy to use and track changes.

Way, way more intuitive and easy to use than Jefit.  Only problem I see is that, when tracking a workout, I don't see any way to view things about the last time you did an exercise other than the sets information.  I can't see any way to view my notes from previous times (I make notes of how I set up machines, etc, so that I don't have to guess every time), or to view more history than the prior time.  All I seem to be able to do is to have it prefill the weight and reps from the last time.  Now, the prior notes and history ARE available in the app, you just can't quickly view them from within the exercise while actively tracking a workout.  And, IMHO, that's precisely when you need that information the most..  Instead, you have to navigate out of your current workout to get to the general description screen for the exercise in order to see the notes and full history.  Why not just give links for this information from directly within a workout (as Jefit does)?

Best way to record sets I've come across so far. Pretty simple actually. I love it. Update: Recently I wanted to add kettlebell exercises to the selection. It never dawned on me that you could just create the exercise. They probably get a lot of request for new exercises, but the truth is you can just set this up yourself. There are a lot of neat little features hidden in this app!

If you are looking for a workout tracking App that is reliable, and doesn't crash this is for you. From loading workouts, to tracking PRs 1RM and more this app is fantastic. It has coaching to show how to properly complete the lift and you can also add any custom workouts to track. There is also a place to add notes to the workouts and a built in rest timer. You can even build a custom workout and share it with friends. I highly recommend this app it is best on the market for sure

Great app!! Especially for a beginner like me. I would like to ask your team if you can create a possibility for me to add a picture to a new exercise when I am creating a new one in addition to a area of the body and the category. I am visual learner and with a picture it much easier to remember. In addition I would like to ask you if you can add a note "common mistakes" in each exercise that I can add some more notes. Thank you for your great work!! Best regards, Lena

App is straight forward and easy to use keeps track of your previous workouts and allows you to see your progress, some exercises are not in the selection but you can always create one from scratch and it will be available to choose in the future. There is a pro version but as of now I have not had a need for it. Granted all I am looking for is something that can replace a pen and paper at the gym not a "Robo Coach".

Not sure how running logs work. I put a time and # of miles and it blanked out the miles when it saved. Also not seeing a place to change the date of a logged workout. Besides that, I'm liking it so far. I switched from StrongLifts 5x5 because I wanted to do different exercises but a similar routine. This gives me that flexibility.

I use Strong as a workout guide. It is extremely helpful in giving you ideas for routines, in demonstrating the workout focus and how-to, and is handy when creating your own workouts (excercises). Plus, it is excellent in keeping track of your progress in both workout frequency, personal records, and previous reps and sets from other workouts. I recommend it a lot to beginners as well as frequent gym goers. I have not purchased the premium account yet and have had the account for 2+ years.

3 years worth of data gone. I'm not able to access my data even though I tried to log back in with the same account. Absolutely infuriating. I recommend getting the app, HEVY instead. Much better UI, better integration with smart devices/other fitness tracking apps, and more frequent updates. The premium service is also cheaper than Strong.

This app is great for tracking gym workouts. I've used the free version for half a year, and there are no annoying ads or videos. It saves all previous workouts, shows if you hit a PR, and saves up to three workout templates. You just search an exercise, type your reps and weight, and check mark when done. If the exercise you're doing isn't listed, type in a name and specify the measurement (distance, repetition, weight, etc.) It'll even show videos on how to use the machines around the gym!

I've used this app for a long time, love the tracking and format and constantly recommend it. As of today, for some reason, I was automatically logged out: "session has expired" (which has never happened) and I had to reset my password. When I logged back on all of my workout history was gone. Quite a few years of recorded workouts/prs, and the analytics and insight tracking have all been completely erased. Needless to say, I'm pretty disappointed.

Strong app is the most practical app on the market. Being able to create exercises is my favorite feature, considering I use the conjugate method. I wish there was an option when creating an exercise for unilateral movements, adding band tension, and make people be able to be friends on the app!!! so you can look at each other's workouts and exercises!!!!

I'm 17 and have a crazy schedule, so using a "12 week workout journal" didn't work well for me. There were a ton of blank pages because of how it, along with most other books of this kind, was structured. That being said, I began to search for another solution... Enter: Strong. This app is great! It has a prebuilt selection of exercises as well as an option to add your own to your personal library. Edit: 20 years old now, 45lbs heavier, and nearly 500 logged lifts. Still loving Strong.

FINALLY WITH RPE!!! This app is great. I've used Strong for over 1.5 years, and it was always "good enough". It was flexible and pretty reliable, but because there was no RPE, I also had to log on paper. With the addition of RPE on Android, Strong is now at the top of the market. There are some niches (e.g. couch to 5k or whatever) that Strong won't compete against, but for a central workout logger, Strong is the best. (Especially for a powerlifter like me). Thanks Strong for making a great app.

Great App! Download it!!! I started using this app, free version approx. 2 months ago. I transfered over from logbook. Took me a little while to figure out how to create my own PPL workouts. Amazing app. The recent update streamlined the app. Suggestion: When adding a new exercise to a designed workout there should be an option to add the new exercise (established/created) where you want it. Not at the end of your designed workout. I may be updating to the paid option. Great App!

I downloaded about 10 apps all at once and started to narrow them down. I wanted easy tracking to add and edit exercises and record measurements amd i wanted graphs for everything. This app does it including adding graph widgets based on exercises. So far no complaints at how easy and nice it is. It beat out bodybulding, jefit, among many others. Jefit was the closest but syncing with google account was topper for me.

Been using the app for years holding out hope that they would someday add the option to categorize workouts into folders/groups but it hasn't happened so I'm looking at other options. Love the simplicity and ease of use but scrolling through a giant list of my workouts that are organized by the time they were created, not by grouping, is just getting old and is way to time consuming when I just want to get in and get out.

This app is amazing! Easily track, customize and even shows you what the exercises look like. The tracking is awesome. You are able to go back to a year ago to see how far you've come. Also tracks the volume in a given workout routine. That would be cool if theres a total volume per exercise as well. Another great feature is that you can customize workout plans to your needs (push, pull, legs, etc). Combining supersets, the app is thoroughly thought through. 👍

This app is seriously perfect. No bloated features and great user experience. I love how they have thought of create custom exercises, replace exercise, show history, etc. all these little simple to use things make it easy to make adjustments mid-workout. I'm doing a push, pull, legs rotation and this is great for mixing in a few different custom template workouts for any of these days. I wanted a bunch of templates so I upgraded to paid, I think free only came with 3 custom templates or so.

I used to have all the features they tout. But I broke my phone, got a new one and now I cannot even do a 5day workout routine. And I am not even sure of my account info as the errors I get trying to log in are not informative. The price is way up to $100 for lifetime. . .it a good app but not at that price. . .especially when you cannot import your exported workouts and routines. There needs to be easier support for users accounts. I understand you need to get paid but. . . Strongapps you have gotten a bit greedy. Open it up features a bit more to make it usable, put ads in it to get paid, add in app purchases to add certain desired features, letting us skip other and sell ad free version and then you will make money without alienating your users. Just saying it's a decent app but others do the same for less. Now I am going back to an older app.

I love this app. It's so simple and does exactly what it says - it tracks your progress and lets you create, edit, and delete workouts. It keeps track of your PRs, and what I find most helpful about it is the calendar that marks every single day you've been at the gym. It is so motivating to see all those blue dots stacking up in the calendar and seeing how far I've come. This app is definitely worth it in my opinion.

This app is exactly what I want and everything I need it to be. I'm NASM certified so I don't need a huge amount of coaching or preprogrammed workouts (it's there if you want it). Just a simple way to log my progress on my regular exercises. Still kinda new to the app, but I would like to have a way to add custom exercises/variations but for now I pick something close and just put a note on it.

Great app. Deserves closer to 4.5. only complaint is a lack of many common exercises. Otherwise, the rest timer and ability to track sets are top notch. Edit: Original review was from 2017 when I gave it 4 stars. Coming back to this app in 2021, it has updated the exercise database and includes just about everything you could ask for. 5 stars

TONS of features. This really is the best workout tracker I've ever used. I believe there was a premium buy in for the full product, but it's worth it. I'm getting greedy only giving 4 stars. I'll give it an easy 5 stars if you can pause workouts. You can finish a workout, or leave the workout running in the background, but you can't pause the workout or choose a recently finished workout and continue. Sometimes, life gets in the way of a routine and I have to finish it a couple hours later.

The simplicity of the app makes it perfect. I just want to track my weight-lifting progress data and this app lets me focus on just that without all the extra advice bloat. Only reason I gave it 4 instead of 5 is because the create exercise feature could use more options, like adding pictures of the exercise, or changing the icon or having an about section like the default exercises.

Love this app, as someone who previously only would workout with my memory as my guide (and maybe a notes app log) this has been a gamechanger. Makes it super easy to see my progress and train accordingly to my goals and weight/rep targets. I just wish more languages were supported. Would be nice if Arabic for example was available. Otherwise, phenomenally app!

Not enough excercises to choose from. You can create exercises that aren't on the list, but there aren't enough categories to choose from to separate body parts or types of equipment when creating your exercise. Once you create an exercise, you can't edit it. Annoying timer if creating a superset in workout. Difficult to cancel a workout that is started, and to figure out how to keep previous templates saved (if there's a way) after editing it. I should have stuck with fitnotes or another app.

Sensational. Great organization of exercises and workout templates, allows easy integration of RPE and other more advanced training considerations. Also, just let me say that it is incredibly pleasant to see an multiplatform app that isn't a bad implementation of the iOS version. In addition, easy pro subscription model with no ads. Wish I could give you guys 6 stars.

I am giving it 5 star only after downloading and using various other competitive apps that even have higher downloads. This app does what I need and without error. I am still not on their premium version (and I feel it will be worth). I am writing this after 7 months of use. It have 3 templates that I use for my 6 days workout and absolutely have no trouble in following up and logging them. Exercises name may differ but you can always use their note option to add a note for the exercise.

I had recently posted that I loved the app. So much so that after a couple of days with it I purchased a monthly subscription. BUT NOW it's not letting me use the pro version to have more than 3 workout routines. Need help because I want to test it and if I like it will purchase the lifetime subscription. If I can't have this issue fixed I will cancel the subscription and find another app.

Very nice, useful app to keep track of you workouts with daily, weekly, monthly logs, measurements, and workout demonstrations also keeps track of weight improvements, sets and repetitions. Highly recommend. Took me a while to find an app like this without paying. No ads aswell for free. Can't imagine if you go pro whats included...

The app has a simple and easy to use user interface that streamlines your workout planning and weight tracking. It helps remind you the last weight and rep count you used on every exercise which is very helpful when you haven't done an exercise in a while. You can write in notes for individual exercises which is helpful when you want to remember what settings on machines you used so the next time you don't have to waste time getting everything in the right position. I love this app.

It's pretty close to what I'm looking for. I used to use WeightXReps years ago, but the WXR app feels clunky. This is close to notebook logging, but I find that the exercise history list is too short, so you have to scroll past a bunch of exercises you don't do to find one of the dozen that you do perform. Another issue is that it's not powerful enough for planning. I can add notes for RPE, but I can't really chart out blocks ahead of time, because the app really only supports static weight.

This application has been such a great companion throughout my weight loss and muscle-building journey. It keeps my workouts on point and allows me to add endless amounts of new exercises very easily. I have many different routines I've built over the years that I can easily hop back into with a tap. The app has steadily improved and I am glad to have paid for Lifetime

4 updates that would make this a 5 star review. 1. Allow reuse of the exercise icons or to upload new icons when I create new exercises not in the database. 2. Have Kettlebells be one of the exercise types. Right now I have to choose "other" 3. Allow us to choose multiple body parts when creating a new exercise. 4. In the workout tab section have the option to make a template without having to fake a workout being completed first. Seems like useless extra steps.

Loving this app so far... I particularly like the ability to mark drop sets and sets taken to failure, as well as the ability to go to history and see weeks volume, something that I feel is key to a great workout program. A dark mode would be nice as well as the ability to remove the default example plans, but other than that, this is your go to gym application for resistance training. Keep up the great work guys!

I have been using the app for a couple years. Got a subscription to be able to save more templates and to track my progress. The new "goal" setting keeps me tracking that I improve each week and I'm loving it. I recently had an issue with my user info and support was responsive and got me back on track in 24 hours. Totally recommend.

Great app. Been using it for years and the dev has continued refining it. But I recently switched from iOS to Android and there are some features missing that I'm hoping you will add soon. For example less chart options (total weight lifted for exercise) and other little features such as % volume gain when you press the 3 dots during a lift

I have been using this app for years and absolutely find it essential when working out! Highly recommend! The only suggestions I have are more stats for the treadmill other than distance and time. The addition of Incline and speed is crucial to me to make sure I hit my times. I currently use a separate note for that. And the other suggestion is the addition of some sort of HIIT timer/mode! I just think that'd be useful for my HIIT workouts. Thanks so much for the work ya'll do.

I've been searching for an app to keep track my workouts and I think this one honestly checks the box on everything I was seeking. Database is good but doesn't even really matter as it allows you to create custom exercises. I had the free version for about an hour until I signed up for the annual membership. I normally don't pay for apps unless there is a lot of value add and this one has not disappointed. Stop looking and just download. For the record, I'm not affiliated with the developer and this review is strictly to help whoever is reading.

Intuitive design makes the app easy to use. Good pre-made selection of workouts plus the ability to create your own. Tracks weight, total volume of workouts, PR's, estimates your 1RM, and remembers previous weight/reps/sets for easy reference during workout sessions. I do wish you could flag an exercise as unilateral when creating your own. Otherwise the total weight of the session is not accurately counted. Still the best app for weightlifting imo.

Exactly what I needed. Easy logging/journaling, a few basic workout templates, and some basic instructions for each exercise. I think a good free feature would be a comment feature for individual sets (e.g., "form deteriorated here") for more detailed tracking. A great paid feature would be a workout generator that works off of the equipment you have available. For example, I plug in "barbell, rack, dumbbells" and it generates plans using that equipment, ignoring cables. Great app

Really loving this app, and while I do have an issue I'm still giving 5 stars because it's generally everything I need. My main issue is that whenever I edit a routine, whether it be adding an exercise, including a note, or changing the order of exercises... updating the routine after the exercise does not include all of these small changes. Not to mention if I have different exercises with the same name (preacher curls that are hammer/barbell/etc) the app can't seem to differentiate.

As a workout tracker it is great. It is really nice not having to remember what resistance I have been doing for every exercise and eliminates the need to carry around a notebook. The share feature is excellent and makes it easy to keep up with friends that can't go to the same gym. Being able to build your own routines and the large variety of exercises to choose from keeps the gym fun. There are a few things that are missing or could use improvement, but as a free workout app definitely a 5!

Pretty good app. Would be great if you good add to the workout movemeny options so I don't have to create so many. But I would really appreciate it if you could make the buttons bigger for inputting the weight and repetitions completed for each set. It's sometimes difficult to correctly input the correct numbers when your hands are shaking and your fingers aren't working the best after completing a set.

Great workout app. Best I've found so far. Love the versatility. This app is one of the few I've found where I can separately time interval between exercises and intervals between sets. Makes my workouts flow smoothly and saves time overall. Also doesn't incorporate glitzy garbage like many other apps. Just all the basics I need to perform and analyze my workouts.

No need to carry around a little notebook for tracking! I've been using this app for YEARS and I've loved it. You have an adjustable countdown time, easily added and altered exercises, and an overall seamless workout experience. I especially enjoy looking at my history for specific exercises to see how much I've improved (or not 😅).

This app is really pretty decent. I paid for the premium for the year and would say it's worth it. There are a few glitches; as far as I know there is no way to remove a set once it has been added without removing the exercise and then adding it back. If you finish a workout and forget to click finished there is no real way to adjust the time. Only gives the option to set to AM. Just a few foibles. I would and have recommended the app to friends.

The best workout app, but consistently buggy. Notable bugs: 1) it has an internal timer that stops working without any notification, 2) the app automatically navigates away from the active screen, forcing you to go back again to figure out where you were, 3) it constantly asks you to save a finished workout as a routine, no matter how many times you tell it not to, but... it's the best available workout app, so suck it up and download.

Simple to use. After trying other popular gym apps this was the best one for me. Clean interface, easy to input numbers as I'm working out, etc. The only questionable thing are the images used to demonstrate the exercises. For example, the benchpress image has the guy holding the bar over his neck. Don't do that. The text explanations tend to be correct though.

I've been using this app for a little over a year, and I think it's great! Definitely one of the best ones out there. I like the customizability of workouts like being able to make supersets and add notes, and the animations for different exercises are very helpful. If I could change anything, it would be to add a feature to group workout templates into "Programs" or something like that. I tend to rotate my weekly workout routines and it gets a little messy trying to organize them all.

This is the single best workout tracking app I have seen. I've used as many as I could find on the play store and once I started with strong I have never looked back. Been tracking for 1.5 years with this app and I won't stop. I hig highly recommend paying the small price for premium (even though the free version is amazing) because the progress charts and ability to save unlimited workouts is soooooo motivational. To the developer - you're amazing. Thank you.

Easy to use, but Pricey for full version. The free version is decent. 3 max for custom workout routines. All of the exercises have instructions and pictures, most have a short gif showing the motion. It is missing some exercies, but has most of the main ones (sometimes theyre named different from what I know) Full version is $99, just outrageous. Id probably pay $5. Seriously don't understand their price point.

Great app. I've tried many different workout log apps and the best thing about this one, for me, (because I mostly do supersets due to time constraints) is that when you start a workout, the whole workout is on one page. You just have to scroll up and down. With a lot of other apps the process would be; log an exercise, hit back button, pick next exercise, log it, hit back again to go back to the previous exercise. Too much back and forth. A lot of other great features, too.

This app is well designed and very intuitive. The timer is fantastic! Being able to add/subtract on the fly is very handy. It's great that you can set individual timers for the exercise you're doing...and it persists that for next time. It's great that you can setup your workout in advance, then work thru it, then save it as a template! I've been training for decades, using notebooks and other apps...this is by far my favorite and very much worth the subscription!

Simplifies keeping track of things. I've been keeping track of all of my workouts through Google keep notes for like two years. This app makes it infinitely easier to track my workouts over time, track my rest periods, change workouts, and it also gives me more fine tune data on how I did. (For example, total pounds lifted/moved during my workout) Can't recommend this app enough.

It is a great app and the ability to aet up routines is nice. I love how it even goes into detail how to perform the workout. The only thing i would like to see is the ability to create folders in routines and drag and drop your routines in any order you see fit. Some workout routines you create may be standard rep count and pyramid style weight ratio while other workouts may be pure warm up and dropset style. Both are viable schools of thought. It would be nice to keep them organized.

Overall great app. Easy to track your workouts. There are a few aspects I find annoying... There's no stopwatch for timed exercises like planks, so I have to open up a timer separately on my phone. Some of the details are not explained to you. For example, it took me a while before I realized you could swipe left to delete a set. Also, if I update the template of a workout, it will delete an exercise if I haven't done it that week. But overall good.

I've been using Strong for 5+ years and it's great. Easily tracks weight, reps, warmup sets, whatever for nearly any strength exercise. And for the ones it doesn't have natively, you can add your own. The one thing I miss in the Android version that was in iOS is the automatic tracking of the last weight and reps for the same exercise.

Not a bad app, I really like that I can customize my workouts and have them all in one spot. I will say, when I add a new workout, I put notes in them, but they don't show up when I'm actually working out. Another thing that would make this app better, is if I could organize the workouts better, instead of having them all in just one list. Maybe on a calendar format with each workout assigned to a specific date or something? Otherwise, terrific app.

I used to really love this app, but there have been a lot of new updates that I just don't love. Both in aesthetic and function. Big buttons, so much white space, the auto rest timer changes, etc. Plus the free version is very limited in the number of workouts, but the pro pricing is insane. Dunno it's just disappointing. Haven't found a better alternative yet though.

I've tracked my workouts on paper for years, never liking the couple of apps I tried. This has been a game changer for me, and has made working out easier after a period of struggling with it. I like the easy tracking of personal bests so that I know where to set the weight next time, and the built in timer. I'm currently using the free version and it meets my needs really well. The paid version has cool bonuses, but it's still a wonderful app regardless, which is how I think it should be.

I've used this to track over 200 workouts at this point, and I really like it. It's straightforward, not many frills, but it is solid and thoughtful. It's easy to use. In addition to tracking a workout by entering the weight and reps, you can add custom timers, designate which sets are warmups/fails, create supersets, save templates, takes notes, and track progress. If what you want is a solid, easy-to-use, workout app, this is it.

This app is worth the (New Year's Special) year subscription to me. I've cancelled my annual subscription, though, because it seems unlikely they'll get a web version which I would prefer to use when planning a new workout phase. I will be hard pressed to find something as good in every other area though (there is nothing that has a web version as good right now). The app is quick, seems to have thought of everything, and is customizable for exercises they don't have.

I've tried over 10 if the "best" weight training apps and this is the best one around. The simple and clean interface makes it easy to use without all of the fluff. The only things that are keeping me from giving it 5 stars are the following: ability to save more than 3 workouts with the free plan (I run a 4 day split), a larger exercise selection like JEFIT, and an app for the Android watch so I wouldn't have to constantly pick up my phone.

This app looks promising. I rate it low because of the way they have chosen to charge. You can only save 3 routines with the free version. That doesn't give me much time decide if I want to pay for the full version. Limit the measuring, charting, and tracking. I'm fine with that. But if I only get to store 3 routines I'll be moving on to a different app before I get close to deciding if I like this one enough to pay the outrageous price for the pro version.

The one thing that can keep me from going to the gym is purpose. Of course I need to go for the activity, but what's the point if your going only to go through the motions. This app won't give you purpose, but it will help you track your purpose and the goals you are looking h to achieve. It reminds me a lot of the sheets we use to use back in high school when I played football, buy much more intuitive. I dropped it on star because it does not sync with Samsung Health, only Google Fit.

This app is really awesome at logging your workouts. It's has a pretty comprehensive database of the more common exercises and will give you instructions on how to do them. It tracks your PRs, rest periods, and let's you customize your own workouts. The free version limits you to 3, but the premium is unlimited, I believe. The main area that could use improvement is more app integration. It does integrate with Google Fit, but that's it. Luckily, Google Fit integrates with most everything else. I pay the monthly subscription fee and feel it's definitely worth it.

New to fitness, learned about this app on reddit. I love it because it has explanations of how to do each exercise with demo if necessary, and its easy to navigate. I set a goal to use it 20 times then im purchasing the premium. Edit: I got premium for Android, and it was not worth the purchase for me. It may be worth it for folks that are advanced and have been using it for years or have set routines, but it isn't worth the cost for me. Unfortunately I bought a full year instead of monthly.

I've tried a lot of digital trackers and this is the only one I'll ever use again. When I started working out I logged my workouts on paper because I hated every app I could find. After finding this one it's the only one I use and now, as a trainer, it's the only digital tracker I recommend to my clients. I have only ever found very minor things I would change, like being able to choose the exact measurement for each exercise, you can customize that, but not to the extent I would prefer.

Really like the app. The only drawback I face is the template limit of 3 in the free version. I wouldn't mind paying to get more, especially if it was a 1 time fee, but the pro subscription is too expensive especially since I only care about workout templates. Would be good to have more pricing tiers, kind of a "semi pro" or "amateur" plan without charts and measurements. Almost perfectly what I wanted :(

This is by far my favorite weight lifting tracking app. It has a ton of lifts pre-loaded exercises and allows you to creat new lifts if needed. The UI is simple and easy to use. It has great reporting and tracking of exercises over time. It is simple and I think that's the beauty of it. Super easy to use and if you just want to track your workouts without a pen and paper, look no further. Their subscription price is very reasonable. I'm a lifetime subscriber. 10/10 would HIGHLY recommend!

So far I have yet to find another app that is so blatantly straight forward and simple as this one. No frills or thrills, just what you need in a tracker app to keep whatever program you're on logged. The days of memorizing the total amount of sets I had to do for each exercise are gone. Now I have more memory bandwidth to remember the more important things in life. Thank you!

Easy to use, great for recording moves/exercises, can also create, add your own. The animations for some moves are very helpful. Cons: only 4 Kettlebell exercises in the library, there should be more with popularity of Kettlebells + should be added as one of the categories. I regret purchasing the PRO, dont see the benefit, especially since there hasn't been an update since July 2022 (The free version is sufficient). Please make the "plate key" feature interactive so we can add plates ourselves.

I've been using this app for almost 4 years I believe and it's been great. I change my workouts often. I love being able to search for exercises. The time between sets is great. I can't say anything negative. The graph showing your gains and your average workouts a week. All great. When I transitioned from iOS to Android I couldn't remember what email address was tied to my account and their customer service was top notch! They responded and got me logged in within several hours. Thank you!

Easy to use and a clean interface. I like the user friendly layout and the ease of entering data as I workout. I really like the countdown timer that I can auto set to start at the completion of each set. Everything is on one page just like the paper logs I have used for the last two years. I've looked at many apps and this is the best one I have found that works the way I need it to for my workouts.

You can only add 3 workout templates before you hit the pay wall. So could not add my 5 days of workouts to put the app through its paces. Most of the app is locked down. It did at least synch and add the workout set I tested to Google Fit. Not worth the price in my opinion. There are much better apps with more features available for less price.

Easy to navigate little app. Haven't run into any issues so far. only logged two hours into it but I like it a lot. A lot of exercises to pick from and if you're not sure what to do even gives you some ideas. Editing: over 40 workouts logged and this thing is still amazing. I decided to pay 4.99/ month to support developer. still find it amazing.

Pretty good. I've been using it for a few weeks now, and upgraded to premium. Could you please allow the use of the keyboard when entering weight and sets? It only allows you to use the touch screen numbers, which can get pretty annoying. I am using a laptop and I keep moving to type it, forgetting I can't use the keyboard. Small annoyance, but it would be nice.

This is THE BEST app for keeping track of things, though I am not a fan of the newest update. I miss the floating + exercise bar. In addition, it keeps asking me if I want to be a paid subscriber when in fact, I already am, and will throw me in this loop asking me if I want a subscription. I really liked it better before as the aesthetics I don't care about so long as the app works and is easy to use. Please bring back the floating exercise so I have to do less scrolling. Bring back favorites and get me out of the become a paid subscriber loop!

Great Workout App. Lots of built in exercise options or make your own. $30 a year or $100 for life. Pretty simplistic, but has everything you need. Post workout needs another option. Right now it's Adjust values, ignore changes, or modify routine. If i modified it, it effectively deletes what I didn't do, and adds what I added. There is no option to add exercises without deleting others. If I juat want to add to a routine mid workout, I cant.

I'm a huge fan of this app because it tracks workouts in a helpful way. I would only say the paid version isn't really worth the money because the graphs I bought didn't provide me with the stats I'm interested in (I wish it had average/best pace for running instead of just total distance or total time alone).

Mediocre but not terrible. Easy fixes would elevate my review. Big new annoyance: doesn't sync across devices. WHY IS A LOGIN REQUIRED THEN?? Lots of exercises MISSING, which is normal for these type of apps. (For example, shoulder rotations.) BIGGER ISSUE is when you try to add exercises that involve Cables, you can't select Cable.

really disappointed with recent updates, you changed the way timers work and now I have to enter in timers for each exercise, I liked the other way where I put a timer and it effects everything. if I'm on chest day like I'm on today, and I went to add push-ups and now it's harder to navigate through the exercises to pick what I want to add.. the old way much better. I like the new screen look or background but that's only about it. need to give us options to customize it the way we want.

I really like this app a lot. You actually get a lot for the free service. You can save 3 workouts, so I save my Push, Pull, Leg. If you pay for the service, I think you can create as many as you want. I really like how it saves your progress, so you can see where you left off and if you do a change to your plan, you are given a choice if you want to update your routine or keep your original. Very impressive. Also, it's easy to quickly set a set to Warmup, drop, and failure which is nice.

Before the most recent update this would have been 5 stars. The update now forces me to remove timers from every exercise every time I do them. It also now malfunctions if I try to do things too quickly, which makes it impossible to add weights or reps to the targeted exercise (instead it fills numbers in previous sets where they shouldn't be). This causes me to have to close and re open the app multiple times during a session.

So there are some super annoying things with this app. It doesn't recognize searches for exercises unless you are extremely specific. Want to do a shoulder press? You must search for "overhead".Press, shoulder, dumbbell, or anything else will NOT find the exercise. Make a mistake and count 100 reps instead of 10 and not notice it until your next workout? Impossible to fix the mistake and you will never see a pr ever ever again due to being unable to correct the error. Dimple programming fixes.

Easy to use, great features, exactly what you need if you want to track your work without hauling a paper and pen with you. Been lifting for nearly 20 years, best app I've tried. It's only weakness, is the exercise library. They've got all of the usual movements, but it could use some expansion. That's all with the free version. Considering going to paid, it's that good.

Generally I really like the app. I have two main gripes. First, they seem to have gotten rid of the exercise timer in the new version. I use that for timing core exercises. Second, they dont have many planned workouts, which it doesnt seem difficult for them to add. Otherwise great.

Love it. One recommendation, if you can have it so that you can merge 2 separate workouts into 1 that would be awesome so that you dont have to spend the time rebuilding 2 workouts into 1. Also if you have the same routine for lets say 1 month then you would scroll left to see the progress instead of having to scroll down and look for that same workout the week or two before. This would eliminate digital clutter. Otherwise I love this app. It's the best app to track workouts I've ever seen.

I've tried a bunch of workout tracker apps over the years, but this one truly stands out. The design is clean and easy to navigate, and it strikes the perfect balance between simplicity and powerful features. I can track my gym sessions, log sets and reps, and see progress over time, all without getting overwhelmed.

This is the best workout app! After having tried all possible gym workout apps I have found finally the most complete, easiest, and most elegant app. The design is slick, the app is fast, the stats are so good and the options to track every exercise with different equipment and track the stats per equipment is so handy. The support is also fantastic, I contacted them a couple of times to report some small bugs and they fixed them right away. I recommend this app to all gym enthusiastics!

HIGHLY RECOMMEND FOR BEGGINERS, gives you a Free Routine to follow, And Also Gives other routines for free, And Guides A begginer easily other than other Apps that let's you do it by your own without knowing what it actually makes you gain, The app Also compares Other similar exercises easily and Highlights muscles from Lowest to brightest on where the exercise focuses more. And each Routine gives how many reps you'd do. Which is a good App for Working out

This app is fantastic. I was going to complain that routines required a set weight (I've been off for a year's time), only to notice that they have added an option that automatically sets the weight from 1RM or previous. This was basically the only thing I was missing, so I am definitely subscribing again. I will say that the free tier is a bit too naggy in my opinion, though. I think the added features are sufficient, so I would drop the nag screen when opening the app, personally.

Terrific app, with great insights and loads of exercises I can leverage to create my own workouts. I was searching for ages for a great dumbbell workout app, but a lot of the popular ones were missing some of my favourite exercises and had what I would say are essential features missing or poorly implemented - been really happy with this app so far as it has everything I wanted and more! Recently decided to go for the premium subscription too - excited to see how development progresses!

I really love this app! I'm still pretty new to the gym so a lot of the exercises I don't feel because I'm not doing them correctly however, this app has videos inside the app that help you. On top of that, I love how the app doesn't force you to pay. A lot of the other apps I've tried out for, you only get like 3 free workouts and then you have to pay however, with this app, you get a free amount per week and you get to choose your workout days which is nice.

Aerialist Fitness Tracker! Ok, this app is not exactly made for aerial silks conditioning, but being able to create my own exercises makes it perfect. I lift weights, but the majority of my workouts are bodyweight / calisthenics. Considering aerialists have to hold themselves 20 feet up with bare hand orbent knees hanging over a metal bar or fabric. I also have attention/focus issues. Having this tool is going to really help me stay accountable and organized. I just may benefit to purchase.

I just love that the free tier is so full of features. I'm not fan of paying a subscription up front before using it or falling in love with the app (like I'm about to do with this one). After trying pretty much all workout and finess apps in the store I really think this one is the best so far and the premium tier is worth it just to be able to log more than twice a week and get metrics and graphs of your progress. LOVING IT. It has so much potentiel.

I have downloaded this app after looking around for some affordable without all the bloat BS that's most won't need, I definitely enjoying this so far. Only improvements I would like to see is -INSTRUCTIONS include a show or take you to images rather just YouTube videos. For me it'll make sense as soon as I see pic, rather watching talking for a few minutes before they get to the point.

Less user friendly than Hevy. Not as many onboard exercises available and nothing to show how they are done. Creating an exercise isn't as easy as with the other app. After trying out both, I subscribed to Premium on the other app and uninstalled this one. Edit: Because you quickly responded and in such a polite way, I increased my review to three stars for good customer service.

I love how customable everything is. The customer service is super quick and friendly, too. I had a few questions before downloading or purchasing a plan, and they got back to me right away. At $55/year for premium membership, you can't go wrong.

I've been looking for something like this for a long time. For me, it replaces a personal trainer. Great layout, no bugs, all the exercises explained clearly, easy to customize. No more needing to plan or think about my routines.

i like the first impression. this is the kind of app im looking for. it feels both fun and powerful. and some game feel while maintaining minimal user-friendly design. i face issue/bug that the RTL languages like arabic breaks the some of app alignment. i have to change my phone lang to english to fix that. hope you fix that issue and makes the app looks same for both RTL and LTR languages.
Awesome, good value for the premium version, but you can't add images to the custom exercises, so I'll keep looking for the perfect app.

This app is the one of the most effective apps in order for you to track your progress in your workouts, But please add more workouts for it and it would be nice if your premium plan stays on that pricelist. 😎
That's a tremendous app, perfect for tracking workout, weight and measurement!
Might have a bright future if the UI is simplified. Currently it's unusable since I can't add custom exericses.
Best, super organized, concise and easy to use. I love planning & tracking my workouts with it
The greatest app for gym logs, it's also SUPER cool looking! it really helps me have fun doing this whole fitness thing
Love this app! So easy to use, and helps me track my workouts. Highly recommended
This app has a great UI however some common exercises aren't there by default, also almost every important feature such as backup are behind a pay wall.
seems great so far, and I'm probably going to upgrade to the full thing.
Such a good user interface, 14 days free subscription. Thanks
Excellent app. Integration with Google fit would've been amazing.
Best app for training in a right way, in gym either at home! Thank you
A great easy usefull app for gym guys .. really like that one ❤
It's the best app ever and the support team is fast to make improves. Tks
Amazing app, so helpful for my workouts 💪 ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
Nice app but the thing I want or maybe my friends too are all in pro(subscription)
The workout didn't change for a year.. all year along worked out the same muscles
I was looking for an app to stay tuned and I found it.
Easy to use, looks cool
Really enjoy using it
Two workouts a week free. Pathetic
Best app this apps features are insane🔥
How to create custom Exercises I didn't find
Awesome ! After two months, I love my body !
very good for begginers
We need more exercises
no picture taking post workout
This apk works great
Love the experience!
Very slick app 👌
App looks great
Up to the time.

Application prometteuse. J'aime beaucoup les designs qui sont épurés des différents groupes musculaires mais efficaces et didactiques. Et tout simplement, le choix entre musculation homme/femme disponible est un vrai "+" !Ce serait top d'avoir davantage de formules de souscription, par exemple une "lifetime subscription"ou des forfaits plus avantageux pour les budgets étudiants. Dans tous les cas, une belle découverte et en attente des futures mises à jours qui approfondiront les fonctionnalités
Good app
Nice
Terrible UI
