Best free app for organization! Works well even for stuff outside of home! Would love to be able to share the list to other people and have them be able to add things as well! Even if it cost a little bit for the premium feature!

I wanted a small, flexible, free phone app in which to implement a complex card-based game I invented. I did not discover Find My Stuff until after installing and deleting dozens of other list apps. This utility has all the basic features I require: unlimited hierarchical structure, flexible text fields, ability to copy and append branches, export and backup capability. And it (mostly*) all works. (*There are issues with opening txt files from Drive...) [more to say but at max chars here]

Really needed this 10y ago! I have a large inventory of old motorcycle parts and supplies. I'm now slowly adding everything to the app. Really like the simplicity of it and how easy it is! I do have 1 question/request: is there a way or plans to add a way to share the inventory? So maybe 2 people can work on 1 inventory and everything is synced?

Every item can be broken down to more items. I wish there is a way to add more information on the item, like input fields for manufacturer, serial number, etc. At the very least, please add a notes section per item.

The app is easy and simple to use. Perfect for keeping inventory. I am able to use barcodes and images to search for my items now. Best app I have tried for home organization. I wish it was available on IOS.

It has everything you need and nothing else. Simple and professional interface. Most important part of organicer app is that its FAST and SIMPLE to use and this app definitely delivers just that. Also easy to backup and export list of items. And all this for FREE. Thank you Miquel

EDIT: I mentioned the lack of fields as a downside, but that's no longer the case :) The UI is super straightforward and responsive. I also love the option to use Google Drive as the cloud backup, and thus avoiding the need for recurring costs.

This app is exactly what I've been looking for for a long time. Need a simple way to record where I put things in my house to help me find them later. App makes it easier to record items (with pictures), organize, and then search.

This app would be wonderful, but it's hard to use due to having multiple phones or sharing with my wife (The share is difficult as it's a file and not cloud based so I would have to reenter everything on each phone.) If not this would be the perfect inventory or item catalog ...RECEIVED QUICK RESPONSE AFTER REVIEW... Until approved for Google drive this is an excellent app for single users ( Best I've tried)

I was looking for quite some time and only found overkill or expensive solutions. This app is exactly what I was looking for. Very clean and intuitive UI. Love those details like "Save+New" to speed up the process. (Even tags are added based on the previous entry) So good! Very powerful search function! Thank you very much!

Ive tried a lot of inventory apps to keep track of my building supplies, tools, and consumable items, and Where’s My Stuff is by far the best. The interface is straightforward, the design is clean, and everything is easy to navigate—perfect for someone like me with ADHD. Unlike many other apps, this one is completely free, which is amazing in a world where everything seems to be pay-to-play. If you need a simple, effective way to organize your stuff then look no further.

I love this app. Thank you Miquel. It has helped me feel more organised and in control of my stuff. I love the fact that it's also free and not littered with ads and distracting. Simple and clean UI. Keep up the good work!

This app is maticulously planned and programmed. If you have a phone camera, it has everything you need to organize your stuff; add more stuff later to an existing container, add individual photos for each item in container, move multiple items at once across containers , add smart tags. add scan QR codes, backup your app data with several choices. Only negative: the back swipe always takes you back to the first container. Not a big deal until your list grows. Incredibly laid out!

I...love...this...app! I was looking for a quick simple app to inventory things that are in storage containers in my garage. This is it! So simple! Title the box, include each item with a name and a photo! Perfect!

I'm very impressed by all of the functionality this app offers for free, I used it for moving into a new house and worked like a charm. it is feature rich and flexible as it allows to import and export data in common formats. No intrusive adds, nothing but a great user experience so far. I will definitely purchase the productivity tools to enhance the experience and support the developers. Thank you for such a great app design!

Okay, but I cannot find out how to add an item or a storage space. There is only one "+" button and it seems to sometimes add one and sometimes the other. Okay, everything is a container. That is a little confusing but totally fine!

I have been searching for this type of app for a long time. I tried some other apps also. But they are not up to your mark. This ideally suits my requirements. Besides the UI is fantastic. So I contributed Rs 480 for the developers. Thank you and Best Wishes.

This app is great, but entering items is a bit monotonous. If you could arrange some way to optionally have labels/items generated by object recognition in the photos, that could really improve usability and speed.
Dead simple design, ergonomic, feels great to use. I just needed something to keep track of my pantry and fridge, and this works fine. Allows you to export your list, which is a nice touch.

I use this app to organize pokemon cards, its incredible! There are so many features and all of them are toggleable, meaning I only see the features I need! Also the new(ish) in-app camera works great and makes things extremely convenient.

I use this to keep track of my tools and it is fantastic, not only does it keep a tool stocktake but you can keep track of who you have loaned your stuff too and add warranty, maintenance and purchase info would recomend use 100%

I wanted a very simple home inventory app for my lyrical storage ways, think cottage fairy🧚‍♀️. I use words more than photos, clouds are a hassle. The container concept is easy: apartment > living room > north Wall art > silver woman with flowers and hand painted flourishes. I don't think this app will slow my shopping though, as I donate like a fairy godmother 🤭. Cannot comment on photo handling.
I tried multiple apps to keep track of my tools and stuff, this one seems to be the best one I found. It's not too complicated and it has just the right amount of options. Also no ads so far!
Almost perfect! Needs larger font and an option to sort your rooms and your things stored or used in your rooms. It's the best of its kind, so far, and it's free!

This app is excellent! Quick adds with pictures and item name. Excellent search facility! Unlimited items with free version with in app purchases for one time gifts to developer, just how I like it! Bravo!!! 👌

This app is everything I've been looking for! MULTIPLE CATEGORIES and sub-categories! Locations and sub-locations! All created & customized by me. I can show "Quantity: 0" without deleting the item. Or the opposite, I can mark an item "Need To Buy" which puts it on the shopping list, even if it's not out of stock yet! And then I can sort the shopping list by store. I use the item description for multiple tags since the search bar finds both names and descriptions. This app deserves 6/5 stars!

As long as I can convince the family to keep using it, it's going to be great. The app itself is great. Easy to add, easy to subtract. Adding the expiration dates is huge, had the kids help scan the pantry, found a couple items expired, showed them how to scan out. Getting the wife to do it will be the real challenge. Suggestion: Have pre built categories. Have an option to auto categorize based on the product information, if someone doesn't want to use it then they can create their own.

great app, streamlined and what I was looking for. very easy to add items, images, modify, and adding stuff to shopping list is a bonus for when things need repurchasing. not as many bells as whistles as other apps but that is partially the appeal (no weird popups or "suggestions" cluttering things up. I love it and bought a year sub so I could share pantry with my partner!

I just downloaded this app yesterday and although I haven't found a need to use the pro version (yet) the free version has changed my life in less than 24 hours. I am still inventorying my pantry and found so many duplicate and outdated items until I felt bad because there are people that literally do not have enough to eat. This app so far is a definite game changer. No more buying what I already have.

This is the perfect lightweight but flexible pantry app. It took me a minute to figure out how to add new categories and places, but then it was super fast to make an inventory. The barcode scan feature is excellent and finds the large majority of products that I scanned. This is huge because it makes it so easy to inventory your pantry. There's also an easy feature to add an item to your shopping list when you use it up. Very glad I found this!

So far it does what it says it does. Alerts when something is expiring is a premium feature. (It says need premium to receive texts) When using scan function to input, it just puts what it is, not the rest of the information like size of can or things like that. Good options for organizing, but I wish I had more color coding choices (no color for category). Maybe mine is glitchy? If you put in expiring dates, it pops most recently expiring to top.

I love the simplicity and customizablility of this. Gets the job done without features that are too complicated to use, so the utility isn't obscured by too much upfront effort. Exactly the features I needed without the clutter. This will make it easier for our household to keep track of freezer meals and ingredients so it's easy to meal plan without having to match what we have to an arbitrary database or follow someone else's level of granularity.

I've spent the last few weeks researching food stock inventory apps, and so far this is the best one that was easy to install, get category set up, and have items sorted! I decided to go ahead and try the trial, so I'll check back in a week, with a hopeful declaration that I love this app! (I already do, but never commit immediately 😁)

I love this app! I have 3 freezers and hated having to look through them to find what I was looking for and it was always in the last one I looked in haha. Now it is so easy to find everything first time, I even adapted it to work for items in my fridge. Makes shopping easier too, because I know at a glance what I have run out of, I highly recommend 👌

I like the app a lot, but recently I noticed whenever I try to edit things in a description of an item... After some time, it starts to highlight wherever I move the cursor to, is annoying but even worse is that it does not clear the highlight after another tap on the screen. I've tried from another phone and the same thing happens, and if I text in another app (like this one) I do not see that issue happening.

Amazing app, the only couple of things bugging me is that I can't remove the expiration date after it's set. I can turn it off in the edit screen, but it still appears in the list. and would be great to have an option to set custom date format. also, I'm using the manufacture date field for when I open the product, so maybe add a field for open date, and ability to show it in the list beside the expiry date.

couldn't live without this app! I mean it's just fantastic. I have mild memory problems and am always losing things. this app has been so helpful in enabling me to log things as to where I put them, and then being able to find them by simply typing in the name. thank you very much!

Great database app. Very well thought out and designed. If you have a large freezer (or more) that has a lot of inventory, this app is a lifesaver for keeping track of not only what you have, but when it expires. I highly recommend!
lifesaver! I use this app exclusively to keep track of our inventory at our lake home. I like that it is customizable for me. Additions and deleting are quick and easy.
fairly easy to use once you get the hang of it but needs better instructions or more intuitive design. not easily customized and lacks flexibility to adapt to various uses.
This is one of the best and most underrated inventory apps on the Play Store! The subscription fees are affordable, the UI is clean and easy to understand, and everything is well categorised.
This is a great app for monitoring inventory. Very useful. There's no ad and the app is respnsive and fast.
easy and simple with option for excel import export. multi user too so u can co manage with a housemate!
Exactly what I was looking for, it's simple, clean, no ads, ... nothing is going wasted anymore!

Excellent app. Offers a simple inventory management system for home use, unlike other apps I tried which are designed for businesses. I really like how items can be grouped by categories, and that you can add extra notes in the description field. Most features are available for free (without ads!), with some added features by subscription. My only gripe is that the subscription costs are not listed, but there is a 7-day free trial if you want to try these out. I haven't gone down that path yet.

Been looking for a simple, basic food inventory app for ages! I love that it's not over-complicated. Interface is easy to navigate and easy to use. I can add my food by barcode, and if it's not there, just simply give it a name, no going back and forth etc. Only bother adding expiry for the food running out soon, but a really great little app, especially for inventories and helping with food waste. Shopping list feature is also really handy. Happy with it 😁

So I with whole family finally figured it out. This app is not intuitive for me, but it is still best in the market. I think having lists and than categories is confusing, because you can add only one category. Maybe if you would be able to assign more (like with meat - frozen, chicken etc.), it would be better. I love how fast I got a reaction from developers, I didn't need help after all, but they care - this is a good sign. Also, without paying it would be useless, but it is not expensive.

Overall a really good app! I love the customisable categories, items can be properly organised! Moving items between inventory and shopping is a bit clunky - you must pick the number of items each time. It's buggy if you have a checklist enabled on an item and switch between "inventory" and "shopping", doesn't mark the item as ticked. Would like to automatically move item to inventory once checked. Lastly, resizing the list or a minimal view for quick reference while shopping would be nice.

Like the categorization, search bar for finding items from all categories (also moved it so that you can choose it rather than it defaulting to search bar upon opening), barcode scanning is cool, they made reorganizing storage available, can move more than 1 item to different storage in one go. I would write these by hand on and place them where I store them, but would have a hard time meal planning and grocery shopping with this method. This makes it easier to access the inventory.

Good app but still unable to move items to other storage without clicking the storage icon which in turn resets the searched list. I also came from their old app (similar app) that they sold and is now trashy. Now I have to pay for the lifetime again. Hope they don't resell this app again. Edit: I rated 3 not 2 to be fair but I'll increase it to 4. The older app is literally unusable at this point. I can t click anything and unable to export my data.

This app in my opinion is the simplest straightforward app for inventory management in multiple different applications. ie on the sales floor or in the back room. **** one feature that would make this app even more of a power house is if you could find a item in your inventory from your shopping list. example: you have a shopping list and want to see if you have the item in your inventory. This way you can use your shopping list as a Pick list as well for your own inventory.******
easy to use, not complicated and affordable
I just put ally personal inventory in. It was really fast and easy!
once you get your head around the different features, it's easy and very helpful
It's easy to add items and update them.
Perfect for my needs and so easy to use 🥰
Good and easy app. I gave it 4 and not 5 stars because of the UX_UI but still it's recommended
has streamlined my freezer and pantry inventory really well

I've had this app for a while now. and it has been really nice. this is not the first such app I've had, but it is the best I've had. I like the detail I can put on the items.The ability to create my own folder headings is very helpful for me since I like to track not only my food pantry but also my diy herbalism products as well.

Great app for grocery or anything with a barcode. (Though an actual barcode isn't needed as items can be manually entered) Features include Location (Multiple for overstock etc), UOM, Source(s) Cloud Based Product Barcode lookup for auto entry, Expiration Date (simply scan product again for multiple exp dates) Paid version has even more features like adding product images. Using free app now, but plan to buy.

Solid app, does everything it says it does. Runs smoothly, scans consistently. If I had any suggestions it would be including a recipe database that suggests meals based on available ingredients or populated a shopping list for ingredients if you need them for that recipe.

I like the app. There are some things that are needed to make it great! I will share those in the dedicated place for recommendations, however, it is a good paid app option. I do wish the "do you like this app" would stop popping up every time I open the app. I will rate the app when I am ready and to have that pop up all the time (as a paid subscriber) is a bit annoying.

At the moment I'm a new user. All I can say for the moment is that it easily does everything I ask it to do. I started with groceries and associated shopping lists. I'm now expanding into household inventories. I'll update the review when I have more facts to relate.

absolutely the best thing I've ever found in the form of an app! I have diminished vision and sometimes it's difficult finding things in my cupboards. I tend to misplace items. with this app, I just search for something in my cupboards or my pantry that I have entered, and it will tell me what shelf it is on. thank you!

I've had a pretty good experience with it. Easy to manage items in categories and storage, which I've had the most experience with. I'd like it if I could move and sort the categories themselves, but it's still a good app.

I just started using it today when I found half my pantry was expired. So far I really like it, especially since you can scan the barcode and add expiration dates for individual cans. It also hasn't asked me to pay for anything other than adding a picture so far.

This has been a great app for keeping track of the contents of the freezer. It does exactly what I wanted. It is easy to make categories and keep track of date purchased. But, best of all, it allows an entry to have a zero value without deleting it. I can see at a glance if I am out of a staple.

Just what I was looking for to keep track of my pantry contents. After I accidentally bought 1lb of dried Oregano TWICE it was time for an app like this. I'd like to see an option of lists with less room between lines to fit more on the screen.

I keep seeing people say easy to use. I believe the app would be very nice but it is difficult to navigate or completely understand, especially when trying to create a folder, storage or category...if there were some basic instructions it may help. Get mixed up with adding items an they show in a different area sometimes called uncategorized? If provided a guideline I'd increase this rating to 4

I needed something that was friendly to glance at because I have to track food for the week. I try not to waste but it happens so now I look during the day see what I have to eat first & that's what I have for supper. Waste is also in my cabinets, refrigerator so I'm doing that name date etc. Just wish it came with instructions it took a little while for me to get the hang of it. Just want to know how to delete something in a box. Haven't figured that out yet. THANK YOU

Since this field has limited characters let me rephrase my thoughts.. My problems with the app: -The description doesn't tell what functions are behind the paywall -The lifetime access price is way too high -Theres no option to change the currency The upside: -Seems like the developers actually read the reviews and respond as well, that deserves a star -Works the same as other inventory apps but the free version is ad-free so far, so there's another star

This is a pretty good app I like how it works I wish there was faster way to transfer a bunch of item from one foulder to another and I think there should be an onbordeing but I do really like how there are NO ADDS and they offer a 1 time payment or mounthly for the premiem (which you dont at all need to use the app they just give a couple quilty of life things) but overall vary good loke the app

A very cleverly programmed app. Almost everything works as I imagine. Only deleting entries in the catalog should be possible. Otherwise perfect. And since the price for unlimited use is still high but affordable: I'll probably pay to support the development.

This app is terrific! it allows me to have multiple freezer inventories. it alphabetizes the entries to make them easy to find. very helpful to keep track so you don't waste money buying what you don't need.

Easy to use. Initial set up can be time consuming. Love the easy way you can put items onto the shopping list. Appreciate the way you can import/export into Excel. Would like to be able to copy item details easily.
Makes for easy inventorying of all food. It takes some time on first inventory, but it's a breeze after that. My only wish is that it allowed for grouping different sizes of the same item together.

Easy to use, customisable to different categories of items. Perfect for my simple need to keep stock of all my essentials, from skincare, toiletries to food items. Love that it's tied to my email account, so info will not be lost when I change phone.

Updated: Moved to this from old app which has crashed & burned. Luckily managed to export/import and have now got everything looking the way I need. Had an issue with the categories not saving dedicated Individual Expiry Warnings. Reported via review as Questions/Suggestions wouldn't work. Super fast response from developer who confirmed fix already being worked on, and has now been implemented today and all resolved. Love the app & really impressed with the support.

I'm still figuring out a few details of it's use, but it works well for my needs. I've tried several other apps and I don't use the shopping list feature, but it is very useful in organizing home inventory with expiration dates and minimum levels. I am very happy with it.

good to use to manage your inventory. so that we have a full view how many left and what need to buy. it would be better if the storage can appear side by side with the item. then we do no need to flip through the item to check the location

Amazing!! So easy to use! Recognized most barcose and even when it didn't recognize the barcode, it used barcodes to allow me to name the item. I was able to inventory my entire cold storage within just a few minutes!

I could use or consume the product without it expiring if I could set a notification a few days before the expiration date. Often, food products go to waste because I forget I bought them. The same applies to cosmetic products. Etc

a very good inventory app to have. simple and easy to use. it will be nice if the uploaded product picture can be shown as thumbnail beside the product list. also good to have a Web based version so that we can work either on mobile on Web page.

Expense manager along with inventory management would have been great. just show me a piechart report of expenses based on category. as I included the purchase cost when adding items. Option to create a budget and track debt payments, such as amounts borrowed or lent to friends, colleagues, and EMI payments. Further reports, such as recurring costs etc Include the option to upload a receipt and warranty card with expiry date notification for non edible products.

This app is absolutely incredible! It's exceptionally easy to use, and the ability to scan both barcodes and QR codes for inventory management is a game-changer. The developers have done an excellent job creating a truly intuitive and efficient tool. This is a must-have for anyone looking to organize their freezer, and we've even found ourselves using it for much more than just that! Highly recommended.

So far, I love it! This is exactly what I need. It's user friendly. Clean interface. And help me to stay organized with my pantry so far. They made the whole process smooth and easy at your fingertips. I haven't tried the Pro version yet though I'm thinking of the possibility of getting one.

I required an alternative to Sheets. I trialed and decided to pay FOREVER. I have also started to use it for other lists such as Tasks. Being able to share on other devices is awesome. On the current application, I have 2 requests. 1. Adding Item : additional date field for stock is checked or updated 2. Adding item : stop the Purchase Date updating the Expiry Date if Expiry Date entered first.

This is a must for me because I get paid with food by the local food bank that I volunteer/work for. I have to move fast in sorting which food goes where, how soon it will go bad, and whether they're to be frozen, shelved, or canned. Knowing exactly where the food is is very important to me. Home Inventory works wonderfully for me.

Great app that easily migrates inventory automatically into a shopping list when quantity gets low. Also amazing customer service and response to several questions in emails. Will be buying the lifetime version!
This is exactly what I needed. I've been using a Google Keep note to keep up with my backup pantry stuff in my basement but it isn't searchable. I like that you can add expiration dates too.
very good app. very easy operations and convenient to use.
i love the app, so convenient and easy to navigate
Simple and to the point. if you are having trouble seeing categories, press settings>global>disable features section and uncheck all of the boxes. Took me a second to figure that out.
I love this app. It has everything I want. Bar code scanner, expiration date, low quantity indicator, price, categories for location, stores and etc. It's the perfect app for shopping.
Easy to use and even the free version is robust. I don't use it for everything, but I like being able to track things I only use occasionally so I don't end up with duplicates.
I like the app but when trying to share it pops up a box to add text but you can't type in it anywhere and it won't send to recipient. I have to have this!
This is a well thought out app. It's flexible enough to apply to my complicated pantry setup. It would be fantastic if it integrated with Alexa somehow!
For my needs it is perfect. I have inventory scattered in different places and this helps me keep track of it and not overspend.
my only problem I have is the camera as a scanner sometimes scans the last number. But I will say that they where very helpful when I told them about the problem!!!
Easy to use, with scanner or manual entry. Wish it would recognize bar codes but I know it will after I enter my favorites.
So far, I really like this app. Normally, I don't leave app reviews until I've used them a while. However, I like it so much that I plan on getting the subscription.

This app has come along way recently and is feature rich. I use it to organise my house and storage unit and love how I can easily move items between storage locations and keel track of all my belongings. A really functional app and a great developer behind it who takes user feedback onboard. Great value for money.
This is how I'd design a pantry app. Customizable, free from bloat, simple, but expandable... Maybe more feedback as I continue to use it, but so far, great.
With all the freezers and panties etc we have this app make it easy to keep track of what I have in hand so I stop wasting money and time buying things we already have!

We decided to make an inventory of everything at home. The first time, I found the most efficient app for the first time. Thanks to the team for their tremendous effort. I already got my lifetime membership.

most everything in my cupboard I scanned the barcode couldn't be found. not going to pay for any app that doesn't even have data on common items. Campbell's cream of mushroom soup "barcode not found" seriously???
Super Buggy - When I delete a category or change an item to a different category, it kicks me out of the app. it's unusable

Great app. Update: No widget, No dark mode, app should have suggestions when I am adding products, for example apples and bananas with images to them, and also things such as soap. There should already be ready made categories instead having to type in it myself.
love this, knowing what I have so I don't over buy.
Very useful and easily applied for a number of purposes!
The barcode scanner function works great. We use it for home freezer management and it works great for small-scale usage.
So far so good, tracking is pretty easy and flexible. I needed an app to track expiration dates and this is doing the trick
Began to use the app, good and simple working interface, a little bit slow when loading soonmuch items , still using it.
Very useful app. Perfect to cataloque and organise everything I want. Easy to manage using barcodes. Thank you!
I love the ability to scan products in with barcodes, organize items, and track expiration dates!
Excellent application. Simple, effective and with all the features I needed to manage my home inventory and shopping list.
This is the easiest one to use for me. I wish it was cuter because I love me a good gimmick. No avatars or scenic backgrounds on this one. But I chose it for it's functionality.

A bit of a learning curve in figuring out how to nest the folders. Now that I'm starting to add my items and I'm realizing how much food I've got, I'm wondering how best to use the app. But I do like to see what's all there.
Without images for a product and a storage, and also QR code for this storage it's useless. Also adding custom fields to everything would be nice.
Very nice app that helps you organize you Pantry and helps you locate where everything is in the house. I also use it for the freezer items.
Does what I need it to do and more. Easy to use. would love ability to scan shopping receipts to add items too
Simple, easy to use, intuitive interface, and a pretty good upc code lookup. I have been using it for about a week and it does the job.
This app is very good and they are very quick to respond and help with any problems. I liked the app so that was an easy decision to pay for the life-time and got rid of ads.
Easy to use. Wish the barcode scanner gave items, but maybe it does outside of Canada. Still love using it
I'm using this app to track food we keep in our basement. The app is super intuitive and tracks everything I want to track!

Very useful. I love the bins and tags. Is it possible to add a feature that allows a single field's data to be copied so that it could then be pasted elsewhere? For example, if I need to input an item's serial number into another app or site, I would love to be able to easily copy only the serial number's information so that I could then paste it elsewhere. This feature would make the app perfect for inventorying product for my job and giving other team members easy access to data. Thanks!

I've been using this app for a few days and I love it! I love that it is a one time fee past the free items and not a subscription. I am currently using this because I'm a materials manager for a week-long youth summer camp. All my leaders can access the inventory which will help us plan better for next year. I am also a professional organizer and I will be recommending this to my clients. A couple of thoughts on improving the app would be to have some customizable fields available.

Great app! You can enter items any way from basic to very detailed with pictures. You can buy additional space for more items if needed. Give yourself peace of mind knowing that you have your home inventoried so you know what you have. plus, it should help if you have to make an insurance claim. I was surprised to find out how much money in household items I have. Before this app I would have severely undervalued my items by a lot of money if I had to make an insurance claim.

This app is really great! I am moving and i'll be living out of boxes for about 6months. This app has been invaluable to keep track of items as I pack and loan them out to family members. I can add locations like "storage", "dads garage", etc. Then I can add the different boxes or furniture pieces in each location, and then add all of the items/groups of items in each container. I can add tags such as "camping" to easily search all the camping equipment I own. Customer service is fantastic! 😀

Very nice looking app for inventory of your items without excess and potentially-confusing "extras" for those you may be sharing access. However, we found the app and data set couldn't be shared between more 2 people. Was surprised by that, and we couldn't get a quick enough response from the developer (no additional staff for support), as we had people in from out of town not able to work with us. We lost several days of work processing items within this app, so that was very disappointing.

Amazing tool for keeping track of your stuff! Some things I wish you could do is be able to list all the rooms you have the same items in (like if I have one case of soda in my garage and one in the basement) and I would love to be able to have a way to show that a pack of something is opened with some of the items taken out (example: a pack of 20 batteries, but 2 have been taken out and used).

I've only started to use it but it seems to work just fine. Allows couple of photos per item and I doubt anyone would go over the max number of items you can enter for the free version. The premium upgrade is reasonable and a one time cost which is good as I would pay a one time fee to upgrade but not a monthly recurring fee.

Used the app today to catalogue my house inventory -- given the increase in wildfires and natural disasters, this has become important for everyone to do! App was very intuitive to use and a great deal with the premium membership compared to some of the other enterprise focused inventory options (i.e. Sortly). Loved the multiple pictures and data fields available. Even made my house clutter look good! A few wishlist ideas - It would be nice to have the app total the value of each container based off the prices already input. And if possible, though I know this would take a lot of data integration, a way to pull in specs based off UPC codes. Overall, everything you need to make a comprehensive house inventory in an easy to use package!

Pretty handy app for keeping track of all those random boxes and items that get used once per year. First time set up takes a while depending on how much of your house you want to organize. But just getting a few items loaded up and testing that the app works is pretty easy.   Looking forward to some additional functionality, like sharing inventory with members of your family, so everyone doesn't have to duplicate efforts.

Far outstrips the other 4 star+ inventory apps I've tried. This is a personal, home inventory app. Not a business inventory tracker. The most important features for me are the: make. and model, and serial numbers data base. Plus location (rooms, outbuildings, and boxes). And, the value of each item; necessary for personal property insurance premiums and claims. And, the data is stored in the cloud, and can be exported to excel data sheet. This app was done right.

10/5 stars Best app for inventorying your stuff there is! We had a shop fire and tried a few other apps, but Housbook is the best and worth every penny on premium. We ended up with 2194 items, and the pdf report looks excellent. It's a huge deal when you can give the insurance company a professional-looking report with prices, models, serials, and locations. I had a minor issue with exporting at first because the report was 944 pages long, but I reached out to Cameron, and he pushed a fix quick

This was very close to what I was looking for. Very simple with a nice visual nested approach. My one complaint is the idea of charging by number of inventory items. That's a completely arbitrary and artificial limit. This always feels like the lazy way to siphon more money without actually adding truly meaningful functionality. For example there are a couple of nice databases that do far more and cost less without artificially limiting the number of records.

5 stars so far! Ive added about a dozen items and really like the simplicity and the layout. I plan to eventually buy extra space whether i need it or not to help support for the app, so long as i don't run into any issues. Purchases are 100% optional it seems so far. 200 free items, then a reasonable price for more. I don't know that I even have 200 items to add for what I'm using it for, but thats definitely enough for all but the most nitty gritty.

Very pleased with this app, purchasing full version. Love how you can layer your entries by adding rooms, items in that room & items that may be housed in another item. My only wish is to be able to sort manually within each "layer". I would prefer to sort rooms/items/containers in the order they are in the house/room rather than scrolling back & forth.

Too expensive/limited - there should be an unlimited option. This seems like it could be a great app, but paying $30 and online getting a total of 400 items seems very expensive. You would think that, for that price, it could be nearly unlimited (perhaps with limited photo storage or something like that). That # might be good for a pantry but there is no way I could use it for more things, especially not books, without it being the most expensive app I've purchased.

I really love this app, the way I can organize things into sub-containers is just perfect. Sadly, the app is missing one key feature for me - seeing quantities at a glance. I need to open a container and see not just which items are in there, but how many of each one (without clicking into the item). Add that feature and it's 5 stars easy! Other feature requests: - Tags on containers - View all tags - Move items between houses - View all items of one brand Overall love it just want more :)

Great app for people with ADHD and issues organizing/remembering where you put things! Premium is useful to have, but isn't necessary -- features aren't overly restricted for free users compared to other apps. I like that there are options to add photos, QR codes, descriptions, etc. if you want, but adding these things aren't required to add rooms/items if you want to keep things simple.

I spend hours and hours and hours and days setting up barcodes, scanning then in. Now when I use a external scanner this app just decides not to recognize the codes UNLESS I use the camera. I spend tons of money to make this work since this app was the affordable option to learn to do barcodes. But this app is broken in so many ways. I can't even search my box names, I just see a blank box in the search results and if I click on it, it just shows me all my items all at once from all rooms :(

I love this app. I am a professional baker and was looking for a way to organize cookie cutters. This allows me to have every cutter organized and filed and I can do a search in the app to find something rather than digging though a bunch of boxes. It saves me so much time! And it is very reasonably priced compared to other similar apps. My only complaint is that if my phone accidentally turns during an item add, the picture and tags get deleted and I have to input them again. Still worth it tho

What I LOVE: easy of setting up rooms, containers, subcontainers, and adding pictures quickly. Love the tags, and quick search function. Would LOVE IT if Google search would consistently find item (as I try to demo to others how great this app is). Would like if I could add more than one picture without having to pay for it, but I get it. Would LOVE IT if the app can show check off boxes so inventory can be checked in and out (ex: making sure all items are back in a campbox post trip).

I have added several items to the HouseBook inventory. It is easy. Mostly though, it is enabling me to catalogue items that would be difficult to prove we owned if an insurance claim was necessary. We are also annotating some items with stories of acquisition to give details to our daughter when she finds herself without us to tell her how we found and bought many of the things with which she grew up.

Great app, does just about everything I need it to. There were a couple things usability wise that I thought weren't features. But Cameron set me straight and explained how to access those features. It was very simple, I just missed them in the settings. I'm using it to catalog all the items in my shop and have the ability to both search by tags, but also to scan a crate and have it tell me what's inside. The sharing feature is also very handy when other people are working in the shop and need access to find where things are.

Only spent a couple of hours with it so far, but other than a lossless crash when I was trying to do too many things at once, I haven't had a single issue with it. Looking at the system log, it wasn't even HouseBook's fault. As far as I can tell I just ran out of memory. It's customizable enough to not leave me frustrated from lack of features, but configurable enough to meet all of my needs. In addition, the dev seems super chill and committed to regular upgrades. Def recommend a test drive!

This app has saved my move. Not my first move so I know things can get lost and since it takes so much to actually unpack with this app I'll know exactly which box to open. Compared to other apps I love this one the most becouse it allows me to create not only rooms but containers and sub containers. So the items from the bookshelf in the livingroom can go in different boxes.... genius!!!

Great start for an inventory type app. Would like to see grouping by room with total costs. I'm hoping to see more robust reporting capacity with different sorting and filters. The interface is very intuitive and quick to add items. And it's easy to go back and edit the items.

Just got it and so far I like it, but it would be nice to have a "Total Value" section, so if you have a box of books or something, you can see exactly how much you spent on all the books in the box. I know there's a section where you can add it in manually, but it would be cool to have it done automatically. Would be cool to see how much you spent on your whole room! Edit: Fair point! That would be a lot more efficient than opening the tabs! (Loving the responsiveness!)

I was looking for app like that for a while. Cameron did a great job with this one! The app has everything I need to keep my house organized. You can organize your house (or two) by the room and different places (containers) within each room. You can take a picture, describe an item, list the brand, model, serial number, quantity, price per item, add a tag, and even scan a QR. Now, I don't have to remember or wonder where anything is. Whenever I need something, I just search it! Great App!

Doesn't get much better than this to organize and find where the heck you put that one thing that you said you will need a some point so you bought it and now you have no clue where it is 😀 ... or in my case, several things. Absolutely 💯 and free to use. I purchased the premium version because it is totally worth it! The developer is also active with the app so it receives updates on occasion. Even supports qr codes so you can organize things outta sight. Please try it out for yourself.

Useful app for organizing home inventory and storage. Still working on entering all my electronics and holiday decor, but hoping it helps me find things in storage / keeps me from buying extras of items I already own. Would love the ability to mass edit tags for multiple items at once (add / remove). Also, inverse filtering by tag (items without a certain tag). Impressed with the progress and responsiveness of the solo dev, Cameron. Thanks!

It doesn't matter how easy or pretty it looks, this is absolutely useless without an excel backup option. This is what you would need to provide for insurance companies, customs if you're packing up to move, or police reports if a break-in happened. If I have to re-enter everything in a spreadsheet, then why use this app?

My review good a character limit, so it's not as detailed as I wanted. TL;DR: Great app. Great dev. This app is pretty darn close to exactly what I was looking for in an inventory app. It's clean, straightforward, well organized, and includes many great features (some of which I didn't even realize I wanted until I began using them, like the links for guests when they come over) I've been using the app for around 5 years now, and Cameron has been great at responding to feedback and requests

Perfect home inventory app. Easy to quickly create rooms then containers & then add items to the containers. I'm using it to keep track of rarely used items after one too many times spending hours looking for something I haven't used in a year. Ideal for people who frequently have house guests or rent out their place via AirBnB. Share the inventory list, even if just a print out of a PDF file, with guests so guests can find things they need without wasting time looking through every drawer.

Organizing craft supplies. I literally have thousands of rubber stamps. This allows me to catalog them, tag them and then search via those tags. By far THE BEST one I have used!! So much so I left another really expensive subscription, at the cost of having to reload everything. WORTH IT!!!

Edit: developer immediately reached out and the problem was resolved within 24 hours. Now that it's working (problem was my account was so old, won't be an issue for new downloads) it's everything I wanted it to be. Loved it before, love it again today. Old review Was working great. Updated it and now it spins "creating account" forever and appears to have deleted everything I had entered.

This is perfect for home inventory management as it has just the features you need without other stuff that ads cost and cluters the UI. It is a clean simple UI with more than enough levels of abstraction to get as detailed as you want. The pricing is fair with a one time payment not a subscription model (rare nowadays) and with 200 items for free most people wont need to upgrade. My only problem is with the website the search bar is only on the home page and not in the top banner

I've been using this app for about a month and it's fantastic! I love that it gives me the freedom to add multiple sub-containers so I can even better categorize things. I'm using it for my needlework patterns and supplies. I have it broken down by the cabinet things are in, the binder or basket, and then further still by designer or manufacturer. It makes avoiding buying duplicates so easy. The developer is also really helpful and, for a one-man operation, provides a good amount of support.

Most useful app like this I found. Tags & additional item info help a lot (like explaining WHERE something is in the closet). It has you add rooms and then containers (and sub containers!!). I just pretend my main containers are rooms. It tells you how many items you have out of the limit. Camera always popping up is annoying (even with the "form" setting selected) when I don't want to add a picture. Another suggestion besides the camera: make the menu open when swiping right (it's easier).
looks like a great app. but premium version, there's still a limit on items. you get 200 free. 39.99 buys you another 300 items.
as advertised. easy to use. would be nice to have more than 200 items, but I'd rather pay the fee than deal with ads so I'm happy with it.
The app looks really good but was after a invertory app that I could also save my receipts that i purchased the item. If that was part of the program, I would use the app.
the app is good but you cant add custom fields. im surprised they missed something like this.

This is an amazing app, I can easily keep track on my inventory, tools, and perishables for my new home. You know that feeling that you know you have something at home, but can't seem to figure out where you kept it because it's been too long since you last used it? This has helped me multiple times once you set up your rooms and containers properly. A definite reccomend!

Housebook has been a lifesaver for me during my recent move. It has made organizing my belongings and packing them into boxes so much easier. With Housebook, I can quickly see which items are in each box and even add photos to make unpacking a breeze. I would highly recommend this app to anyone who is in the process of moving or who just wants to get more organized.

I love this app. It's easy to use, it does what i need and it's esthetically pleasing. I would have paid for it but there were two issues. 1. We are a family of 5, we have way more than 500 items in our home. 2. I would have loved the option to select multiple items from rooms or containers to temporarily move to a "vacation" house. Kids leave things behind and this would be so easy to track.

On 1st appearance this seems like a good option for apps in this category in terms of features and UI, and is closest to what I had in mind when I started looking for a home inventory app. Unfortunately the app keeps crashing when trying to add a new brand or tag, on the product entry page. Fortunately the bugs in this app forced me to look for alternatives, and I found a much better app at a quarter of the price, so all is well!

really lovely experience with this, its good for doing small company inventory too (i know its not designed for that but its really good at doing it). you can use the free version or pay one off payments for upgrades and to remove adds. i wish more developers would be like this, no subscription model would get it 100 stars if i could. really cant recommend it enough

I currently use this app for a specific use, which is to search through my inventory of cables when I need one. As such, I can quickly see if I have the one I need and where it is. Unfortunately, there's a dealbreaker limitation to my use which is that when moving an item that has a quantity over 1 to another container, you can't decide to apply the action to a part of that quantity. I could always copy/duplicate the item but it isn't a logical way to do.

Very happy with the app so far. The container system is logical and the items have all the fields that I would use. Pricing is reasonable. My only qualm is that I don't see a way to take a look at my tags and filter by them. I can only find them using search but that requires me to remember the tag name and the search also finds other items they have the tag name in their other fields. A simple tag menu for managing and filtering tags would solve mu problems and would make it a 5 star app.

This has almost everything you'd want to organize your home. For people who always forget where things are, this is the perfect app for you. Just a suggestion to be added to the app is a stock number system. For example, you have 10 garbage bags left, the app can then notify you when your stocks are low (which you can set how much). Thanks for this app! Amazing developer! Pricing is also justifiable and worth it. Especially that it works with Google.

I really appreciate this app. It is easy to use. I also like that I can organise and structure things as I want, eg there is not a given structure you have to use. Great app. A key point for me to use this app was also the ability to backup and to be able to export the inventory into a file. For future extension/updates I would love to see that values that are entered for items in a container are automatically summarised at container level and maybe even summarised at room level.

Awesome app so far! It's the only app I have found that can create containers and subcontainers. It's surprising how such a simple concept is missed by other app developers. One suggestion: would it be possible to include functionality to select multiple items. It will make it easier to move more than 1 item instead of having to click move multiple times. It would make the app even more awesome!

Fantastic app! I use it to organize my BTS collection of Albums, merch, photocards, dvds etc. The fields are exactly what i was looking for, i have bins/boxes of BTS items that i generally rotate throughout the year for my display shelves, the way the app is structured helps me find items easier! I've looked at other apps specifically for kpop collecting but never worked for me. I purchased the upgrade for a second house to use for my BT21 collection :)

Nice app, help me to keep my house clean. However there is still some disadvantage: 1. The email which invite housemate can't be received every time. 2. The color of the photos become very strange, maybe the compress algorithm should be replaced. 3. There is no self-host license, may cause data leakage.

This app is very helpful to our laboratory. The app is easy to use and navigate. Important details as well as the barcode of our items can be recorded. This has made our lives easier in the lab easier. Researchers come and go but the reagents remain in the lab. Its very difficult to find items when you don't know what they look like since it was purchased years ago. The visual help of images made finding things easier as well as the smart organization this app provides
Intuitive, well designed, easy to understand, no malfunctions.

The app functionality is top notch, its very simple to use and navigate. For that it deserves recognition, however the item count limit system is predatory and unfair. Knowing ill never be able to manage my entire household inventory without shelling out potentially unknown amounts for a few hundred more items at a time ruins the apps use entirely for me. I dont know the entire item count of my house, im not gonna suffer micro transactions each time I hit a new limit.

I highly recommend this software!! This app is awesome! I have been looking for an inventory app for so long that I could use to keep up with all the fabric, and supplies that I have in my sewing room. I wanted an app that I could have a picture, description, and detailed locations for each item. This one does all of that and so much more! I can even back it up to the cloud! It is valuable in all the rooms & storage buildings or rooms you have. And Customer service is top-notch! Answered in 1day

The only inventory app that can start with taking a photo (wow), no extra crop step (is good). Fast interface, but not as comfortable as should be. No way to add receipts (can only use extra photo slots). No subscription (good) but (hefty) one time payment for +300 items, etc. Very limited fields available, currency only global, not changeable per item. Knockout criticism: no offline export for backup purposes. Also, which cloud does this go into? Not changeable. In this incomplete state, nope.

A great, easy to use app! I reviewed a number of home inventory apps. I selected and started using Housebook. The app focuses on 1) what objects I want to add (represented by easy insert photos, standard fields and category tags); and 2) where these objects are located using nested containers (such as rooms, cabinets, shelves, etc). The app can create an Excel/Google Sheets "csv" list of all objects and their field values. Also, app modification requests are responded too in a timely manner!

Actions on Google is sunsetting, but there's App Actions replacing them...so why don't voice commands work? other than that this app works well...for the most part... I would definitely pay for premium if it included unlimited items AND if the integration with Google assistant worked for logging (Like "hey Google, my umbrella is in the closet" or "tell housebook my umbrella is in the closet" or something of the sort), as well as recalling ("hey Google where is my umbrella?")

Straightforward, works with multiple authors using cloud storage. Some quirks for logging in, and some features to be desired but still a great app, better than others I've tried. Used it with a basic description and pictures per item. The pictures are down sampled to reduce size, but still have high enough detail . Backup to pdf gives a basic reference for the pictures and backup to csv provided all the textual data. Generous pricing model, with 200 free items is plenty for simple projects.

In the process of getting ready to move to a new place. Using the app to catalog possessions and what is staying at the old house and what's coming with. The only problem I have is I'm unable to move items from 1 house to the next in the app. Edit: Thank you for the fast reply. I was testing out the app with a few items, so for now just going to add the items I'm taking with me and sort them after I move. The items that I'm not taking are just going to be added to my current house.

Like the simple UI elements for adding and updating inventory data. One observation: does it support non-English languages(like Bengali (বাংলা))? Because I used Bengali for some item names and then when exported data to pdf, found that those Bengali names are not visible in document. Otherwise so far I am loving it. Kudos Cameron! Edit: The font issue got fixed in latest update. Thanks to Dev for quick response 😊

I was looking for something that could help me locate everything that I own in one place. This app offers that and much more. I can now locate any item but also check on relevant details like warranty and serial number for items as and when needed. This is a huge time saver!

This is the best inventory/storage app I have used. You can set your home up by room, then containers or just add items directly to room. Plenty of customized fields for recording item information and can even add a picture of items, containers, rooms and so much more. Give the free version a try and I bet you will want to upgrade to full version. The app creator Cameron Henige is super easy to reach and he contacts you back faster than most developers I've dealt with. A keeper app for sure.

App is very good but premium is WAAAAAAAY overpriced. £26.49 for only a few extra features is a ridiculous price. Should be £4.99. Really like being able to create containers in rooms and add items to that container. A search for an item then shows where is is (which is my use case). The one thing that is really needed is the ability to categorise (and sub categorise) items. So for example DIY->Power tools. Or Household->Christmas decorations. Can use tags but category drop down would be easier

I don't care if this ends up being the worst app ever, the pricing is fair. Looks promising, & I'm about to try it. Previous service tried to change my paid for app, to $20 every month which is insane for a single user with a few things. That would be $10k for life. We can't pay more to track our things than they are worth. AFTER USING: Camera is automatically started when adding every single item and just runs. Does NOT respect the setting choice to just start at list. Totally kills battery

Worked great till I was hours into my organizing, then it tells you to pay for extra storage. I had to pay for the extra storage then it wipes out my list of items while editing and cleaning things up. Not happy. It started working again and i didnt loose anything. 4 stars cause of the initial incident. The only quirk i have is when searching for something by name i click on an item and if i clicked on the wrong one i have to start over by typing what im searching for instead of just going back

Great app for landlords and tenants. Easy to use and works without issues so far. Love the option to add containers. They are like folders to keep things organized. 200 free items and one time prices to add more items is also way more preferable then the monthly suscribtion costs of some competitors.

This is the best house organization app I have tried. I struggle with organization. If I can't see something I don't know where it is. This app will be a life changer. Linking it to Google assistant is pure genius. Thank you so much I have just had a thought. I don't know whether I haven't found it yet or maybe it's a premium offer. I would like to be able to have lockable containers. I have kids & I would like to be able to share the app without them finding Christmas presents, and 18+ items!

Easy to use. Cloud sync. I love Google assistant integrated feature the most. But I hesitate to pay for premium because I have 3 house, premium add only 1 house that not enough for me. And I'm Gunpla and figures collecter 300 items must not enough for sure.Pls consider to increase this starter package limit. Great app by the way, thanks for your work 😀

This App is really what i've been looking for! I've wanted to organize my items at home with boxes using barcodes and came across this App. It's really well made and easy to use. One feature suggestion i have is to add support for data-matrix codes, as i'd like to use them insted of qr-codes ;)

Not a bot, but a super happy customer! Literally the best app around. Ive tried several for about 2 hours, and then finally found this one. Simple, clean, works well, can be fully used even without the premium (the biggest issue with other apps - useless unless you pay), altho getting premium has its perks! Its so well done that even my mom could easily use it to its full extent. Has literally everything you'll need!

I'm using this for my whole house, and have even put numbered qr codes on every container. A few suggestions: When moving an item or container, the ability to search for destination via QR code scan. Please make the QR code value searchable when typing manually. Please add the ability to select and edit multiple items/containers at once (especially tags and location) Hope to keep using this for years to come :)

It's so limited it's worthless! No way to create the catagories of your contents. Only allows you to have 100 items on the free version. If more than 100 items then you have to pay a whopping $30!!! I've never paid even $5 for any app! But even if you buy the premium version, then it only allows you to have a total of 300 items in all homes and locations!!! I must have thousands of items just my main home! What a waste of time this was!!! What a greedy, stingy developer!!!

Easy to use, clean interface, and lots of great features that make finding items in my home a breeze. I struggle with short term memory, so the ability to quickly locate items and have tags and photos is making such a huge difference in my life. Very friendly app developer as well!

I'm using HouseBook to inventory our belongings before we move (we're a military family so we move every few years). Moves can go well or terribly so figured this time around, I'd have record of our items to make reconciliation with the moving company easier. I also used it as we took down our holiday decorations to help us remember what we have to use in our next house. App designer Cameron was very prompt in answering a question & was gracious enough to go above & beyond! Highly recommended.

I tried out a lot of inventory apps and settled on this one since it had the best UI and had most of the features I desired. I wanted to start using one because I have terrible memory issues due to ADHD. Only thing that I would add for usability is the ability to include any of the other fields besides title in the search function. Thanks for the great app!

The app is so user-friendly, and it makes our lives so much easier to stay organized, especially moving frequently with the military! The free version works great, but if you need extra item slots etc, the premium version is also WELL worth it.

New install crashes. I created a room, container, and added an item to the container. However when I click on the item the app crashes. Update: Turns out if you add an item via their website, the app cannot open that item without crashing. Adding an item from the app though works as expected.

So far so good. This app is exactly what I needed. I had only one minor problem where I had 10 containers in a "furniture" and I tried to organise them by number however, the app was not accepting number 10? Because it moved my 10 container between number 2 and 3. So I named it "9b" in order to be moved in the right place. Moreover, I didn't find a way to move organise them in order manually.

Good idea, but not totally functional... I'm not able to edit an item with my cell, it can only be done on a computer, which is not practical, when doing inventory far from a computer... Otherwise, great app. Thanks for correcting that bug if possible!

Great app. Exactly what I was looking for. It'd be good to be able to navigate rooms with smaller picture size. The current item picture size is too big to allow for comfortable navigation of the inventory. Maybe add different methods to view?

This really is a great app. Great for me as I have memory issues.! It's fairly straightforward to set up. You can link it to Google Nest. I like the search facility so it's now only a few taps to find out where I stored something instead of rummaging around looking for items! Thank you. :-)

I use this app to keep track of where I put things. You know, the stuff that you put somewhere safe and then a year later can not find it. I take a picture, and right under the photo I can write the location. I have used it for several items, and I think it will work well for my needs.

It's a great concept, a bit time consuming and I wish they would shrink the photos we take because they seem to slow the app down. I decided to get this one because apparently you can ask your assistant where things are located and she will answer you using the info from this app. I haven't tried that bit yet.

Polished looks, although I stopped using it: - no offline functionality, so it doesn't work in the basement - prices are hidden until you register, they are not even listed on the webpage. For others: premium 30€, +100 items 5EUR, +200 items 9EUR, +500 items 15EUR. So don't bother tracking small items :/

Advertised as a home inventory organizer. Truth is, you are limited to just 100 items! Lets do the math... Oh, not enough is correct. And, heres the catch, the app requires an email. Sounds like a recipe for im not gonna try it with a dash of spyware!

I like this app. It seems to be intuitive & well-organized. However, as someone trying to take an inventory of an entire house full of stuff... I'd appreciate a few additional features, like duplicating containers (with or without their contents), importing lists from another file format (like csv or xml or txt), templates for rooms (including typical containers & their contents), etc. So far, there's a lot of typing & clicking around just to get one room set up, g I'd like to see that reduced.

Amazing developer: I emailed him asking how come I can't search based on data entered in the description field. He implemented and released this feature in less than 2 days. The app does everything I was looking for. I tested 4 or 5 other apps, but this one convinced me, especially now with the search description feature :-). I like the ability to nest items, add images to items, move items easily, etc... The only other feature I would like to see is the ability to create custom fields.

I downloaded a few different apps before finding this one, and this one did exactly what I needed it to do. I work at an Art Gallery so this is a fantastic app to be able to document stock and move around works quickly and efficiently. Also being able to know for insurance reasons how much your stock is worth is incredibly helpful with too. Designer was prompt to address any concerns via email. Thanks!

Web UI is extremely sluggish. Nearly unusable once you have a lot of items. Breadcrumb navigation aren't clickable links to speed navigation. Bug when you take a photo, hit the checkmark to add it to the item, rotate your phone, photo disappears.

Great app and outstanding support by the developer. I finally feel organized in my garage after wasting countless hours looking for things that I know I have somewhere. With this app I quickly and easily catalog my stuff and toss it in a box and number it. Super easy to find everything now. I had one minor issue (my own fault) and Cameron addressed it in a timely fashion. Thanks!!!

I'm getting ready to move and I have executive functioning problems and this app has been so helpful in getting me organized. I can easily mark items that I need to pack and where they should go at the new place. I like that it seems like there is an unlimited number of sub-containers and it is nice being able to add pictures.

Totally functional without premium. I use the photo feature to add screenshots of receipts or photos of the serial number which makes completing inventory easier. I love that you can export it to a spreadsheet too.

Great tool for categorizing items from my in-laws estate. I document each item in the boxes. I have had to reach out to Cameron once with an issue and it was resolved quickly. Only suggestion would be to be able to make a pdf of each room individually instead of the whole house. The file is so large and I don't always need a pdf of the entire house, sometimes just need one room.

This is probably one of the best home inventory apps out there. Allows you to organize by room by bin and by item. Even upload photos and use QR codes to further organize. This is an app that will probably stay in my phone for a very long time.

Super easy, extremely functional, but needs to be cloud based. The layout is easy on the eye & easy to understand, & I haven't run into any bugs. It's perfect if you are the head of your house's domestic affairs, or you work really closely with your partner (ie sharing phones w/o suspicion), but if you need privacy or to compartmentalize tech yet share work (companies/organizations), it's not cloudbased. It saves to Google drive, but drive isn't good for team-sharing. Desktop version needs work

easy to use and very effective. however I cant add any house mates. I've tried the email link, which is the only way to add people, and no one has recieved it. I even tried my own email and never recieved it. kinda pointless for me to use if I cant share it with anyone to collaborate with.

The app is beautifully and thoughtfully made! Perfect for cataloging and keeping track of my most important possessions. Love that it seamlessly backs up to my Google Drive for peace of mind. Out of all the inventory apps I've tried, it's by far the most user-friendly and customizable to your needs.
