playStoreApps.name: Truelancer: Freelance Work App
playStoreApps.developer: Trulancer: Job Search App, Freelance Jobs

playStoreReviews.text: As of this review, the app is definitely incomplete, perhaps Alpha or Beta stage, but it's clear to me that the developer is actively taking in feedback about new features. I see this app as an opportunity to help someone develop the right freelancer app from the ground up. It's only $2.49/mo right now and I'm happy to pay that for what this app currently delivers on. I'm a freelance AV Technician and I use the app to log the Gigs (projects) my Labor Brokers (clients) offer, and when I get paid.
Good app
Surprisingly useful!
Good

Please fix system app bugs for new people, and please have a way to contact IT support when the app decides to glitch out and you cannot fix it on your end. And AI chatbots can only do so much. Like, I should be able to apply to jobs without a promo code, and then I can't get the promo code (because I don't have one), I could get one with a membership plan, but even that is unavailable in the app, so I have to somehow find a way to FORCIBLY pay for a membership I DON'T WANT in order start work!

I personally prefer the UI that is available before you create an account, allowing you to select specific jobs you can look into applying for (Logo design, proofreading). Another thing that I think would be nice is being able to reach out to the potential client instead of needing to "Buy Connect", or allow new users to have a certain amount to start with to get them used to the process and having the money to afford it.

great concept, horrible execution. half of the app just doesn't work at all a lot of links don't work and it's impossible to get verified. as an employer it's almost impossible to see somebody's full portfolio without paying for a consultation and as an employee, way to much competition for them and not enough employers like myself. app needs serious work

Upwork used to be fine years ago! now it sucks!!! probably new greedy owners with poor management or smth, not sure. I just paid to get some connects (it's been a while since I used it) and it asked me to get verified while I was on my pc, then for verification asked to scan the qr code and now after scannin it does absolutely nothing! so I'm stuck unverified and wasted $ for not being able to bid!!!! seems like it's time to completely forget about this platform and switch to others!

So you do not need to be verified to bid on jobs but if you chose the OPTIONAL route of getting verified, they freeze your account until verification is complete. Its been days with no communication or status update. If being verified is not required in the first place then why does trying to get verified suspend your ability to bid on jobs that you are able to do without getting verified.

Never keeps me logged in, can't apply for jobs with the allotted amount. They say they allow for applying. even did the paid prescription and it still was not beneficial.And it never leaves me logged in.So I don't get notifications. It's very hard to navigate connecting with others and finding opportunities even with the paid subscription.

Upwork has turned into a money-making machine for beginners. You create a profile, upload your resume, spend time building everything professionally — and then realize you can barely apply for jobs unless you constantly buy “Connects.” 100 Connects cost around $15, and many jobs require multiple Connects just to apply. There is absolutely no guarantee you will get hired, or even get viewed. So beginners end up trapped in an endless cycle of paying more and more just for the chance to compete.

No access to live support for new freelancers. I'm having an issue uploading a simple photo to my profile, I have exhausted all the troubleshooting suggestions from the AI agent. After that, there's no further means to escalate to a live person for review. All support routes funnel through the AI agent which seems to kick you out the door if you can't figure out the issue.

the app is good in theory, but there is a lot of competition for the few jobs that are available. and they take a pretty good chunk of your hard work. there are better platforms. also, when you complete a job it takes about 2 weeks to actually get paid. so if you need money now, this isn't the place. the upgrade fee is definitely not worth it. in addition every time I uploaded a profile photo that was me and unaltered I got aeasage that my profile was at risk because of my profile pic. don't ree

so I downloaded and started looking into jobs. found a few promising ones. bought the membership, paid the credits. now my profile is under manual review and even though I've bought the subscription I'm still unable to bid on jobs. so far a complete waste of time and money. I'll update when/if the situation is corrected.

Dark pattern ads. It seems they've introduced Fullscreen ads for their apps, which is displayed once a day (?). There's no option to disallow it, and the worse thing about it is, there's a small dot at the top left of the close button, so when you can't close it easily. Otherwise the app is fine in most cases. I'll update my review if the ad is stopped or there's an option to stop the ad. This is really unexpected from Upwork, doing something so cheap.

Be very careful to make sure you enter a contract. Do not chat with the clients on "telegram" or outside of the Upwork app. Do not do any work until a contract is initiated. Like any other platform it's prone to having inconsistent dealings, so protect yourself. Other than that if you are diligent it works. The UI is a bit cluttered and can be hard to find things but it is still acceptable. For their efforts to mitigate issues, I give some props.

Site is good but the app needs serious help... 1) I cannot decline invitations from my phone. 2) Messages do not update or refresh automatically. 3) Notifications are either late or don't come in at all. 4) Messages take forever to load if they load at all. For example, if I see a notification (even when it's late), I MUST click the notification to view the message. If I attempt to open the app to view all messages first, it won't load. 5) Typing is a chore thanks to their selection method.

Overall it works well except for a few things that I've noticed. The most annoying is that message notifications are really delayed. Somethimes is only 10min other times it's more. The other really annoying thing is when you get a notification that you have been invited to a job, go to notifications and, click respond, you are taken out of the app to your browser.

The concept is sound, The ability to post skills and search for jobs people need completed is a nice option to have. I have not been on long enough to find any potential contracts, but it seems like a solid app. The one issue that loses a few points for you is not so much the spending of connects to bid on jobs, (though not a fan) it is that It was somewhat hidden before the sign up process was complete. There is a chance I just did not see it. **would be great if edits were clearly marked. 👍

They require a verified phone number before you can get paid. This shouldn't be a problem, but my phone number came up as being registered to another user. This should have been an easy fix, but tech "support" kept insisting that there was nothing they could do about it. They said I should use another number, like I have extra phone numbers laying around! I ended up closing my account out of frustration from dealing with these imbeciles.

Terrible business practice. You buy and spend "connects" to bid on contracts, but all of this is done prior to identification verification (which is automated). You can only do the verification after you're offered your first contract. If there's an error with your verification, you get suspended immediately, lose the contract, and lose all of the money and time you already invested. They have no real support, just ai. Not a good experience.

I've had mixed feelings about my experience with Upwork. It has been great for finding freelance gigs and the app itself is easy enough to use. However, I feel like they need to be more transparent about their payment processes. I've gone from waiting two weeks to get my first paycheck. Now today, when I am supposed to get paid. I learn about a ten day security hold. Meaning you will actually have to wait 3+ weeks to get paid. Read up on all their payment processes before you take a job!!

This platform has completely fallen apart. Maddening that's 2023 and links in job alert emails don't open the mobile app. Also, a vast majority of the jobs offer egregious pay. Worst of all, I just spend the past month bidding on gigs that prospects couldn't see because my profile was set to private, which can't be easily turned back on unless you go through the support area of the site. Basically, I just wasted a month applying for gigs that there's virtually no way I could land.

I love this app, it's easy to navigate and I can do almost everything through it. But there are 2 big flaws. 1, to check on earnings it takes me to my browser where I have to log in again. I don't like that. Second, which is extremely annoying, you are required to have the client app and freelancer app. But it only keeps one account logged in on both! So I have to log out of my client account on the freelancer app and log into my freelancer account! And vice versa! Why 2 apps then???

Cancelled account & I'm still being charged. Signed up for one month then cancelled membership. However they keep charging me $15 a month for the past 4 months. Multiple requests submitted through their chat bot. They tell me to sign into my account for help, but their website says my email isn't tied to an account in their system. I can't receive help from a real person. I'm so frustrated.

Can't even truly get started. I got as far as trying to enter my education details. The instructions say to use the "drop down list," but there is no drop down list, even after I type some text into the available field. I presume that I would have to navigate to their website, log in, and try to complete my profile through there. However, the lack of implementation of a feature that they clearly planned for, speaks ill of the competency of this company. Unfortunate.

Every time I go into the Upwork app, it prompts me to log in. The app must have a bug because then it doesn't allow me to click into the space to input my login. The search tool also doesn't work well because you can't search anything except for their suggested topics. I think they would have a lot more users if the experience was more user-friendly.

Update: as of the last 2 updates, the app doesn't work at all. It opens to a full-page screen that demands I download the client app, then it crashes. There's no option to close or ignore the demand. This app is literally useless now. This version of the app is just a more sluggish version of the website. Prefer the old app for quickly checking and responding to messages.

I wish I could even get started. I am having a problem uploading my profile picture. At first, I didn't pay attention to neither the sizing of the photo,nor the pixels min and max. Then I finally found a photo with the correct dimensions and it's a JPG and it is still not uploading. It has been 45 mins and I am super frustrated. I'm ready, but something as tedious as a photo upload is holding me back. SMH.

They make using PayPal to buy connects super difficult, I am assuming, so that you'll give them your card info directly. Be very careful of companies with this behavior. If you are on the basic plan, they seem to make it as difficult as possible to get connects in order to apply to jobs. It's very irritating. The app often crashes, and I have to close it and reopen several times. It's becoming inconvenient. Fiverr works much better and more friendly to freelancers.

UW is it terrible? I didn't like having to pay for credits for bids, but the amount you pay and the amount of credits they give isn't bad. The usability is really good, especially because I am blind. And I didn't have any issues with using the mobile app or the desktop version. Setting up your profile, uploading your portfolio, and communicating with your hire client is simple. I would say, just be patient when it comes to landing your first gig. It took me a month to get my first contract.

This website takes an insane amount of money from its freelancers - twenty percent at least, and for doing little to nothing. Customer service is unimpressive and everything takes 3x longer than it should, especially getting paid. You wait nearly two weeks after the contract is completed, which is just ridiculous. I've been a top-rated member for years, but now they want to charge for Connects. It's all BS and I'm thankful for the off-site clients I've found over the years. I miss Elance.

I don't normally write reviews, but I figured out the glitch everyone was having, so I thought I'd share. This update shouldn't be an update at all, but a new download. I noticed two different icons on my phone. The one I had been using kept requiring me to reload it and defaulted to the old version if I opted not to, but the new icon found way in the back of my phone works perfectly. So, if you're having this problem, uninstall the old app and use the new.

Messages are impossible to use on Android. Text disappears and reappears at random, the cursor cant be scrubbed through the message without deleting or rearranging text, if there is any sort of typo, good luck, the "edit" feature is nonexistent and more often than not just jumbles and destroys the entire message. I have had to resort to typing out any replies to messages on a note or blank e-mail and then copy/pasting it into the message. Uwork is shotty in general but the app is pitiful.

Can't hide or archive messages. Annoying navigation and menus. Constantly logs you out. Notifications aren't reliable. Fees are extremely high. Not enough "connects", forcing you to pay them for the luxury of connecting with customers you could talk to yourself. Did I mention they changed it so that you have to "bid" and fight amongst your fellow freelancers, Vs everyone having a fair chance at applying like they used to do it? This "bidding" makes you purchase more connects. Not happy. UpFees.

The app worked fine for me. Upwork is the problem. They have the worse customer support I've ever experienced. Taking upwards of 3+ days just to respond. They suspend your account for no reasons. Give you a way to get the account back, and then still suspend it even after you go through all the steps to get the account back. And all without giving an actual reason for anything.

Send on the phone app is extremely difficult to make work. In fact, I have given up on it and just go to my computer. Plus, the phone app keeps the alert notification on for long periods of time. The one on now has been there a day and a half. There is also trouble connecting. The establishing connection label is frequent and long lasting.

So far, not good. You have to buy the units called "connect in order to apply " so after the 3 first application you are done. I applied for 3 different jobs and all 3 of them seem to be scams. They all say something about contacting them on "telegram" and includes a link that takes outside the app and its not supported by it. I would keep trying but im afraid I've run out of "connects" and im not about to spend money in something that looks so skeptic.

The app is ok, but the Upwork platform is not a good place to find work. The scammers are plentiful, and the hiring clients are constantly in violation of the TOS. Most of the job postings I see fall into those categories. And most of the clients want to pay way below minimum wage. My experience has been I've had to sort through tons of postings (that should not be allowed to begin with) just to find some that are allowed. Then I have to apply for 50 or so just to get one gig. No thanks.

Most of the clients.....if they are indeed real, suck up the connects and are unresponsive. So it's a waste of time most of the time. The app has definitely changed since I last used it a couple years ago. It also has issues like allowing you to upload or change your profile pic which is important because that is the first impression to the client and if you are going in a different direction with your career, you want your profile pic to reflect that.

It advertises itself as a free remote vote, work job, search app, and as you go through, like a million steps, before it finally says in order to apply for any position you have to buy something and every time I click on it, it just consistently loads instead of opening the payment thing, so I can't even see how much it is. they already take out a service fee for every hour worked of around a buck fifty why do they need you to pay more to use the app

I have been a person who hires which can be very good, with a few if and exceptions here and there. However as an employee it seems to be the Suck. You need points to apply for jobs which the app encorages you to pay ontop of they're salary which seems to grow every year as your recommended rate lowers. Its very competitive, and when your lucky to get in, your in.

It's a very slow app. The company should look at finding ways to make it quicker or perhaps try to come up with a 'lite' version, if too many features in the app working simultaneously slow it down. That being said, it has all I need for my regular Upwork routine, which is awesome!

I have downloaded the app twice. The first time, it gave me an error message and would not allow me to move forward, even when I closed the app completely and then tried to open it again. I uninstalled the app, went to the website to ensure I had the correct log-in info, and then downloaded the app again. It did the same exact thing! Same error message! I'll be deleting it again. There's no need to let it hold storage space on my phone if it isn't going to work. I got Error 403...

It seemed like it could have potential at first, but there is no way to search by location. searching for jobs only results in hundreds of postings from other countries or hundreds of miles away for lowballing clients. Even when using the U.S. only filter, 90% of the listings either show the OP located in China/India, or the OP doesn't even bother providing a location and I'm expected to PAY to apply to jobs that may be thousands of miles away?! Nope!

I like the ease of use and safety features. However, unless you possess a remote skill, it is difficult to find consistent side work. I have 18 years of experience working in clinical laboratories with medical devices, but there isn't much work for that remotely. Most healthcare jobs are for RNs, MDs, PhD, or regulatory. After a couple of months, the only jobs I have gotten hired for are website user testing and product reviews.

I started on Upwork almost a decade ago & back then it was a great app for freelancers. You could always buy connects but also received some every month, most jobs only required a few to apply. The most I ever used for a single application was 5. Now you have to pay and jobs are upwards of 20 connects to AND you no longer get them back. Not to mention the sheer amount of scams and leeches now running rampant on the app. Just another classic example of corporate greed. Don't waste your time

Aside from notifications, this app has become absolutely useless. Logs me out every time I open it, so I need to log in again. Once I log in, will have to refresh a message page in order to see messages from the previous month. Will not allow me to see any messages posted more recently. Not only does it not provide any help, it actively wastes your time

Good, but could be faster. It's convenient to be able to browse job posts and see messages from my phone, but it boots up pretty slowly. The current version of the app is significantly slower than the previous version. (My phone is a bit older, though, so that could be causing it.) For anyone getting stuck on the "download client app" screen, uninstalling and then reinstalling the app fixed that for me.

This app is very sluggish. On Wi-Fi or on 4G data, they're both just as slow. The messaging is really bad. It can take 1-2 minutes just to load the messages. Sometimes it won't load any recent messages, just ones from days ago. Everything else seems to work fine though. Just use the desktop version if your computer is available. Don't bother with the app until it's fixed.

This review is specifically for the app. I have no complaints about using UpWork via browser on a PC. The app is terrible. The buttons aren't buttons; nothing happens when you push them. It makes opening files and links (especially zoom meeting links) provided by clients almost impossible to use on my tablet. And just recently I've stopped receiving push notifications from the app so I never know when I get a message from a client anymore. This feature at least used to work.

The user interface on the app is clunky. The back button almost always takes you out of the app. Something occasionally causes the search results to scroll all the way to the bottom. My account was banned from upwork because I browse jobs but haven't received any recent work on the site. Seems legit. In general, if you're a freelancer looking for the occasional side gig, you won't find it here. Maintaining a good standing where you get promoted with upwork is nearly impossible.

Freezes often. When scrolling through jobs, if I click on a job posting, the app will freeze for several seconds upon trying to return to the full list of current openings. It also needs to be opened and closed 2x to fully update my messages, even when I have notifications for them all. It's also 50/50 whether or not I receive notifications, which has cost me an assignment more than once. Great site and company, but this app desperately needs work, it's been like this for a while now.

Most of the positions I have seen so far are alot less lucrative than led to believe when signing up. I thought I was really underbidding putting my rates between $40-50/hr. I have yet to even see any positions above $25/hr before platform fees. I can make low income like that anywhere. The majority of the positions are product testing. Not worth the cost of connects and the platform subscription with what little is being paid considering the time it takes to fulfill the purchase.

It's a lagging platform, not a product you want to use. I spent so much time as a freelancer trying to find clients here - but nothing worked. I tried to book a consultation with a talent - but it was automatically canceled a few minutes after the booking was created. But no one was going to revert my payment. I could even write to support because the chatbot on the platform wouldn't let me do it. I don't recommend anyone use it, and I hope some new platform will come up to complete the job.

I would've gave this app a 5 start review, but after an email I received recently, I'm done using it COMPLETELY. I thoroughly enjoyed the app for the meanwhile, despite a few ailments (the slow loading speed of the messages and filtered pages, the small variation of jobs pertaining to a specific art category, and the app closing during proposals). But what changed my mind is when they decided to start charging freelancers money for finding jobs to earn, guess what, money. So no thanks, I'm out.

This app is really slow and the message system, which is crucial for me as a freelance to contact my clients and contractors, is messed up. The message system always take a lot of time to properly load and sometimes it doesn't even load. I almost lost some jobs because of that. Please, fix this app asap. We need it to run better in order to keep using your platform.

The web version is a little bit better and I like all of the features in messages, like the able to save attachments from a conversation, and to keep private notes about a customer. That is great. But on both platforms it's easy to get scammed and way too hard to report a scam. And it's nearly impossible to talk to a human. You have to send one message then wait two days, and hope they answered you, otherwise you gotta wait and wait.

It's not possible to purchase connects without a "promo code," and you can't get a promo code. You also can't just upgrade to a paid account. Maybe there's some requirement to have more resume items filled out, or you have to wait a certain amount of time to be sent the code, but I can't find any clues-- and why make someone wait when they're willing to pay you money??

Overall, it's a good app. But a few things could be improved: 1. The chat thread gets glitchy when there's a lot of text. The cursor drops down to the bottom of what I'm typing, which creates errors that I'm constantly correcting. 2. Notifications should go away once the app has been checked. Unlike Gmail, for example, the chat notifications remain at the top of my phone even after I've read the messages. This creates extra swipes to clear notifications. 3. "Available Now" is not reliable.

I don't recommend Upwork to professional architects or engineers. My account was blocked for allegedly offering to receive payments outside of the platform. In reality, I was only able to input my hourly rate and couldn't show the client the total fee. His project was valued around $30,000, and I simply informed him that the complexity of the job might make Upwork an unsuitable platform. As a result, my account was blocked, making Upwork unreliable for handling large, complex projects.

Okay, so I've never written an app review before, but this app is so frustrating and useless that I feel compelled to say something. On a platform where getting back to clients quickly is critical for getting and maintaining work, it is absolutely unacceptable for an app to send notifications hours later, or not at all. And then when I go to check my messages, not all of the recent ones show up. This may have cost me jobs on some occasions, and it should've been fixed a long time ago.

great for a beginner freelancer like myself. it has been easy and I hadn't had to put in too much of my own money to get started, just enough to get some connects to apply for the jobs. I started out with smaller jobs that paid less until I got a hang of it and to build up my profile ratings, after purchasing some connects I got hired pretty quick and then just keep your account active or it'll be deleted. There's a lot of helpful material, just stay in the guidelines!!

I'm rating because the app prompted me to... So here I am! Overall the bid system has been pretty bad for me, with it taking way too many bids to even show up on top bids, and even when I bid like $2-$3 worth on a bid, I don't even get interviews or responses. I've noticed a decrease in my chances of winning contracts despite having more skills than before.

Because the Upwork app didn't notify me, I have lost two prospects for not responding on time. Getting clients to respond to your proposal has been hard and on two separate occasions where they did, Upwork sent no notification. The first time I knew later through my email the second time, nothing. I checked Upwork app just to see I had been sent a message two days ago. This is not fair!!!!!! The app always has one issue with Android I have checked all the notification settings. It is on!

I haven't even been able to finish setting up my profile. Every time I try to upload my picture I get a white screen, or the picture will show up but error when I try to attach. It wad also extremely hard to set my profile up. I couldn't even add "Bachelors" in my education section. Seeming like this might be a waste of time.

Constantly logs me out, takes forever to update chat streams, cancels uploads constantly, and I can't easily reach my timesheets compared to how easy it is on a computer. As a side note: the website on the computer takes forever to load (minutes) and the time tracker app crashes my computer constantly now, and it didn't use to do that before the last update.

Core systems and features are buggy. Cannot buy connects from within app when bidding on work and the chat system randomly sends a text in the middle of typing or enters multiple timesadding new lines. I have to apologize ahead of time to every client I talk to because half a sentence can be very awkward when you're quickly trying to type out the second half. Very difficult to work with.

I love Upwork as a company and have been pleased with the ease of creating client connections, making contracts, tracking hours, etc. The only loss of the star is due to my preference for the desktop version over the mobile. It still works fine, but I think the layout and features are underperforming compared to on a computer.

Twice now, the app has tried to bill me without notifying me. Just randomly tries to draft my account. When it doesn't go through, I get an email saying that my account will be suspended by a particular date and then it gets suspended several days prior to that date. I pay the bill and my account still has a financial hold... I'll not be using Upwork again.

App was 5 stars. Taken down to 3 due to a few recent glitches. The badge for unread messages shows properly on the main messages screen, but the latest messages don't show when a particular conversation is selected (despite clearing cache & data, etc.). All feeds except US only show only 1 or 2 jobs when there are definitely more listed. Declining invites often results in an error & you must refresh to make sure reply was processed. Sometimes push notifications are delayed or don't work.

Can't log in. Try over and over. Recognizes my email but then the next screen says it doesn't. I set up an account and use the email again and it says the account exists. So I have the CORRECT email. I click on "forgot password" and it logs me out instead of sending me an email to reset it. I just updated the app in hopes this glitch would stop. I can't log in until you fix this....

Nice -ish love Upwork. But this app really needs an update. I want to be able to go my portfolio and open the pictures I put in there to save and send to clients. But you can't even see the entire picture on your phone at all. Lots of lag. Just needs a bit of work and this app would be so much better. You guys already take on money out of what we are paid. Just fix things!

So far I've gotten mixed clients as a freelancer. One was a money mule scam, and the others were legitimately great people. I wish the platform was more secure of these things, and at the very least, faster than it currently is. However, all that aside the app has some potential if you work hard enough, and thank you specifically to the app's team of people for dedicating themselves to keep this operating.

The app is good, but I have had issues with the platform. In one instance, I started work for a project, but then got a notification that the project had been removed by UpWork because the job poster broke the terms of UpWork. It would be great if they caught this, prior to me starting work on in. In other instances, I have done work and submitted the milestones, but payment by the job poster was delayed or never happened. When job posters hold up their end of the bargain, then it's great.

Upwork is one of the best freelancing platforms for professionals around the world. The app is easy to use, and I can manage proposals, communicate with clients, track contracts, and receive notifications on the go. As a freelance chemical engineer and formulation chemist, it has helped me connect with clients from different countries and build long-term working relationships. The interface is smooth, and regular updates continue to improve the experience. Highly recommended for anyone.

This app doesn't even show if the client is active or not, your connects would go to waste, a client won't open job applications and you won't know it until you submit job proposal. it's just a way of extorting money from those who are looking for a job. very very greedy app and people. as if service fee and commission isn't already enough for them, they have increased the connects required to bid on jobs.

The platform has been great so far.... quite competitive, easygoing interface. so far the experience has been sharp. still learning about it, intentional about my freelancing career on the platform also I discovered I had to buy connect to be verified on the platform. Right now I don't have the capital for it but still, great app. warmly Shedrach A

Much recommend for freelancers for a short time in your life: create a profile and discover different kinds of frauds. Don't add any billing method and don't purchase any connects. Huge fees to be paid to Upwork and connects must be purchased before applying for the useless jobs. But opportunities are cheap! And the app is also full of glitch.

- you have to pay for credits, for boosting your proposal, and also there is the subscription. - no cancelling the subscription in ~3day window in renewal, i lost more money.... - a lot of fake, scam jobs. - my notifications for new job listings don't align to my profile at all. - always have to log in all over again since a few months now - often getting stuck at log in page?? - increase of hourly fees from 10 to 15 % for my role - lots of underpaying unless you're from Philippines I guess

So far, my experience with Upwork has been positive. The platform is easy to navigate, professional, and offers access to opportunities from clients around the world. I appreciate the clear job descriptions and application process. As a new freelancer, I would love to see more support for beginners, but overall it has been a great platform to start building my freelance career.

Clearly its not working for new freelancers. i got the monthly subscription for the connect points to bid. did bid for like 10 projects. many didn't even choose their freelancer even after 2 weeks. Even with 10+ years of experience, i am not selected for a freelance. Very bad experience. Poor system.

A Masterpiece. Truely a great app, giving each individual the opportunity to work, and also providing a reliable option to receive earnings into one's bank account directly, in almost all the countries. One of the greatest application of All time.

Locked my account for no reason, I found a good and applied for a job this time and it just deactivated my account for absolutely nothing but it didn't like my information or something. I double checked the information and it just suspended my account after the AI didn't like my information or something. No double check or anything, just suspended it.

I’m changing my review from 5 stars to 1 star because of the changes in bidding connects. Now, getting a job on Upwork feels more dependent on spending extra connects rather than actual skills or experience. Earlier, the maximum connects required to submit a bid was only 6, which felt fair for freelancers. But now, the platform is pushing users to spend more and more connects just to stay competitive. This change makes it harder for genuine freelancers, especially beginners..

UPDATE, 05/15/26: I just installed recent update and it appears the Jobs and Contracts tabs work again! Hurrah! ORIG, 05/13/26:I've been using this app for at least 6.5yrs if not longer. I noticed in the past week that they've made a lot of changes. However, for the last 2 days, the Jobs and Contracts tabs show up blank-- can't see anything. I installed the latest update just now and that didn't help. Still blank. ***Pls fix this and earn your stars back***

A very bad user experience, their system is so bad. Any particular task you have to login 100 times. The app is a complete mess, and a bad verification process very slow. They completely rely on virtual assistants, and Artificial Intelligence which caused in restricting my account, and some freelancers if they don't like, or agree with your offer they will report you and cause the account to be restricted. I don't recommend this app if you have a big project for business start-ups.

literally the worst experience ever cuz tell me why I bought connects then payed for it and I didn't receive it . I later asked for a refund and I haven't still gotten any . this is so frustrating 😡 u guyz should do better . last years interface was better., it was literally solving the problem of looking for jobs but this year is adding MORE problems.

So many walls. Go past one, another appears. Aggrivation, and I still don't know if this whole thing will progress. 1-star because I feel this has wasted my time. *Another day, another error on their page [Something went wrong] Sad little company. They have my money, so I guess that's okay. Who knew I'd have to pay to see companies, pay to verify my identity, then can't get in? *Can't get a refund unless I verify my identity - such a scam.

The app is incredibly slow, as loading a page takes around 30 seconds each time, and bugs are plentiful. Functions you would expect, like refreshing the page or only available jobs being shown (half of them are no longer available when you open them), are missing. Uploading an image took 30 minutes as nothing could be uploaded or edited until it just happened to work. Terrible experience: 0/10.

if there was a way to rate without a star, I'd have done that. Why will I open the app and see my account automatically logged out, then I've tried to login for over 10 days, but the response I get is “Due to technical difficulties we are unable to process your request. Please try again later “. After which I can't login, but I can access my account on the browser, please fix the app!!

Have only started using the app and at first in the beginning it seemed great, I could easily login and access my buisness accounts, and everything was running smoothly. However, having reopened another account, it has crashed terribly and it won't allow me to "verify my phone number" so I can get my new profile started. Such a huge waste of time now and would like this issue to be resolved ASAP.

The app has not been working well since I updated it. The job section is always showing black without being able to see the jobs posted. I thought it was my screen, until I used another app and saw that it is working perfectly.

kinda scamish. It cost $5.25 to verify your identity with the same process that is free every other time I've done it somewhere else. then you have to buy things called " connects" 35 of them are 5.25$ on average it cost about 10 to 15 connects to apply for a job. yeah. on top of them taking about 10% of your hourly wage.

Poor support! If you face any issues you can only contact an ai agent who wouldn't solve anything. Then you should raise an appeal and wait for a long time until someone responds. Very bad support, they restricted my account without any reason and now I'm waiting and have no idea what to do.

Fees are way too high! We work for low enough rates only for Upwork to take majority of it. You mentioned 15% for the entire contract which you do not abide to. Upwork deducts per milestone. Kindly revise that. The fees are too high!

Most of the positions are temporary, being that it's freelance work they need help with a project or consultants. They post for positions within my field that are looking for someone full-time but the hourly rate is so low because they're able to hire overseas and pay $8 an hr. that part is so discouraging, you literally have to stay on this app constantly looking for new post to stay ahead of all the hundreds of applicants before you and you have to pay to apply with each post costs varying.

The platform has become extremely expensive for new freelancers. Free connects are rarely available, and bidding requires too many connects, making it difficult to get genuine responses without spending a lot of money. Upwork needs to provide more free connects or lower the bidding cost so that genuine professionals can show their skills.

For weeks now, the jobs side of the app has been blank, showing absolutely nothing, I have to use the website to apply for jobs. It's frustrating, I've uninstalled and installed the app several times, still the glitch persists. Fix this ASAP

Zero customer support. Just sends you into a loop of articles to rea. Frustrating experience. Such an insult when you spend so much in their app that nothing gets resolved. As for the employees/freelancers, they are getting something like 15% of the pay taken??? Way to exploit people who invested building their profile on your app.

I think upwork is asking for too much money. For IDV, to apply for jobs - everything. I have heard that it gives 10 free connects, but I got none. Also it takes 12-16 connects per proposal and connects are way more costly.

Upwork is a very reliable and professional freelancing platform where both clients and freelancers can work easily. The interface is user-friendly, and there are many job opportunities in different categories. The payment system is secure, which builds trust for new users. It is also a good platform for beginners to start their freelancing career if they stay consistent and work hard. Overall, my experience has been very positive.

I really like the Upwork app and it’s been a great tool overall for finding work and managing jobs. However, I’ve been running into a pretty frustrating visual bug recently. Whenever I open the app, I can briefly see the job postings for a split second when scrolling up, but then they disappear and I can’t view them properly after that. I’ve tried clearing the cache and restarting the app, but the issue still persists. Right now, the job feed basically

It's been over 2 weeks since I opened my account and I still have 0 connections. My bank card is not ready for payment. So I'm having a hard time finding work. There are many jobs that suit me, but my account is still not active. I'm just starting out in the freelance field, so I don't know the platform's processes well. But the processes on the platform are encouraging. Thank you.

First they don't have a live chat support, or any cosy support contact, so the user can contact in case of issues (I contacted via Skype, but no answer) . Second, I did a mistake in my account profile, changed it, but the public info is not changed. Third, one of the basic and important windows - job requests, is not seen in the windows list. Either it does not exist, or a poor visibility is provided. When I choose Project Invitations on mobile app, it is empty, though I had received several.

For me as a designer and Illustrator, truelancer is a nice place to make some money and the fee is not too high like an other platform, it just 10% for the freelancer. I just have a 3 client recently from this.. Hope they are more and more people using this and I can get more job from there. Don't hesitate to use this app.. It's worth a try!!

This is such a bad app. It is very slow and it is so hard to find a job. The amount they cut is also a lot and the minimum amount required to withdraw or deposit is extravagant. The time taken to also process a payment is so slow,you have to wait for money that you have earned for almost 5 daysssss. Also the variety of work found in it is not a lot. The app also has a lot of bugs and there is false communication ie you have been invited for a job when they just want you to big. Do better

Very bad experience especially related to payment . Once I applied for payout . It showed 4 days but it has been more than 4.5 days since I didnt received the payment . The response is very slow and they are not at all supportive. Moreover if an app is dealing with money it should have fast and smooth payment process but its just the opposite .

All gigs are completely underpaid! $4 per hour and $110 for a website (that would normally run thousands). These developers need to wake up and set an hourly minimum in their app, that's at least the minimum wage in each state, as well as displaying the recommended rates for the type of work they need done (usually $30 to $80 per hour for creative work, not $4)! Then they use bidding for work, which is just as bad of practice...

Must read..! May be the review is long but it will help you.! This is the worst freelancing app.I have earned money from this but the app's server always blocks my account without any reason.I lost almost 10 clients due to this.My friend suggested me to buy the pro membership to avoid this,I trusted him because his problems were solved by this method but still they blocked my account. The most important thing that after buying the membership,they called me and forced to buy annual membership.

Vile. This service has potential but I am totally turned off. I'm not a fan of the interface, there are some major flaws such as: -Why is it so hard to see WHEN the ad was posted? -Can't filter by number of applicants -The category system does NOT work, you end up with a tonne of irrelevant job listings no matter what category you go to. -They send spammy emails and some are straight up lies. Example: "You have 11 profile views" , you go to their app to see that in reality you have 0.

I found one job one time but it didn't last long. I also almost got scammed a few times. Not a great app. Also some random person who claimed to be from the company tried to convince over the course of several weeks to purchase some £150 package. I had to block the number several times. Not great. Just got another app honestly

I purchased their paid plan and made the payment, but within just 5 minutes, my account was blocked without any clear reason. Since then, I’ve sent multiple emails over the last 5 days and haven’t received a single response. I have always followed the platform guidelines, yet there has been no communication or support from their side. This kind of treatment is unacceptable for freelancers who invest their time and money into the platform.

I cannot imagine a worse "freelance" app. Unable to setup payout, everything is extremely laggy like the whole thing is written in Python. For some reason, it is constantly, nonstop, getting your device location. I would suggest firing all your programmers and get some just from your own app. Time well wasted, thanks ✌️

This freelancing app is very easy to use unlike other freelancing platforms. I love that you can easily track your progress such as your earnings, proposals and profile views 😉 The filter function in "find job" tab makes it easy for me to categorize the job that I am looking for so I don't have to toggle in all the jobs posted. 10/10 for this app 😘👌

Keeps crashing. I tried uploading files for proposals and it immediately crashed. Tried again, crashed again. Finally decided to leave the files out of my proposal and after I successfully submitted ONE proposal, the app froze. How am I supposed to get any work done if it can't even stay open for a single proposal? Definitely will be staying with my other freelance platform.

It's a very non- user-friendly app. I can't explain my frustration when i look for job and keeps on loading. And the worst part is that when i open a job and go back, i have to start from the beginning. I will not use truelancer until this app is updated . Ps: You should really learn from freelancer's app. (Its such a user friendly app) Edit: One more thing, what is the use of notification when a user has to wait 5 minutes for loading it after clicking! Such an annoying app!

Quite a handy app for the platform but there is a lot to improve this app: 1. Loading super slow, especially the message box 2. Tab for updating project completion keeping on popping up, that I cannot use it properly. I always need to choose 10% to stop tabs popping up and then choose the correct process, and delete the tabs if I have msg to leave.

Great concept, and the app has a neat interface as well. But I'm docking two stars because it lacks an option to post a service (via app). Also, there needs to be a quick and easy tutorial explaining the various aspects of the app. For example I'm not sure how one gets a verified 'blue tick' to their account.

So far my experience has been really poor. The app refuses to work right if I'm using WiFi and then if I'm trying to apply to jobs with very few bids it'll say 'Job not found'. If I do use WiFi I ultimately have to delete the app and download it again to be able to check on any potential messages or projects because it freezes! Please fix this issue so people can actually utilize your services.

Okay your app is genuinely full of bugs. And I've tried it in various devices so the problem is definitely yours. Everytime I install it, I cannot login with my LinkedIn, which is how it is linked. The app crashes if I try to attach a file with the proposal. This has been happening so long that it is just irritating now.

I've been a member of this freelancing platform for a few months now, and it's incredible to have an app for it already in the play store. Whenever I go somewhere and have my phone with me, I can easily send a proposal for various projects, and it's the quickest way for me to communicate with my clients on workstream. Truelancer has always done an excellent job of connecting us freelances with more opportunities; the payment method and withdrawal processing is also quick. I recommend this app!

My experience on the app has been stunted , it is not allowing to save my changes to my profile , it identifies when the field is incorrect and what I should correct before I am able to save. It doesnt register the save. And upon leaving the options it asked me if I am sure I want to leave without saving my changes.

I like everything about Truelancer. But one thing is bothering me. One of my long term clients sent me a proposal after some time. I was working on two other projects and couldn't take it cause my hands are full. So I accepted his refund request and Truelancer gives me an automated one star. The refund request was in mutual agreement. It doesn't make sense to give me a one star that would affect my rankings. I hope you take action to change this.

I have been using this app for the past few months but I have not even got one job. they asked to buy membership to apply for certain jobs. But even after that I still have not got any job. Also the same jobs are being repeated each time I open it. There should be a option to click unrelated or not interested jobs so that it doesn't come the next time we open it

The app is good, design is acceptable (not so beautiful but fairly responsive) the most important thing that is missing is you can't can't know if your cover letter is going to reach the project owner because some projects require specific locations and you can't know until you waste your time writing a good cover letter and at the end finally you'll be shown a message: "you can't apply for this job " that is annoying

Truelancer is Awesome for Freelancers, Perfect Plateform for Freelancers to get Job but App isn't very great, I didn't get notification of My Clients Approval that's why i lose my Job because of 3 Late reply also you don't get any notifications for Shortlisting, Not Having Proper Options to Submit your entries in contest, Also My 100rs is Stuck in seller Lock from Past 7hrs means how much time do you really need, Please Fix This Problems

Good platform to find freelancers. But a lot of UI improvements have to be done. The messages loading is time consuming. some times it doesnt even load and app crashes. Please look into this issue, so that you will be best website for freelancing.

It is so annoying. The app gets loaded, bothering me with no option to click when I try to add description to my portfolio; it is not even saved. The team has to immediately fix the issue otherwise it will be difficult for us to use the app.

What the hell is this platform is ? I have nearly done with the client's final change and truelancer suddenly blocked my account.. No explanation nothing . Client is messaging me and I am recieving on my mail that client send a msg.but I can't see that , I can't reply because of blocked account. I have trying to reach to the support asking what is the reason, how can I fix it. No response, nothing , My total hardwork is become zero

Truelancer is frustrating, when I apply for jobs that requires handling the clients social media handles, they will block my account after I send my proposal stating how I will do the work, but the client will be spared.
